Robotics Daily Papers

Daily papers related to Robotics, Reinforcement Learning, Vision-Language Models, World Models, LLMs, VLA, and VLN

February 04, 2026

QVLA: Not All Channels Are Equal in Vision-Language-Action Model's Quantization

Abstract (English): The advent of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models represents a significant leap for embodied intelligence, yet their immense computational demands critically hinder deployment on resource-constrained robotic platforms. Intuitively, low-bit quantization is a prevalent and preferred technique for large-scale model compression. However, we find that a systematic analysis of VLA model's quantization is fundamentally lacking. We argue that naively applying uniform-bit quantization from Large Language Models (LLMs) to robotics is flawed, as these methods prioritize passive data fidelity while ignoring how minor action deviations compound into catastrophic task failures. To bridge this gap, we introduce QVLA, the first action-centric quantization framework specifically designed for embodied control. In a sharp departure from the rigid, uniform-bit quantization of LLM-based methods, QVLA introduces a highly granular, channel-wise bit allocation strategy. Its core mechanism is to directly measure the final action-space sensitivity when quantizing each individual channel to various bit-widths. This process yields a precise, per-channel importance metric that guides a global optimization, which elegantly unifies quantization and pruning (0-bit) into a single, cohesive framework. Extensive evaluations on different baselines demonstrate the superiority of our approach. In the LIBERO, the quantization version of OpenVLA-OFT with our method requires only 29.2% of the original model's VRAM while maintaining 98.9% of its original performance and achieving a 1.49x speedup. This translates to a 22.6% performance improvement over the LLM-derived method SmoothQuant. Our work establishes a new, principled foundation for compressing VLA models in robotics, paving the way for deploying powerful, large-scale models on real-world hardware. Code will be released.

摘要（中文）: 视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型的出现代表了具身智能领域的一次重大飞跃，但其巨大的计算需求严重阻碍了在资源受限的机器人平台上的部署。直观上，低比特量化是一种流行且首选的大规模模型压缩技术。然而，我们发现对VLA模型量化的系统性分析非常缺乏。我们认为，将大型语言模型（LLM）中的统一比特量化直接应用于机器人是存在缺陷的，因为这些方法侧重于被动的数据忠诚度，而忽略了微小的动作偏差如何累积成灾难性的任务失败。为了弥合这一差距，我们推出了QVLA，这是第一个专门为具身控制设计的以动作为中心的量化框架。与基于LLM方法的刚性、统一比特量化截然不同的是，QVLA引入了一种高度精细的、通道级的比特分配策略。其核心机制是直接测量将每个通道量化到不同比特宽度时最终动作空间的敏感度。这个过程产生了一个精确的、逐通道的重要性度量，它指导一个全局最优化过程，优雅地将量化和剪枝（0比特）统一到一个单一的、有凝聚力的框架中。对不同基线的大量评估证明了我们方法的优越性。在LIBERO中，使用我们方法的OpenVLA-OFT量化版本仅需要原始模型29.2%的VRAM，同时保持原始性能的98.9%，并实现1.49倍的加速。与LLM派生的SmoothQuant方法相比，这转化为22.6%的性能提升。我们的工作为压缩机器人中的VLA模型建立了一个新的、有原则的基础，从而为在现实世界硬件上部署强大的、大规模模型铺平了道路。代码将会发布。

TLDR: This paper introduces QVLA, an action-centric quantization framework for Vision-Language-Action models, which uses channel-wise bit allocation based on action-space sensitivity to achieve significant model compression and speedup while maintaining performance compared to directly applying LLM quantization methods.

简要说明: 本文介绍了 QVLA，一个面向视觉-语言-动作模型的动作中心量化框架。该框架使用基于动作空间敏感性的通道式比特分配，与直接应用 LLM 量化方法相比，在保持性能的同时实现了显著的模型压缩和加速。

Authors: Yuhao Xu, Yantai Yang, Zhenyang Fan, Yufan Liu, Yuming Li, Bing Li, Zhipeng Zhang

CRL-VLA: Continual Vision-Language-Action Learning

Abstract (English): Lifelong learning is critical for embodied agents in open-world environments, where reinforcement learning fine-tuning has emerged as an important paradigm to enable Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models to master dexterous manipulation through environmental interaction. Thus, Continual Reinforcement Learning (CRL) is a promising pathway for deploying VLA models in lifelong robotic scenarios, yet balancing stability (retaining old skills) and plasticity (learning new ones) remains a formidable challenge for existing methods. We introduce CRL-VLA, a framework for continual post-training of VLA models with rigorous theoretical bounds. We derive a unified performance bound linking the stability-plasticity trade-off to goal-conditioned advantage magnitude, scaled by policy divergence. CRL-VLA resolves this dilemma via asymmetric regulation: constraining advantage magnitudes on prior tasks while enabling controlled growth on new tasks. This is realized through a simple but effective dual-critic architecture with novel Goal-Conditioned Value Formulation (GCVF), where a frozen critic anchors semantic consistency and a trainable estimator drives adaptation. Experiments on the LIBERO benchmark demonstrate that CRL-VLA effectively harmonizes these conflicting objectives, outperforming baselines in both anti-forgetting and forward adaptation.

摘要（中文）: 终身学习对于开放世界环境中具身智能体至关重要，其中强化学习微调已成为一种重要的范式，使视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型能够通过环境交互掌握灵巧操作。因此，持续强化学习（CRL）是VLA模型部署在终身机器人场景中的一条有希望的途径，然而，平衡稳定性（保持旧技能）和可塑性（学习新技能）对于现有方法来说仍然是一个巨大的挑战。我们引入了CRL-VLA，一个用于VLA模型持续后训练的框架，并具有严格的理论界限。我们推导出一个统一的性能界限，该界限将稳定性-可塑性权衡与目标条件优势幅度联系起来，并按策略散度进行缩放。CRL-VLA通过非对称调控来解决这个困境：约束先前任务的优势幅度，同时允许新任务上受控的增长。这通过一个简单但有效的双重评论家架构以及新颖的目标条件价值公式（GCVF）来实现，其中冻结的评论家锚定语义一致性，而可训练的估计器驱动适应。在LIBERO基准上的实验表明，CRL-VLA有效地协调了这些冲突的目标，在抗遗忘和正向适应方面均优于基线。

TLDR: The paper introduces CRL-VLA, a framework for continual post-training of Vision-Language-Action models that addresses the stability-plasticity trade-off in lifelong robotic scenarios using a dual-critic architecture with goal-conditioned value formulation, achieving state-of-the-art results on the LIBERO benchmark.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了CRL-VLA，一个用于持续后训练视觉-语言-动作模型的框架。该框架通过使用具有目标条件价值公式的双重评论家架构，解决了终身机器人场景中的稳定性-可塑性权衡问题，并在LIBERO基准测试中取得了最先进的结果。

Authors: Qixin Zeng, Shuo Zhang, Hongyin Zhang, Renjie Wang, Han Zhao, Libang Zhao, Runze Li, Donglin Wang, Chao Huang

RDT2: Exploring the Scaling Limit of UMI Data Towards Zero-Shot Cross-Embodiment Generalization

Abstract (English): Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models hold promise for generalist robotics but currently struggle with data scarcity, architectural inefficiencies, and the inability to generalize across different hardware platforms. We introduce RDT2, a robotic foundation model built upon a 7B parameter VLM designed to enable zero-shot deployment on novel embodiments for open-vocabulary tasks. To achieve this, we collected one of the largest open-source robotic datasets--over 10,000 hours of demonstrations in diverse families--using an enhanced, embodiment-agnostic Universal Manipulation Interface (UMI). Our approach employs a novel three-stage training recipe that aligns discrete linguistic knowledge with continuous control via Residual Vector Quantization (RVQ), flow-matching, and distillation for real-time inference. Consequently, RDT2 becomes one of the first models that simultaneously zero-shot generalizes to unseen objects, scenes, instructions, and even robotic platforms. Besides, it outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in dexterous, long-horizon, and dynamic downstream tasks like playing table tennis. See https://rdt-robotics.github.io/rdt2/ for more information.

摘要（中文）: 视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 模型展现了通用机器人技术的潜力，但目前面临数据稀缺、架构效率低下以及无法跨不同硬件平台泛化的问题。我们提出了 RDT2，一个基于 70 亿参数 VLM 构建的机器人基础模型，旨在实现对新型具身机器人进行零样本部署，以完成开放词汇任务。为了实现这一目标，我们使用增强的、与具身无关的通用操作界面 (UMI) 收集了最大的开源机器人数据集之一，其中包含超过 10,000 小时的各种类型的演示数据。我们的方法采用了一种新颖的三阶段训练方案，该方案通过残差矢量量化 (RVQ)、流匹配和蒸馏将离散的语言知识与连续控制对齐，以实现实时推理。因此，RDT2 成为首批能够同时零样本泛化到未见过的物体、场景、指令，甚至机器人平台的模型之一。此外，在灵巧、长时程和动态的下游任务中，例如打乒乓球，它也优于最先进的基线模型。更多信息请访问 https://rdt-robotics.github.io/rdt2/ 。

TLDR: RDT2 is a VLA model trained on a large, diverse robotics dataset using a novel three-stage training recipe to enable zero-shot cross-embodiment generalization, outperforming SOTA baselines in several tasks.

简要说明: RDT2是一个VLA模型，它使用一种新的三阶段训练方法，在一个大型的、多样化的机器人数据集上进行训练，从而实现零样本跨平台泛化，并在多个任务中优于SOTA基线。

Authors: Songming Liu, Bangguo Li, Kai Ma, Lingxuan Wu, Hengkai Tan, Xiao Ouyang, Hang Su, Jun Zhu

TIC-VLA: A Think-in-Control Vision-Language-Action Model for Robot Navigation in Dynamic Environments

Abstract (English): Robots in dynamic, human-centric environments must follow language instructions while maintaining real-time reactive control. Vision-language-action (VLA) models offer a promising framework, but they assume temporally aligned reasoning and control, despite semantic inference being inherently delayed relative to real-time action. We introduce Think-in-Control (TIC)-VLA, a latency-aware framework that explicitly models delayed semantic reasoning during action generation. TIC-VLA defines a delayed semantic-control interface that conditions action generation on delayed vision-language semantic states and explicit latency metadata, in addition to current observations, enabling policies to compensate for asynchronous reasoning. We further propose a latency-consistent training pipeline that injects reasoning inference delays during imitation learning and online reinforcement learning, aligning training with asynchronous deployment. To support realistic evaluation, we present DynaNav, a physics-accurate, photo-realistic simulation suite for language-guided navigation in dynamic environments. Extensive experiments in simulation and on a real robot show that TIC-VLA consistently outperforms prior VLA models while maintaining robust real-time control under multi-second reasoning latency. Project website: https://ucla-mobility.github.io/TIC-VLA/

摘要（中文）: 在动态、以人为中心的环境中，机器人必须遵循语言指令，同时保持实时的反应式控制。视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 模型提供了一个有前景的框架，但它们假设时间上对齐的推理和控制，尽管语义推理相对于实时动作而言具有固有的延迟。我们引入了 Think-in-Control (TIC)-VLA，这是一个延迟感知框架，它在动作生成过程中显式地建模了延迟的语义推理。TIC-VLA 定义了一个延迟的语义-控制接口，该接口将动作生成建立在延迟的视觉-语言语义状态和显式延迟元数据之上，以及当前观察结果，从而使策略能够补偿异步推理。我们进一步提出了一个延迟一致的训练流程，该流程在模仿学习和在线强化学习期间注入推理推导延迟，使训练与异步部署保持一致。为了支持真实的评估，我们提出了 DynaNav，这是一个物理精确、照片级真实的仿真套件，用于动态环境中受语言引导的导航。在模拟和真实机器人上的大量实验表明，TIC-VLA始终优于先前的 VLA 模型，同时在数秒推理延迟下保持了强大的实时控制。项目网站：https://ucla-mobility.github.io/TIC-VLA/

TLDR: The paper introduces Think-in-Control (TIC)-VLA, a novel vision-language-action model that explicitly addresses the issue of latency between semantic reasoning and real-time action generation for robot navigation in dynamic environments, along with a new simulation environment, DynaNav.

简要说明: 这篇论文介绍了Think-in-Control (TIC)-VLA，一种新型的视觉-语言-动作模型，它显式地解决了动态环境中机器人导航时语义推理与实时动作生成之间的延迟问题，并提出了一个新的仿真环境DynaNav。

Authors: Zhiyu Huang, Yun Zhang, Johnson Liu, Rui Song, Chen Tang, Jiaqi Ma

World-Gymnast: Training Robots with Reinforcement Learning in a World Model

Abstract (English): Robot learning from interacting with the physical world is fundamentally bottlenecked by the cost of physical interaction. The two alternatives, supervised finetuning (SFT) from expert demonstrations and reinforcement learning (RL) in a software-based simulator, are limited by the amount of expert data available and the sim-to-real gap for manipulation. With the recent emergence of world models learned from real-world video-action data, we ask the question of whether training a policy in a world model can be more effective than supervised learning or software simulation in achieving better real-robot performance. We propose World-Gymnast, which performs RL finetuning of a vision-language-action (VLA) policy by rolling out the policy in an action-conditioned video world model and rewarding the rollouts with a vision-language model (VLM). On the Bridge robot setup, World-Gymnast outperforms SFT by as much as 18x and outperforms software simulator by as much as 2x. More importantly, World-Gymnast demonstrates intriguing capabilities of RL with a world model, including training on diverse language instructions and novel scenes from the world model, test-time training in a novel scene, and online iterative world model and policy improvement. Our results suggest learning a world model and training robot policies in the cloud could be the key to bridging the gap between robots that work in demonstrations and robots that can work in anyone's household.

摘要（中文）: 从与物理世界交互中进行机器人学习，其根本瓶颈在于物理交互的成本。两种替代方案，即从专家演示中进行监督微调（SFT）和在基于软件的模拟器中进行强化学习（RL），都受限于可用的专家数据量和操作的模拟到真实差距。随着最近从真实世界视频-动作数据中学习的世界模型的出现，我们提出这样一个问题：在世界模型中训练策略是否比监督学习或软件仿真更能有效地实现更好的真实机器人性能。我们提出了 World-Gymnast，它通过在动作条件视频世界模型中执行策略来进行视觉-语言-动作（VLA）策略的强化学习微调，并使用视觉-语言模型（VLM）奖励这些rollout。在 Bridge 机器人设置上，World-Gymnast 的性能超越 SFT 高达 18 倍，超越软件模拟器高达 2 倍。更重要的是，World-Gymnast 展示了强化学习与世界模型结合的有趣能力，包括在来自世界模型的多样化语言指令和新场景上进行训练、在新场景中进行测试时训练，以及在线迭代的世界模型和策略改进。我们的结果表明，学习世界模型并在云端训练机器人策略可能是弥合仅能根据演示工作和能服务于任何人家庭的机器人之间差距的关键。

TLDR: The paper introduces World-Gymnast, a method for training robots using reinforcement learning within a world model, achieving significant performance improvements over supervised finetuning and software simulation in real-world robotic tasks. It demonstrates the potential of cloud-based robot training to bridge the gap to more adaptable robots.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了 World-Gymnast，一种在世界模型中使用强化学习训练机器人的方法，在真实机器人任务中，该方法比监督微调和软件模拟实现了显著的性能提升。它展示了基于云的机器人训练在弥合机器人与更具适应性的机器人之间差距的潜力。

Authors: Ansh Kumar Sharma, Yixiang Sun, Ninghao Lu, Yunzhe Zhang, Jiarao Liu, Sherry Yang

Agentic Proposing: Enhancing Large Language Model Reasoning via Compositional Skill Synthesis

Abstract (English): Advancing complex reasoning in large language models relies on high-quality, verifiable datasets, yet human annotation remains cost-prohibitive and difficult to scale. Current synthesis paradigms often face a recurring trade-off: maintaining structural validity typically restricts problem complexity, while relaxing constraints to increase difficulty frequently leads to inconsistent or unsolvable instances. To address this, we propose Agentic Proposing, a framework that models problem synthesis as a goal-driven sequential decision process where a specialized agent dynamically selects and composes modular reasoning skills. Through an iterative workflow of internal reflection and tool-use, we develop the Agentic-Proposer-4B using Multi-Granularity Policy Optimization (MGPO) to generate high-precision, verifiable training trajectories across mathematics, coding, and science. Empirical results demonstrate that downstream solvers trained on agent-synthesized data significantly outperform leading baselines and exhibit robust cross-domain generalization. Notably, a 30B solver trained on only 11,000 synthesized trajectories achieves a state-of-the-art 91.6% accuracy on AIME25, rivaling frontier-scale proprietary models such as GPT-5 and proving that a small volume of high-quality synthetic signals can effectively substitute for massive human-curated datasets.

摘要（中文）: 为了提升大型语言模型中的复杂推理能力，需要高质量、可验证的数据集，然而人工标注成本高昂且难以规模化。现有的合成范式常常面临一个反复出现的权衡：保持结构有效性通常会限制问题的复杂度，而放宽约束以增加难度则常常导致不一致或无解的实例。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种名为Agentic Proposing的框架，该框架将问题合成建模为一个目标驱动的序列决策过程，其中一个专门的智能体动态地选择和组合模块化的推理技能。通过内部反思和工具使用的迭代工作流程，我们使用多粒度策略优化（MGPO）开发了Agentic-Proposer-4B，以生成跨数学、编码和科学的高精度、可验证的训练轨迹。实验结果表明，在智能体合成的数据上训练的下游求解器显著优于领先的基线，并表现出强大的跨领域泛化能力。值得注意的是，一个仅在11,000条合成轨迹上训练的30B求解器在AIME25上实现了91.6%的最新准确率，与GPT-5等前沿规模的专有模型相媲美，证明少量高质量的合成信号可以有效地替代大规模的人工策划数据集。

TLDR: This paper introduces Agentic Proposing, a framework for generating high-quality, verifiable training data for LLMs by using an agent to dynamically compose reasoning skills, achieving state-of-the-art results on AIME25 with significantly less data than human-curated datasets.

简要说明: 本文介绍了 Agentic Proposing，一个通过使用智能体动态组合推理技能，为大型语言模型生成高质量、可验证训练数据的框架。该框架在 AIME25 上取得了最先进的结果，且所需数据量远少于人工管理的数据集。

Authors: Zhengbo Jiao, Shaobo Wang, Zifan Zhang, Xuan Ren, Wei Wang, Bing Zhao, Hu Wei, Linfeng Zhang

CSR-Bench: A Benchmark for Evaluating the Cross-modal Safety and Reliability of MLLMs

Abstract (English): Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) enable interaction over both text and images, but their safety behavior can be driven by unimodal shortcuts instead of true joint intent understanding. We introduce CSR-Bench, a benchmark for evaluating cross-modal reliability through four stress-testing interaction patterns spanning Safety, Over-rejection, Bias, and Hallucination, covering 61 fine-grained types. Each instance is constructed to require integrated image-text interpretation, and we additionally provide paired text-only controls to diagnose modality-induced behavior shifts. We evaluate 16 state-of-the-art MLLMs and observe systematic cross-modal alignment gaps. Models show weak safety awareness, strong language dominance under interference, and consistent performance degradation from text-only controls to multimodal inputs. We also observe a clear trade-off between reducing over-rejection and maintaining safe, non-discriminatory behavior, suggesting that some apparent safety gains may come from refusal-oriented heuristics rather than robust intent understanding. WARNING: This paper contains unsafe contents.

摘要（中文）: 多模态大型语言模型(MLLMs)能够实现基于文本和图像的交互，但其安全行为可能受限于单模态捷径，而非真正的联合意图理解。我们推出了CSR-Bench，一个用于评估跨模态可靠性的基准，通过涵盖安全、过度拒绝、偏见和幻觉这四个压力测试交互模式，共计61种细粒度类型。每个实例都旨在要求综合的图像-文本解读，并且我们额外提供了配对的纯文本控制，以诊断模态诱导的行为转变。我们评估了16个最先进的MLLMs，并观察到系统的跨模态对齐差距。模型表现出较弱的安全意识、干扰下的强语言主导地位，以及从纯文本控制到多模态输入的一致性性能下降。我们还观察到减少过度拒绝和维持安全、非歧视行为之间存在明显的权衡，表明一些表面上的安全提升可能来源于面向拒绝的启发式方法，而不是稳健的意图理解。警告：本文包含不安全内容。

TLDR: The paper introduces CSR-Bench, a benchmark for evaluating the cross-modal reliability (safety, over-rejection, bias, hallucination) of MLLMs, highlighting their weaknesses in joint intent understanding and showcasing performance degradation compared to text-only inputs.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了CSR-Bench，一个用于评估多模态大型语言模型(MLLMs)的跨模态可靠性（安全性，过度拒绝，偏见，幻觉）的基准，强调了它们在联合意图理解方面的弱点，并展示了它们与纯文本输入相比的性能下降。

Authors: Yuxuan Liu, Yuntian Shi, Kun Wang, Haoting Shen, Kun Yang

ConsisDrive: Identity-Preserving Driving World Models for Video Generation by Instance Mask

Abstract (English): Autonomous driving relies on robust models trained on large-scale, high-quality multi-view driving videos. Although world models provide a cost-effective solution for generating realistic driving data, they often suffer from identity drift, where the same object changes its appearance or category across frames due to the absence of instance-level temporal constraints. We introduce ConsisDrive, an identity-preserving driving world model designed to enforce temporal consistency at the instance level. Our framework incorporates two key components: (1) Instance-Masked Attention, which applies instance identity masks and trajectory masks within attention blocks to ensure that visual tokens interact only with their corresponding instance features across spatial and temporal dimensions, thereby preserving object identity consistency; and (2) Instance-Masked Loss, which adaptively emphasizes foreground regions with probabilistic instance masking, reducing background noise while maintaining overall scene fidelity. By integrating these mechanisms, ConsisDrive achieves state-of-the-art driving video generation quality and demonstrates significant improvements in downstream autonomous driving tasks on the nuScenes dataset. Our project page is https://shanpoyang654.github.io/ConsisDrive/page.html.

摘要（中文）: 自动驾驶依赖于在大型高质量多视角驾驶视频上训练的鲁棒模型。尽管世界模型为生成逼真的驾驶数据提供了一种经济高效的解决方案，但它们通常会受到身份漂移的影响，即由于缺乏实例级别的时序约束，同一物体在不同帧之间会改变其外观或类别。我们提出了 ConsisDrive，一种身份保持的驾驶世界模型，旨在在实例级别强制执行时间一致性。我们的框架包含两个关键组件：（1）实例掩码注意力，它在注意力模块中应用实例身份掩码和轨迹掩码，以确保视觉令牌仅与其对应的实例特征在空间和时间维度上进行交互，从而保持对象身份一致性；及（2）实例掩码损失，它通过概率实例掩码自适应地强调前景区域，减少背景噪声，同时保持整体场景的逼真度。通过整合这些机制，ConsisDrive 实现了最先进的驾驶视频生成质量，并在 nuScenes 数据集上的下游自动驾驶任务中表现出显著的改进。我们的项目主页是 https://shanpoyang654.github.io/ConsisDrive/page.html。

TLDR: ConsisDrive improves driving video generation by enforcing instance-level temporal consistency using instance-masked attention and loss, leading to better performance on downstream autonomous driving tasks.

简要说明: ConsisDrive 通过使用实例掩码注意力机制和损失函数，增强实例级别的时序一致性，从而改进驾驶视频生成，并在下游自动驾驶任务中表现更佳。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8.5/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Zhuoran Yang, Yanyong Zhang

BridgeV2W: Bridging Video Generation Models to Embodied World Models via Embodiment Masks

Abstract (English): Embodied world models have emerged as a promising paradigm in robotics, most of which leverage large-scale Internet videos or pretrained video generation models to enrich visual and motion priors. However, they still face key challenges: a misalignment between coordinate-space actions and pixel-space videos, sensitivity to camera viewpoint, and non-unified architectures across embodiments. To this end, we present BridgeV2W, which converts coordinate-space actions into pixel-aligned embodiment masks rendered from the URDF and camera parameters. These masks are then injected into a pretrained video generation model via a ControlNet-style pathway, which aligns the action control signals with predicted videos, adds view-specific conditioning to accommodate camera viewpoints, and yields a unified world model architecture across embodiments. To mitigate overfitting to static backgrounds, BridgeV2W further introduces a flow-based motion loss that focuses on learning dynamic and task-relevant regions. Experiments on single-arm (DROID) and dual-arm (AgiBot-G1) datasets, covering diverse and challenging conditions with unseen viewpoints and scenes, show that BridgeV2W improves video generation quality compared to prior state-of-the-art methods. We further demonstrate the potential of BridgeV2W on downstream real-world tasks, including policy evaluation and goal-conditioned planning. More results can be found on our project website at https://BridgeV2W.github.io .

摘要（中文）: 具身世界模型已成为机器人领域一种很有前景的范式，其中大多数利用大规模互联网视频或预训练的视频生成模型来丰富视觉和运动先验。然而，它们仍然面临着关键挑战：坐标空间动作与像素空间视频之间的不对齐、对相机视角的敏感性以及跨具身非统一架构。为此，我们提出了BridgeV2W，它将坐标空间动作转换为从URDF和相机参数渲染的像素对齐具身掩码。然后，这些掩码通过ControlNet风格的路径注入到预训练的视频生成模型中，从而将动作控制信号与预测的视频对齐，添加特定于视角的条件以适应相机视角，并产生跨具身的统一世界模型架构。为了减轻对静态背景的过度拟合，BridgeV2W进一步引入了一种基于光流的运动损失，该损失侧重于学习动态和任务相关的区域。在单臂（DROID）和双臂（AgiBot-G1）数据集上的实验，涵盖了具有未见视角和场景的各种具有挑战性的条件，表明BridgeV2W相比于先前的最先进方法，提高了视频生成质量。我们进一步展示了BridgeV2W在下游真实世界任务上的潜力，包括策略评估和目标条件下的规划。更多结果可在我们的项目网站https://BridgeV2W.github.io上找到。

TLDR: The paper introduces BridgeV2W, a method for aligning action control signals with predicted videos in embodied world models by converting coordinate-space actions into pixel-aligned embodiment masks and injecting them into a video generation model. Experiments show improved video generation quality and potential for real-world robotic tasks.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了BridgeV2W，一种将坐标空间动作转换为像素对齐的实体模型掩码，并将其注入视频生成模型的方法，用于对齐实体世界模型中的动作控制信号与预测视频。实验表明，与现有方法相比，视频生成质量得到提高，并具有应用于真实世界机器人任务的潜力。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Yixiang Chen, Peiyan Li, Jiabing Yang, Keji He, Xiangnan Wu, Yuan Xu, Kai Wang, Jing Liu, Nianfeng Liu, Yan Huang, Liang Wang

A Scene Graph Backed Approach to Open Set Semantic Mapping

Abstract (English): While Open Set Semantic Mapping and 3D Semantic Scene Graphs (3DSSGs) are established paradigms in robotic perception, deploying them effectively to support high-level reasoning in large-scale, real-world environments remains a significant challenge. Most existing approaches decouple perception from representation, treating the scene graph as a derivative layer generated post hoc. This limits both consistency and scalability. In contrast, we propose a mapping architecture where the 3DSSG serves as the foundational backend, acting as the primary knowledge representation for the entire mapping process. Our approach leverages prior work on incremental scene graph prediction to infer and update the graph structure in real-time as the environment is explored. This ensures that the map remains topologically consistent and computationally efficient, even during extended operations in large-scale settings. By maintaining an explicit, spatially grounded representation that supports both flat and hierarchical topologies, we bridge the gap between sub-symbolic raw sensor data and high-level symbolic reasoning. Consequently, this provides a stable, verifiable structure that knowledge-driven frameworks, ranging from knowledge graphs and ontologies to Large Language Models (LLMs), can directly exploit, enabling agents to operate with enhanced interpretability, trustworthiness, and alignment to human concepts.

摘要（中文）: 开放集语义地图构建和3D语义场景图（3DSSG）是机器人感知领域中成熟的范例，但如何有效部署它们以支持大规模真实环境中的高级推理仍然是一个重大挑战。现有的大多数方法将感知与表征分离，将场景图视为事后生成的衍生层。这限制了其一致性和可扩展性。 与此相反，我们提出了一种将3DSSG作为基础后端的地图构建架构，将其作为整个地图构建过程的主要知识表征。 我们的方法利用了先前在增量式场景图预测方面的工作，以在探索环境时实时推断和更新图结构。这确保了即使在大规模环境中进行长时间操作，地图也能保持拓扑一致性和计算效率。 通过维护一个支持扁平化和分层拓扑的显式、空间定位的表征，我们弥合了亚符号原始传感器数据与高级符号推理之间的差距。 因此，这提供了一个稳定、可验证的结构，从知识图谱和本体到大型语言模型（LLM）的各种知识驱动框架可以直接利用它，从而使智能体能够以增强的可解释性、可信度和与人类概念的一致性进行操作。

TLDR: This paper proposes a scene graph-backed mapping architecture for robotics that addresses the challenge of scaling semantic mapping to large, real-world environments by using a 3DSSG as the primary knowledge representation. It aims to bridge the gap between raw sensor data and high-level reasoning, enabling integration with knowledge-driven frameworks like LLMs.

简要说明: 本文提出了一种基于场景图的机器人制图架构，通过使用3DSSG作为主要知识表示，解决了语义地图扩展到大型真实世界环境的挑战。它旨在弥合原始传感器数据和高级推理之间的差距，从而能够与LLM等知识驱动框架集成。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Martin Günther, Felix Igelbrink, Oscar Lima, Lennart Niecksch, Marian Renz, Martin Atzmueller

MVP-LAM: Learning Action-Centric Latent Action via Cross-Viewpoint Reconstruction

Abstract (English): Learning \emph{latent actions} from diverse human videos enables scaling robot learning beyond embodiment-specific robot datasets, and these latent actions have recently been used as pseudo-action labels for vision-language-action (VLA) model pretraining. To make VLA pretraining effective, latent actions should contain information about the underlying agent's actions despite the absence of ground-truth labels. We propose \textbf{M}ulti-\textbf{V}iew\textbf{P}oint \textbf{L}atent \textbf{A}ction \textbf{M}odel (\textbf{MVP-LAM}), which learns discrete latent actions that are highly informative about ground-truth actions from time-synchronized multi-view videos. MVP-LAM trains latent actions with a \emph{cross-viewpoint reconstruction} objective, so that a latent action inferred from one view must explain the future in another view, reducing reliance on viewpoint-specific cues. On Bridge V2, MVP-LAM produces more action-centric latent actions, achieving higher mutual information with ground-truth actions and improved action prediction, including under out-of-distribution evaluation. Finally, pretraining VLAs with MVP-LAM latent actions improves downstream manipulation performance on the SIMPLER and LIBERO-Long benchmarks.

摘要（中文）: 从多样化的人类视频中学习\emph{隐式动作}，能够将机器人学习扩展到特定于具体机器人的数据集之外，并且这些隐式动作最近已被用作视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型预训练的伪标签。为了使VLA预训练有效，即使在没有真实标签的情况下，隐式动作也应该包含关于底层代理动作的信息。我们提出了\textbf{多}视点\textbf{隐式}动作\textbf{模}型 (\textbf{MVP-LAM})，该模型学习来自时间同步的多视角视频的离散隐式动作，这些动作高度包含关于真实动作的信息。MVP-LAM以\emph{跨视点重建}为目标训练隐式动作，使得从一个视点推断的隐式动作必须能够解释另一个视点中的未来，从而减少对视点特定线索的依赖。在Bridge V2上，MVP-LAM产生了更多以动作为中心的隐式动作，实现了与真实动作更高的互信息和改进的动作预测，包括在分布外评估下。最后，使用MVP-LAM隐式动作预训练VLA可以提高在SIMPLER和LIBERO-Long基准上的下游操作性能。

TLDR: The paper introduces MVP-LAM, a method for learning action-centric latent actions from multi-view videos using a cross-viewpoint reconstruction objective, improving VLA pretraining and downstream robotic manipulation performance.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了MVP-LAM，一种通过交叉视点重建目标从多视角视频中学习以动作为中心的潜在动作的方法，从而改进了VLA预训练和下游机器人操作性能。

Novelty: (8/10)

Authors: Jung Min Lee, Dohyeok Lee, Seokhun Ju, Taehyun Cho, Jin Woo Koo, Li Zhao, Sangwoo Hong, Jungwoo Lee

AffordanceGrasp-R1:Leveraging Reasoning-Based Affordance Segmentation with Reinforcement Learning for Robotic Grasping

Abstract (English): We introduce AffordanceGrasp-R1, a reasoning-driven affordance segmentation framework for robotic grasping that combines a chain-of-thought (CoT) cold-start strategy with reinforcement learning to enhance deduction and spatial grounding. In addition, we redesign the grasping pipeline to be more context-aware by generating grasp candidates from the global scene point cloud and subsequently filtering them using instruction-conditioned affordance masks. Extensive experiments demonstrate that AffordanceGrasp-R1 consistently outperforms state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods on benchmark datasets, and real-world robotic grasping evaluations further validate its robustness and generalization under complex language-conditioned manipulation scenarios.

摘要（中文）: 我们提出了AffordanceGrasp-R1，一个基于推理的用于机器人抓取的示能分割框架，它结合了思维链（CoT）冷启动策略和强化学习，以增强推理能力和空间定位。此外，我们重新设计了抓取流程，使其更具上下文感知能力，通过从全局场景点云生成抓取候选，并随后使用指令条件示能掩码对其进行过滤。大量实验表明，AffordanceGrasp-R1在基准数据集上始终优于最先进（SOTA）方法，并且真实世界机器人抓取评估进一步验证了其在复杂的语言条件操作场景下的鲁棒性和泛化能力。

TLDR: AffordanceGrasp-R1 enhances robotic grasping by integrating reasoning-based affordance segmentation with reinforcement learning and a context-aware grasping pipeline, achieving SOTA performance in both benchmark datasets and real-world scenarios.

简要说明: AffordanceGrasp-R1通过结合基于推理的认知分割、强化学习以及上下文感知的抓取流程来改进机器人抓取，在基准数据集和实际场景中均实现了SOTA性能。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Dingyi Zhou, Mu He, Zhuowei Fang, Xiangtong Yao, Yinlong Liu, Alois Knoll, Hu Cao

CMR: Contractive Mapping Embeddings for Robust Humanoid Locomotion on Unstructured Terrains

Abstract (English): Robust disturbance rejection remains a longstanding challenge in humanoid locomotion, particularly on unstructured terrains where sensing is unreliable and model mismatch is pronounced. While perception information, such as height map, enhances terrain awareness, sensor noise and sim-to-real gaps can destabilize policies in practice. In this work, we provide theoretical analysis that bounds the return gap under observation noise, when the induced latent dynamics are contractive. Furthermore, we present Contractive Mapping for Robustness (CMR) framework that maps high-dimensional, disturbance-prone observations into a latent space, where local perturbations are attenuated over time. Specifically, this approach couples contrastive representation learning with Lipschitz regularization to preserve task-relevant geometry while explicitly controlling sensitivity. Notably, the formulation can be incorporated into modern deep reinforcement learning pipelines as an auxiliary loss term with minimal additional technical effort required. Further, our extensive humanoid experiments show that CMR potently outperforms other locomotion algorithms under increased noise.

摘要（中文）: 在人形机器人运动中，鲁棒的抗扰能力一直是一个长期存在的挑战，尤其是在非结构化地形上，那里的感知往往不可靠且模型失配明显。虽然像高度图这样的感知信息可以增强对地形的感知，但传感器噪声和模拟到现实的差距会在实践中 destabilize 策略。在这项工作中，我们提供了理论分析，在诱导潜在动力学具有收缩性时，限定了观测噪声下的回报差距。此外，我们提出了一种用于提升鲁棒性的收缩映射（CMR）框架，该框架将高维的、易受扰动的观测映射到一个潜在空间，在这个空间中局部扰动会随着时间的推移而衰减。具体而言，这种方法将对比表示学习与 Lipschitz 正则化相结合，以保留任务相关的几何结构，同时显式地控制灵敏度。值得注意的是，该公式可以作为辅助损失项整合到现代深度强化学习流程中，而无需额外的技术投入。此外，我们广泛的人形机器人实验表明，在噪声增加的情况下，CMR 显著优于其他运动算法。

TLDR: This paper introduces a Contractive Mapping for Robustness (CMR) framework that leverages contrastive representation learning and Lipschitz regularization to improve humanoid locomotion robustness on unstructured terrains by mapping noisy observations into a contractive latent space.

简要说明: 本文介绍了一种鲁棒性契约映射（CMR）框架，该框架利用对比表示学习和Lipschitz正则化，通过将带噪声的观察映射到契约潜在空间，从而提高人形机器人在非结构化地形上的运动鲁棒性。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Qixin Zeng, Hongyin Zhang, Shangke Lyu, Junxi Jin, Donglin Wang, Chao Huang

ProAct: A Benchmark and Multimodal Framework for Structure-Aware Proactive Response

Abstract (English): While passive agents merely follow instructions, proactive agents align with higher-level objectives, such as assistance and safety by continuously monitoring the environment to determine when and how to act. However, developing proactive agents is hindered by the lack of specialized resources. To address this, we introduce ProAct-75, a benchmark designed to train and evaluate proactive agents across diverse domains, including assistance, maintenance, and safety monitoring. Spanning 75 tasks, our dataset features 91,581 step-level annotations enriched with explicit task graphs. These graphs encode step dependencies and parallel execution possibilities, providing the structural grounding necessary for complex decision-making. Building on this benchmark, we propose ProAct-Helper, a reference baseline powered by a Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) that grounds decision-making in state detection, and leveraging task graphs to enable entropy-driven heuristic search for action selection, allowing agents to execute parallel threads independently rather than mirroring the human's next step. Extensive experiments demonstrate that ProAct-Helper outperforms strong closed-source models, improving trigger detection mF1 by 6.21%, saving 0.25 more steps in online one-step decision, and increasing the rate of parallel actions by 15.58%.

摘要（中文）: 被动式智能体仅遵循指令，而主动式智能体则会与更高级别的目标对齐，例如通过持续监控环境来确定何时以及如何行动，以实现协助和安全。然而，缺乏专门的资源阻碍了主动式智能体的开发。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了ProAct-75，这是一个旨在训练和评估主动式智能体在不同领域（包括协助、维护和安全监控）表现的基准。我们的数据集涵盖75个任务，包含91,581个步骤级别的标注，并附有明确的任务图。这些图编码了步骤依赖关系和平行执行的可能性，为复杂的决策提供了必要的结构性基础。在此基准的基础上，我们提出了ProAct-Helper，一个由多模态大型语言模型（MLLM）驱动的参考基线，它将决策建立在状态检测的基础上，并利用任务图来实现基于熵驱动的启发式搜索来进行动作选择，从而使得智能体能够独立执行平行的线程，而不是镜像人类的下一步。大量的实验表明，ProAct-Helper优于强大的闭源模型，触发检测的mF1提高了6.21%，在线一步决策中节省了0.25个步骤，并将并行动作的比例提高了15.58%。

TLDR: The paper introduces ProAct-75, a benchmark dataset for training and evaluating proactive agents, along with ProAct-Helper, a multimodal LLM-based baseline, demonstrating improved performance in trigger detection and parallel action execution.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了ProAct-75，一个用于训练和评估主动代理的基准数据集，以及ProAct-Helper，一个基于多模态LLM的基线模型，展示了在触发检测和并行动作执行方面的改进性能。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Xiaomeng Zhu, Fengming Zhu, Weijie Zhou, Ye Tian, Zhenlin Hu, Yufei Huang, Yuchun Guo, Xinyu Wu, Zhengyou Zhang, Fangzhen Lin, Xuantang Xiong

Learning-based Initialization of Trajectory Optimization for Path-following Problems of Redundant Manipulators

Abstract (English): Trajectory optimization (TO) is an efficient tool to generate a redundant manipulator's joint trajectory following a 6-dimensional Cartesian path. The optimization performance largely depends on the quality of initial trajectories. However, the selection of a high-quality initial trajectory is non-trivial and requires a considerable time budget due to the extremely large space of the solution trajectories and the lack of prior knowledge about task constraints in configuration space. To alleviate the issue, we present a learning-based initial trajectory generation method that generates high-quality initial trajectories in a short time budget by adopting example-guided reinforcement learning. In addition, we suggest a null-space projected imitation reward to consider null-space constraints by efficiently learning kinematically feasible motion captured in expert demonstrations. Our statistical evaluation in simulation shows the improved optimality, efficiency, and applicability of TO when we plug in our method's output, compared with three other baselines. We also show the performance improvement and feasibility via real-world experiments with a seven-degree-of-freedom manipulator.

摘要（中文）: 轨迹优化 (TO) 是一种高效的工具，用于生成冗余机械臂跟随六维笛卡尔路径的关节轨迹。 优化性能很大程度上取决于初始轨迹的质量。 然而，由于解轨迹空间极其庞大，且缺乏构型空间中任务约束的先验知识，选择高质量的初始轨迹并非易事，需要可观的时间预算。 为了缓解这个问题，我们提出了一种基于学习的初始轨迹生成方法，该方法通过采用示例引导的强化学习，在较短的时间预算内生成高质量的初始轨迹。 此外，我们提出了一种零空间投影的模仿奖励，通过有效地学习专家演示中捕获的运动学可行运动，来考虑零空间约束。 我们的仿真统计评估表明，与另外三种基线方法相比，当我们使用我们方法的输出时，TO 的最优性、效率和适用性都得到了提高。 我们还通过七自由度机械臂的真实世界实验展示了性能的提升和可行性。

TLDR: This paper proposes a learning-based approach using reinforcement learning and imitation learning to generate high-quality initial trajectories for trajectory optimization of redundant manipulators, improving optimality and efficiency compared to other baselines in both simulation and real-world experiments.

简要说明: 本文提出了一种基于学习的方法，使用强化学习和模仿学习来为冗余机械臂的轨迹优化生成高质量的初始轨迹，与模拟和现实世界实验中的其他基线相比，提高了最优性和效率。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Minsung Yoon, Mincheul Kang, Daehyung Park, Sung-Eui Yoon

PlanTRansformer: Unified Prediction and Planning with Goal-conditioned Transformer

Abstract (English): Trajectory prediction and planning are fundamental yet disconnected components in autonomous driving. Prediction models forecast surrounding agent motion under unknown intentions, producing multimodal distributions, while planning assumes known ego objectives and generates deterministic trajectories. This mismatch creates a critical bottleneck: prediction lacks supervision for agent intentions, while planning requires this information. Existing prediction models, despite strong benchmarking performance, often remain disconnected from planning constraints such as collision avoidance and dynamic feasibility. We introduce Plan TRansformer (PTR), a unified Gaussian Mixture Transformer framework integrating goal-conditioned prediction, dynamic feasibility, interaction awareness, and lane-level topology reasoning. A teacher-student training strategy progressively masks surrounding agent commands during training to align with inference conditions where agent intentions are unavailable. PTR achieves 4.3%/3.5% improvement in marginal/joint mAP compared to the baseline Motion Transformer (MTR) and 15.5% planning error reduction at 5s horizon compared to GameFormer. The architecture-agnostic design enables application to diverse Transformer-based prediction models. Project Website: https://github.com/SelzerConst/PlanTRansformer

摘要（中文）: 轨迹预测和规划是自动驾驶中基础但又相互脱节的组成部分。预测模型在未知意图下预测周围智能体的运动，产生多模态分布，而规划则假定已知的自车目标并生成确定性的轨迹。这种不匹配造成了一个关键瓶颈：预测缺乏对智能体意图的监督，而规划需要这些信息。现有的预测模型，尽管具有强大的基准测试性能，但通常与避撞和动态可行性等规划约束保持脱节。我们引入了Plan TRansformer (PTR)，一个统一的高斯混合Transformer框架，集成了目标条件预测、动态可行性、交互感知和车道级拓扑推理。一种师生训练策略在训练期间逐步掩盖周围智能体的指令，以与智能体意图不可用的推理条件对齐。与基线Motion Transformer (MTR)相比，PTR在边缘/联合mAP上分别实现了4.3%/3.5%的提升，与GameFormer相比，在5秒时程上实现了15.5%的规划误差降低。与架构无关的设计使其能够应用于各种基于Transformer的预测模型。项目网站：https://github.com/SelzerConst/PlanTRansformer

TLDR: The paper introduces PlanTRansformer (PTR), a unified Transformer framework for trajectory prediction and planning in autonomous driving that integrates goal-conditioned prediction with planning constraints, achieving improved performance compared to baselines. It bridges the gap between intention-unaware prediction and intention-aware planning.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了PlanTRansformer (PTR)，一个用于自动驾驶中轨迹预测和规划的统一Transformer框架，它将目标条件预测与规划约束相结合，与基线相比实现了性能提升。它弥合了不考虑意图的预测和考虑意图的规划之间的差距。

Relevance: (7/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Constantin Selzer, Fabina B. Flohr

When Attention Betrays: Erasing Backdoor Attacks in Robotic Policies by Reconstructing Visual Tokens

Abstract (English): Downstream fine-tuning of vision-language-action (VLA) models enhances robotics, yet exposes the pipeline to backdoor risks. Attackers can pretrain VLAs on poisoned data to implant backdoors that remain stealthy but can trigger harmful behavior during inference. However, existing defenses either lack mechanistic insight into multimodal backdoors or impose prohibitive computational costs via full-model retraining. To this end, we uncover a deep-layer attention grabbing mechanism: backdoors redirect late-stage attention and form compact embedding clusters near the clean manifold. Leveraging this insight, we introduce Bera, a test-time backdoor erasure framework that detects tokens with anomalous attention via latent-space localization, masks suspicious regions using deep-layer cues, and reconstructs a trigger-free image to break the trigger-unsafe-action mapping while restoring correct behavior. Unlike prior defenses, Bera requires neither retraining of VLAs nor any changes to the training pipeline. Extensive experiments across multiple embodied platforms and tasks show that Bera effectively maintains nominal performance, significantly reduces attack success rates, and consistently restores benign behavior from backdoored outputs, thereby offering a robust and practical defense mechanism for securing robotic systems.

摘要（中文）: 视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 模型下游微调增强了机器人技术，但也使流程面临后门风险。攻击者可以预训练具有中毒数据的 VLA，植入后门，这些后门保持隐蔽，但可以在推理过程中触发有害行为。然而，现有的防御方法要么缺乏对多模态后门机制的深入了解，要么通过完整模型重新训练带来巨大的计算成本。为此，我们揭示了一种深层注意力抓取机制：后门重定向了后期注意力，并在接近干净流形的区域形成了紧凑的嵌入簇。基于这一洞察，我们提出 Bera，一个测试时后门擦除框架，它通过潜在空间定位检测具有异常注意力的token，使用深层线索屏蔽可疑区域，并重建一个无触发图像以打破触发-不安全-动作的映射，同时恢复正确的行为。与之前的防御方法不同，Bera既不需要重新训练VLA，也不需要对训练流程进行任何更改。在多个具身平台和任务上的大量实验表明，Bera有效地保持了名义性能，显著降低了攻击成功率，并持续地从后门输出中恢复良性行为，从而为确保机器人系统的安全提供了一种稳健且实用的防御机制。

TLDR: This paper introduces Bera, a novel test-time backdoor defense framework for robotic vision-language-action models that leverages attention analysis and image reconstruction to mitigate trigger effects without retraining, showcasing effectiveness across multiple robotic platforms and tasks.

简要说明: 本文介绍了一种名为Bera的新型测试时后门防御框架，用于机器人视觉-语言-动作模型。该框架利用注意力分析和图像重建来减轻触发效果，无需重新训练，并在多个机器人平台和任务中展示了有效性。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Xuetao Li, Pinhan Fu, Wenke Huang, Nengyuan Pan, Songhua Yang, Kaiyan Zhao, Guancheng Wan, Mengde Li, Jifeng Xuan, Miao Li

RPL: Learning Robust Humanoid Perceptive Locomotion on Challenging Terrains

Abstract (English): Humanoid perceptive locomotion has made significant progress and shows great promise, yet achieving robust multi-directional locomotion on complex terrains remains underexplored. To tackle this challenge, we propose RPL, a two-stage training framework that enables multi-directional locomotion on challenging terrains, and remains robust with payloads. RPL first trains terrain-specific expert policies with privileged height map observations to master decoupled locomotion and manipulation skills across different terrains, and then distills them into a transformer policy that leverages multiple depth cameras to cover a wide range of views. During distillation, we introduce two techniques to robustify multi-directional locomotion, depth feature scaling based on velocity commands and random side masking, which are critical for asymmetric depth observations and unseen widths of terrains. For scalable depth distillation, we develop an efficient multi-depth system that ray-casts against both dynamic robot meshes and static terrain meshes in massively parallel environments, achieving a 5-times speedup over the depth rendering pipelines in existing simulators while modeling realistic sensor latency, noise, and dropout. Extensive real-world experiments demonstrate robust multi-directional locomotion with payloads (2kg) across challenging terrains, including 20° slopes, staircases with different step lengths (22 cm, 25 cm, 30 cm), and 25 cm by 25 cm stepping stones separated by 60 cm gaps.

摘要（中文）: 人形机器人的感知运动已经取得了显著进展并展现出巨大潜力，但在复杂地形上实现鲁棒的多方向运动仍然有待探索。为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了RPL，一个两阶段训练框架，能够在具有挑战性的地形上实现多方向运动，并且在负载情况下保持鲁棒性。 RPL 首先利用特权高度图观测训练特定地形的专家策略，以掌握不同地形上解耦的运动和操作技能，然后将它们提炼成一个Transformer策略，该策略利用多个深度相机来覆盖广泛的视角。在知识提炼过程中，我们引入了两项技术来增强多方向运动的鲁棒性：基于速度指令的深度特征缩放和随机侧面掩蔽，这对于不对称深度观测和未见过的地形宽度至关重要。为了实现可扩展的深度知识提炼，我们开发了一个高效的多深度系统，该系统在大规模并行环境中针对动态机器人网格和静态地形网格进行光线投射，在模拟真实传感器延迟、噪声和dropout的同时，与现有模拟器中的深度渲染流程相比，速度提高了5倍。 广泛的真实世界实验证明了在具有挑战性的地形上的鲁棒多方向运动，以及携带载荷（2千克）的情况，包括20°斜坡、具有不同步长（22厘米、25厘米、30厘米）的楼梯，以及间距为60厘米的25厘米x25厘米的踏脚石。

TLDR: The paper introduces RPL, a two-stage training framework using terrain-specific expert policies and depth distillation to enable robust multi-directional humanoid locomotion on challenging terrains, validated with real-world experiments.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了一种名为RPL的两阶段训练框架，该框架使用特定地形的专家策略和深度蒸馏，以实现类人机器人在复杂地形上鲁棒的多向运动，并通过真实世界的实验进行了验证。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Yuanhang Zhang, Younggyo Seo, Juyue Chen, Yifu Yuan, Koushil Sreenath, Pieter Abbeel, Carmelo Sferrazza, Karen Liu, Rocky Duan, Guanya Shi

Embodiment-Aware Generalist Specialist Distillation for Unified Humanoid Whole-Body Control

Abstract (English): Humanoid Whole-Body Controllers trained with reinforcement learning (RL) have recently achieved remarkable performance, yet many target a single robot embodiment. Variations in dynamics, degrees of freedom (DoFs), and kinematic topology still hinder a single policy from commanding diverse humanoids. Moreover, obtaining a generalist policy that not only transfers across embodiments but also supports richer behaviors-beyond simple walking to squatting, leaning-remains especially challenging. In this work, we tackle these obstacles by introducing EAGLE, an iterative generalist-specialist distillation framework that produces a single unified policy that controls multiple heterogeneous humanoids without per-robot reward tuning. During each cycle, embodiment-specific specialists are forked from the current generalist, refined on their respective robots, and new skills are distilled back into the generalist by training on the pooled embodiment set. Repeating this loop until performance convergence produces a robust Whole-Body Controller validated on robots such as Unitree H1, G1, and Fourier N1. We conducted experiments on five different robots in simulation and four in real-world settings. Through quantitative evaluations, EAGLE achieves high tracking accuracy and robustness compared to other methods, marking a step toward scalable, fleet-level humanoid control. See more details at https://eagle-wbc.github.io/

摘要（中文）: 摘要： 利用强化学习(RL)训练的人形机器人全身控制器最近取得了显著的性能，但许多控制器都针对单一机器人形态。动力学、自由度(DoFs)和运动学拓扑的差异仍然阻碍了单一策略控制不同的人形机器人。此外，获得一种通用的策略，不仅可以跨形态转移，还能支持更丰富的行为——超越简单的行走，扩展到如蹲姿，倾斜姿态——仍然极具挑战性。本文中，我们通过引入EAGLE，一种迭代的通用-专用知识蒸馏框架来解决这些障碍，该框架生成一个统一的策略，无需针对每个机器人进行奖励调整即可控制多个异构人形机器人。在每个循环中，特定形态的专家模型从当前的通用模型中分离出来，在其各自的机器人上进行精细化，并通过在汇集的形态数据集上训练将其新技能蒸馏回通用模型。重复这个循环直到性能收敛，从而产生一个鲁棒的全身控制器，并在如宇树H1、G1和傅里叶N1等机器人上得到验证。我们在仿真环境中对五种不同的机器人和在真实环境中对四种机器人进行了实验。通过定量评估，EAGLE相较于其他方法实现了高跟踪精度和鲁棒性，标志着朝着可扩展、机群级的人形机器人控制迈出了一步。更多细节请见 https://eagle-wbc.github.io/

TLDR: The paper introduces EAGLE, an iterative generalist-specialist distillation framework for training a single unified policy that controls multiple heterogeneous humanoid robots without robot-specific reward tuning, validated on both simulated and real-world robots.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了一种名为EAGLE的迭代通用-专用知识提炼框架，用于训练能够控制多种异构人形机器人的统一策略，无需针对每个机器人进行奖励调整，已在模拟和真实世界的机器人上验证。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Quanquan Peng, Yunfeng Lin, Yufei Xue, Jiangmiao Pang, Weinan Zhang

Moving On, Even When You're Broken: Fail-Active Trajectory Generation via Diffusion Policies Conditioned on Embodiment and Task

Abstract (English): Robot failure is detrimental and disruptive, often requiring human intervention to recover. Maintaining safe operation under impairment to achieve task completion, i.e. fail-active operation, is our target. Focusing on actuation failures, we introduce DEFT, a diffusion-based trajectory generator conditioned on the robot's current embodiment and task constraints. DEFT generalizes across failure types, supports constrained and unconstrained motions, and enables task completion under arbitrary failure. We evaluated DEFT in both simulation and real-world scenarios using a 7-DoF robotic arm. In simulation over thousands of joint-failure cases across multiple tasks, DEFT outperformed the baseline by up to 2 times. On failures unseen during training, it continued to outperform the baseline, indicating robust generalization in simulation. Further, we performed real-world evaluations on two multi-step tasks, drawer manipulation and whiteboard erasing. These experiments demonstrated DEFT succeeding on tasks where classical methods failed. Our results show that DEFT achieves fail-active manipulation across arbitrary failure configurations and real-world deployments.

摘要（中文）: 机器人故障是有害且具有破坏性的，通常需要人工干预才能恢复。我们的目标是在受损情况下维持安全操作以完成任务，即故障激活操作。针对驱动失效，我们引入 DEFT，这是一种基于扩散的轨迹生成器，其条件设定为机器人的当前形态和任务约束。DEFT 可以泛化到各种故障类型，支持约束和无约束运动，并能够在任意故障下完成任务。我们使用一个 7-自由度机械臂在模拟和真实场景中评估了 DEFT。在模拟中对数千个跨多个任务的关节失效案例进行评估，DEFT 的性能优于基线方法高达 2 倍。在训练期间未见过的失效情况下，DEFT 仍然优于基线方法，表明其在模拟中具有强大的泛化能力。此外，我们还在两个多步骤任务中进行了真实世界的评估：抽屉操作和白板擦除。这些实验证明 DEFT 成功地完成了经典方法失败的任务。我们的结果表明，DEFT 可以在任意故障配置和真实世界部署中实现故障激活的操控。

TLDR: This paper introduces DEFT, a diffusion-based trajectory generator conditioned on robot embodiment and task, enabling fail-active robotic manipulation by completing tasks even with actuation failures, demonstrated in simulation and real-world experiments.

简要说明: 本文介绍了DEFT，一种基于扩散的轨迹生成器，它以机器人形态和任务为条件，通过在发生驱动故障时完成任务来实现失效激活的机器人操作，并在模拟和现实世界实验中进行了演示。

Relevance: (7/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Gilberto G. Briscoe-Martinez, Yaashia Gautam, Rahul Shetty, Anuj Pasricha, Marco M. Nicotra, Alessandro Roncone

Accelerating Structured Chain-of-Thought in Autonomous Vehicles

Abstract (English): Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning enhances the decision-making capabilities of vision-language-action models in autonomous driving, but its autoregressive nature introduces significant inference latency, making it impractical for real-time applications. To address this, we introduce FastDriveCoT, a novel parallel decoding method that accelerates template-structured CoT. Our approach decomposes the reasoning process into a dependency graph of distinct sub-tasks, such as identifying critical objects and summarizing traffic rules, some of which can be generated in parallel. By generating multiple independent reasoning steps concurrently within a single forward pass, we significantly reduce the number of sequential computations. Experiments demonstrate a 3-4$\times$ speedup in CoT generation and a substantial reduction in end-to-end latency across various model architectures, all while preserving the original downstream task improvements brought by incorporating CoT reasoning.

摘要（中文）: 思维链（CoT）推理增强了视觉-语言-动作模型在自动驾驶中的决策能力，但其自回归特性引入了显著的推理延迟，使其无法在实时应用中使用。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了FastDriveCoT，一种新颖的并行解码方法，用于加速模板结构的CoT。我们的方法将推理过程分解为一系列具有依赖关系的子任务，例如识别关键物体和总结交通规则，其中的一些子任务可以并行生成。通过在单个前向传播过程中并发地生成多个独立的推理步骤，我们显著减少了顺序计算的次数。实验表明，在各种模型架构上，CoT生成速度提升3-4倍，并且显著降低了端到端延迟，同时保留了整合CoT推理所带来的原始下游任务改进。

TLDR: The paper introduces FastDriveCoT, a method for parallel decoding of structured Chain-of-Thought reasoning, achieving significant speedups in autonomous driving applications without sacrificing performance.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了FastDriveCoT，一种用于并行解码结构化思维链推理的方法，在不牺牲性能的情况下，显著提高了自动驾驶应用的推理速度。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Yi Gu, Yan Wang, Yuxiao Chen, Yurong You, Wenjie Luo, Yue Wang, Wenhao Ding, Boyi Li, Heng Yang, Boris Ivanovic, Marco Pavone

IMAGINE: Intelligent Multi-Agent Godot-based Indoor Networked Exploration

Abstract (English): The exploration of unknown, Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) denied environments by an autonomous communication-aware and collaborative group of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) presents significant challenges in coordination, perception, and decentralized decision-making. This paper implements Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) to address these challenges in a 2D indoor environment, using high-fidelity game-engine simulations (Godot) and continuous action spaces. Policy training aims to achieve emergent collaborative behaviours and decision-making under uncertainty using Network-Distributed Partially Observable Markov Decision Processes (ND-POMDPs). Each UAV is equipped with a Light Detection and Ranging (LiDAR) sensor and can share data (sensor measurements and a local occupancy map) with neighbouring agents. Inter-agent communication constraints include limited range, bandwidth and latency. Extensive ablation studies evaluated MARL training paradigms, reward function, communication system, neural network (NN) architecture, memory mechanisms, and POMDP formulations. This work jointly addresses several key limitations in prior research, namely reliance on discrete actions, single-agent or centralized formulations, assumptions of a priori knowledge and permanent connectivity, inability to handle dynamic obstacles, short planning horizons and architectural complexity in Recurrent NNs/Transformers. Results show that the scalable training paradigm, combined with a simplified architecture, enables rapid autonomous exploration of an indoor area. The implementation of Curriculum-Learning (five increasingly complex levels) also enabled faster, more robust training. This combination of high-fidelity simulation, MARL formulation, and computational efficiency establishes a strong foundation for deploying learned cooperative strategies in physical robotic systems.

摘要（中文）: 由自主的、具有通信感知能力和协同能力的无人机（UAV）群探索未知的、全球导航卫星系统（GNSS）受限环境，在协调、感知和去中心化决策方面提出了重大挑战。本文采用多智能体强化学习（MARL）来解决这些挑战，环境设定为一个二维室内环境，并使用高保真游戏引擎仿真（Godot）和连续动作空间。策略训练旨在实现新兴的协同行为以及在使用网络分布式部分可观测马尔可夫决策过程（ND-POMDPs）条件下的不确定性决策。每个无人机配备一部激光雷达（LiDAR）传感器，并可以与相邻智能体共享数据（传感器测量值和局部占据栅格地图）。智能体间通信约束包括有限的范围、带宽和延迟。通过广泛的消融研究评估了MARL训练范式、奖励函数、通信系统、神经网络（NN）架构、记忆机制和POMDP公式。本工作共同解决了先前研究中的几个关键局限性，即依赖于离散动作、单智能体或集中式公式、先验知识和永久连接的假设、无法处理动态障碍物、短规划范围以及循环神经网络/Transformer的架构复杂性。结果表明，可扩展的训练范式与简化的架构相结合，能够快速自主地探索室内区域。课程学习（五个复杂度递增的等级）的实施也实现了更快、更稳健的训练。这种高保真仿真、MARL公式和计算效率的结合，为在物理机器人系统中部署学习到的协同策略奠定了坚实的基础。

TLDR: This paper explores multi-agent reinforcement learning for autonomous exploration of indoor environments using UAVs in a simulated environment, addressing limitations of prior work through a scalable training paradigm and simplified architecture.

简要说明: 本文探索了多智能体强化学习在模拟环境中利用无人机自主探索室内环境的方法， 通过可扩展的训练模式和简化的架构，解决了先前研究的局限性。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Tiago Leite, Maria Conceição, António Grilo

Latent Perspective-Taking via a Schrödinger Bridge in Influence-Augmented Local Models

Abstract (English): Operating in environments alongside humans requires robots to make decisions under uncertainty. In addition to exogenous dynamics, they must reason over others' hidden mental-models and mental-states. While Interactive POMDPs and Bayesian Theory of Mind formulations are principled, exact nested-belief inference is intractable, and hand-specified models are brittle in open-world settings. We address both by learning structured mental-models and an estimator of others' mental-states. Building on the Influence-Based Abstraction, we instantiate an Influence-Augmented Local Model to decompose socially-aware robot tasks into local dynamics, social influences, and exogenous factors. We propose (a) a neuro-symbolic world model instantiating a factored, discrete Dynamic Bayesian Network, and (b) a perspective-shift operator modeled as an amortized Schrödinger Bridge over the learned local dynamics that transports factored egocentric beliefs into other-centric beliefs. We show that this architecture enables agents to synthesize socially-aware policies in model-based reinforcement learning, via decision-time mental-state planning (a Schrödinger Bridge in belief space), with preliminary results in a MiniGrid social navigation task.

摘要（中文）: 在与人类共存的环境中运行，机器人需要在不确定性下做出决策。除了外生动态因素，它们还必须推断他人的隐藏心理模型和心理状态。虽然交互式POMDP和贝叶斯心智理论的公式具有理论依据，但精确的嵌套信念推理难以处理，并且手工指定的模型在开放世界环境中脆弱。我们通过学习结构化的心理模型和他人的心理状态估计器来解决这两个问题。基于影响抽象，我们实例化一个影响增强的局部模型，将具有社会意识的机器人任务分解为局部动态，社会影响和外生因素。我们提出：（a）一个神经符号世界模型，实例化一个分解的、离散的动态贝叶斯网络；（b）一个视角转换算子，该算子被建模为通过学习到的局部动态上的摊销薛定谔桥，将分解的自我中心信念转换为他人中心信念。我们证明了这种架构能够使智能体在基于模型的强化学习中合成具有社会意识的策略，通过决策时的心理状态规划（信念空间中的薛定谔桥），并在MiniGrid社会导航任务中获得了初步结果。

TLDR: This paper introduces a neuro-symbolic world model with a Schrödinger Bridge perspective-shift operator for socially-aware robot decision-making in environments with humans, demonstrated in a MiniGrid social navigation task.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了一种神经符号世界模型，该模型具有Schrödinger桥透视转换算子，用于在具有人类的环境中进行社会感知机器人决策，并在MiniGrid社交导航任务中得到了演示。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (8/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Kevin Alcedo, Pedro U. Lima, Rachid Alami

Causal Flow Q-Learning for Robust Offline Reinforcement Learning

Abstract (English): Expressive policies based on flow-matching have been successfully applied in reinforcement learning (RL) more recently due to their ability to model complex action distributions from offline data. These algorithms build on standard policy gradients, which assume that there is no unmeasured confounding in the data. However, this condition does not necessarily hold for pixel-based demonstrations when a mismatch exists between the demonstrator's and the learner's sensory capabilities, leading to implicit confounding biases in offline data. We address the challenge by investigating the problem of confounded observations in offline RL from a causal perspective. We develop a novel causal offline RL objective that optimizes policies' worst-case performance that may arise due to confounding biases. Based on this new objective, we introduce a practical implementation that learns expressive flow-matching policies from confounded demonstrations, employing a deep discriminator to assess the discrepancy between the target policy and the nominal behavioral policy. Experiments across 25 pixel-based tasks demonstrate that our proposed confounding-robust augmentation procedure achieves a success rate 120\% that of confounding-unaware, state-of-the-art offline RL methods.

摘要（中文）: 基于流匹配的表达性策略由于其能够从离线数据中建模复杂动作分布，近年来已成功应用于强化学习(RL)。这些算法建立在标准策略梯度之上，后者假设数据中不存在未测量的混杂因素。然而，当演示者和学习者的感知能力存在不匹配时，对于基于像素的演示，此条件不一定成立，导致离线数据中存在隐式混杂偏差。我们从因果角度研究了离线RL中混杂观测的问题，从而应对这一挑战。我们开发了一种新的因果离线RL目标，该目标优化策略在可能由混杂偏差引起的的最坏情况下性能。基于这一新目标，我们引入了一种实际实现，该实现从混杂的演示中学习表达性流匹配策略，并采用深度判别器来评估目标策略与名义行为策略之间的差异。在25个基于像素的任务中的实验表明，我们提出的混杂鲁棒性增强程序所达到的成功率是未考虑混杂因素的最先进离线RL方法成功率的120%。

TLDR: This paper addresses the problem of confounding biases in offline reinforcement learning, particularly in pixel-based environments, by proposing a causal offline RL objective and a flow-matching policy learning implementation. Results show significant improvement compared to existing methods.

简要说明: 本文通过提出一种因果离线强化学习目标和一种流量匹配策略学习方法，解决了离线强化学习中（尤其是在基于像素的环境中）的混淆偏差问题。结果表明，与现有方法相比，性能有显著提高。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Mingxuan Li, Junzhe Zhang, Elias Bareinboim

Language Movement Primitives: Grounding Language Models in Robot Motion

Abstract (English): Enabling robots to perform novel manipulation tasks from natural language instructions remains a fundamental challenge in robotics, despite significant progress in generalized problem solving with foundational models. Large vision and language models (VLMs) are capable of processing high-dimensional input data for visual scene and language understanding, as well as decomposing tasks into a sequence of logical steps; however, they struggle to ground those steps in embodied robot motion. On the other hand, robotics foundation models output action commands, but require in-domain fine-tuning or experience before they are able to perform novel tasks successfully. At its core, there still remains the fundamental challenge of connecting abstract task reasoning with low-level motion control. To address this disconnect, we propose Language Movement Primitives (LMPs), a framework that grounds VLM reasoning in Dynamic Movement Primitive (DMP) parameterization. Our key insight is that DMPs provide a small number of interpretable parameters, and VLMs can set these parameters to specify diverse, continuous, and stable trajectories. Put another way: VLMs can reason over free-form natural language task descriptions, and semantically ground their desired motions into DMPs -- bridging the gap between high-level task reasoning and low-level position and velocity control. Building on this combination of VLMs and DMPs, we formulate our LMP pipeline for zero-shot robot manipulation that effectively completes tabletop manipulation problems by generating a sequence of DMP motions. Across 20 real-world manipulation tasks, we show that LMP achieves 80% task success as compared to 31% for the best-performing baseline. See videos at our website: https://collab.me.vt.edu/lmp

摘要（中文）: 尽管使用基础模型进行通用问题求解取得了显著进展，但使机器人能够根据自然语言指令执行新的操控任务仍然是机器人领域的一项根本性挑战。 大型视觉语言模型 (VLM) 能够处理高维输入数据，进行视觉场景和语言理解，并将任务分解为一系列逻辑步骤；然而，它们难以将这些步骤与具身机器人运动相结合。 另一方面，机器人基础模型可以输出动作指令，但需要在特定领域进行微调或积累经验才能成功执行新任务。 问题的核心仍然是连接抽象的任务推理与底层运动控制这一根本性挑战。 为了解决这种脱节，我们提出了语言运动基元（LMPs），这是一个将VLM推理与动态运动基元 (DMP) 参数化相结合的框架。 我们的关键见解是，DMP提供少量可解释的参数，而VLM可以设置这些参数来指定多样、连续且稳定的轨迹。 换句话说，VLM可以基于自由形式的自然语言任务描述进行推理，并将所需的运动在语义上与DMP相结合——从而弥合了高层任务推理和底层位置与速度控制之间的差距。 基于VLM和DMP的这种结合，我们提出了LMP流水线，用于零样本机器人操控，通过生成一系列DMP运动来有效地完成桌面操控问题。 在20个真实世界的操控任务中，我们表明LMP实现了80%的任务成功率，而表现最佳的基线方法仅为31%。 相关视频请访问我们的网站：https://collab.me.vt.edu/lmp

TLDR: This paper introduces Language Movement Primitives (LMPs), a framework that combines Vision-Language Models (VLMs) and Dynamic Movement Primitives (DMPs) to enable zero-shot robot manipulation with high success rates compared to baselines.

简要说明: 本文介绍了语言运动基元（LMPs），该框架结合了视觉语言模型（VLMs）和动态运动基元（DMPs），以实现零样本机器人操作，与基线相比具有更高的成功率。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Yinlong Dai, Benjamin A. Christie, Daniel J. Evans, Dylan P. Losey, Simon Stepputtis

Adaptive Linear Path Model-Based Diffusion

Abstract (English): The interest in combining model-based control approaches with diffusion models has been growing. Although we have seen many impressive robotic control results in difficult tasks, the performance of diffusion models is highly sensitive to the choice of scheduling parameters, making parameter tuning one of the most critical challenges. We introduce Linear Path Model-Based Diffusion (LP-MBD), which replaces the variance-preserving schedule with a flow-matching-inspired linear probability path. This yields a geometrically interpretable and decoupled parameterization that reduces tuning complexity and provides a stable foundation for adaptation. Building on this, we propose Adaptive LP-MBD (ALP-MBD), which leverages reinforcement learning to adjust diffusion steps and noise levels according to task complexity and environmental conditions. Across numerical studies, Brax benchmarks, and mobile-robot trajectory tracking, LP-MBD simplifies scheduling while maintaining strong performance, and ALP-MBD further improves robustness, adaptability, and real-time efficiency.

摘要（中文）: 将基于模型的控制方法与扩散模型相结合越来越受到关注。尽管我们已经在困难任务中看到了许多令人印象深刻的机器人控制成果，但扩散模型的性能对调度参数的选择高度敏感，使得参数调整成为最关键的挑战之一。我们引入了基于线性路径模型的扩散（LP-MBD），它用受流匹配启发的线性概率路径取代了方差保持调度。这产生了一种几何上可解释且解耦的参数化方案，从而降低了调整复杂性，并为适应性提供了稳定的基础。在此基础上，我们提出了自适应LP-MBD（ALP-MBD），它利用强化学习来根据任务复杂性和环境条件调整扩散步数和噪声水平。通过数值研究、Brax基准测试和移动机器人轨迹跟踪，LP-MBD简化了调度，同时保持了强大的性能，而ALP-MBD进一步提高了鲁棒性、适应性和实时效率。

TLDR: This paper introduces Linear Path Model-Based Diffusion (LP-MBD) and Adaptive LP-MBD (ALP-MBD) for robotic control, simplifying diffusion model scheduling and enhancing robustness by adapting diffusion steps and noise levels using reinforcement learning.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了基于线性路径模型的扩散（LP-MBD）和自适应LP-MBD（ALP-MBD），用于机器人控制，通过强化学习调整扩散步长和噪声水平，简化了扩散模型调度并增强了鲁棒性。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Yutaka Shimizu, Masayoshi Tomizuka

PokeNet: Learning Kinematic Models of Articulated Objects from Human Observations

Abstract (English): Articulation modeling enables robots to learn joint parameters of articulated objects for effective manipulation which can then be used downstream for skill learning or planning. Existing approaches often rely on prior knowledge about the objects, such as the number or type of joints. Some of these approaches also fail to recover occluded joints that are only revealed during interaction. Others require large numbers of multi-view images for every object, which is impractical in real-world settings. Furthermore, prior works neglect the order of manipulations, which is essential for many multi-DoF objects where one joint must be operated before another, such as a dishwasher. We introduce PokeNet, an end-to-end framework that estimates articulation models from a single human demonstration without prior object knowledge. Given a sequence of point cloud observations of a human manipulating an unknown object, PokeNet predicts joint parameters, infers manipulation order, and tracks joint states over time. PokeNet outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods, improving joint axis and state estimation accuracy by an average of over 27% across diverse objects, including novel and unseen categories. We demonstrate these gains in both simulation and real-world environments.

摘要（中文）: 铰接建模使机器人能够学习铰接对象的关节参数，从而实现有效的操作，进而可用于下游技能学习或规划。现有方法通常依赖于关于对象的先验知识，例如关节的数量或类型。其中一些方法也无法恢复遮挡的关节，这些关节仅在交互过程中显现。其他方法则需要大量多视角图像，这在现实环境中是不切实际的。此外，先前的工作忽略了操作的顺序，这对于许多多自由度对象至关重要，其中一个关节必须在另一个关节之前操作，例如洗碗机。我们介绍 PokeNet，这是一个端到端框架，它可以在没有先验对象知识的情况下，从单个人类演示中估计铰接模型。给定一个人操纵未知对象的点云观测序列，PokeNet 预测关节参数，推断操作顺序，并随时间跟踪关节状态。PokeNet 优于现有的最先进方法，在各种对象（包括新颖和未见过的类别）中，关节轴和状态估计的准确率平均提高了 27% 以上。我们在模拟和真实世界环境中都展示了这些改进。

TLDR: PokeNet is a novel end-to-end framework for estimating articulation models of objects from a single human demonstration, without prior object knowledge, outperforming existing methods in joint axis and state estimation.

简要说明: PokeNet是一个新颖的端到端框架，仅通过一次人类演示即可估计物体的铰接模型，无需先验对象知识，在关节轴和状态估计方面优于现有方法。

Relevance: (7/10)
Novelty: (9/10)
Clarity: (8/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Anmol Gupta, Weiwei Gu, Omkar Patil, Jun Ki Lee, Nakul Gopalan

Hierarchical Entity-centric Reinforcement Learning with Factored Subgoal Diffusion

Abstract (English): We propose a hierarchical entity-centric framework for offline Goal-Conditioned Reinforcement Learning (GCRL) that combines subgoal decomposition with factored structure to solve long-horizon tasks in domains with multiple entities. Achieving long-horizon goals in complex environments remains a core challenge in Reinforcement Learning (RL). Domains with multiple entities are particularly difficult due to their combinatorial complexity. GCRL facilitates generalization across goals and the use of subgoal structure, but struggles with high-dimensional observations and combinatorial state-spaces, especially under sparse reward. We employ a two-level hierarchy composed of a value-based GCRL agent and a factored subgoal-generating conditional diffusion model. The RL agent and subgoal generator are trained independently and composed post hoc through selective subgoal generation based on the value function, making the approach modular and compatible with existing GCRL algorithms. We introduce new variations to benchmark tasks that highlight the challenges of multi-entity domains, and show that our method consistently boosts performance of the underlying RL agent on image-based long-horizon tasks with sparse rewards, achieving over 150% higher success rates on the hardest task in our suite and generalizing to increasing horizons and numbers of entities. Rollout videos are provided at: https://sites.google.com/view/hecrl

摘要（中文）: 我们提出了一种层级式的、以实体为中心的离线目标条件强化学习（GCRL）框架，该框架将子目标分解与分解结构相结合，以解决具有多个实体的领域中的长时域任务。在复杂环境中实现长时域目标仍然是强化学习（RL）中的核心挑战。由于其组合复杂性，具有多个实体的领域尤其困难。GCRL有助于跨目标的泛化和子目标结构的使用，但在高维观测和组合状态空间中表现不佳，尤其是在稀疏奖励下。我们采用一个两级层级结构，由一个基于价值的GCRL代理和一个分解的子目标生成条件扩散模型组成。RL代理和子目标生成器独立训练，并通过基于价值函数的选择性子目标生成进行事后组合，从而使该方法具有模块化特性，并与现有的GCRL算法兼容。我们引入了新的变体来基准测试任务，这些任务突出了多实体域的挑战，并表明我们的方法持续提升底层RL代理在基于图像的长时域稀疏奖励任务中的性能，在我们套件中最难的任务中实现了超过150%更高的成功率，并推广到增加的时域和实体数量。实验视频可在以下网址观看：https://sites.google.com/view/hecrl

TLDR: The paper introduces a hierarchical entity-centric reinforcement learning framework for solving long-horizon, multi-entity tasks by combining a value-based GCRL agent with a factored subgoal-generating conditional diffusion model, demonstrating improved performance in sparse reward environments.

简要说明: 该论文提出了一种分层的以实体为中心的强化学习框架，通过结合基于价值的GCRL智能体和因子化子目标生成条件扩散模型，来解决长时程、多实体的任务，并在稀疏奖励环境中表现出更好的性能。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Dan Haramati, Carl Qi, Tal Daniel, Amy Zhang, Aviv Tamar, George Konidaris

Flow Policy Gradients for Robot Control

Abstract (English): Likelihood-based policy gradient methods are the dominant approach for training robot control policies from rewards. These methods rely on differentiable action likelihoods, which constrain policy outputs to simple distributions like Gaussians. In this work, we show how flow matching policy gradients -- a recent framework that bypasses likelihood computation -- can be made effective for training and fine-tuning more expressive policies in challenging robot control settings. We introduce an improved objective that enables success in legged locomotion, humanoid motion tracking, and manipulation tasks, as well as robust sim-to-real transfer on two humanoid robots. We then present ablations and analysis on training dynamics. Results show how policies can exploit the flow representation for exploration when training from scratch, as well as improved fine-tuning robustness over baselines.

摘要（中文）: **基于似然的策略梯度方法是训练机器人控制策略以获得奖励的主流方法。这些方法依赖于可微的动作似然，这会将策略输出约束为诸如高斯分布之类的简单分布。 在这项工作中，我们展示了流程匹配策略梯度——一种绕过似然计算的最新框架——如何能够在具有挑战性的机器人控制环境中有效地训练和微调更具表现力的策略。 我们引入了一个改进的目标函数，该函数能够成功完成腿部运动、人形运动跟踪和操作任务，以及在两个人形机器人上稳健的模拟到真实转换。 然后，我们对训练动态进行消融研究和分析。 结果表明，策略如何在从头开始训练时利用流程表示进行探索，以及相对于基线，改进的微调鲁棒性。**

TLDR: This paper presents flow matching policy gradients for robot control, enabling the use of more expressive policies in challenging tasks and demonstrating improved performance in sim-to-real transfer compared to likelihood-based methods.

简要说明: 本文提出了用于机器人控制的流匹配策略梯度，从而能够在具有挑战性的任务中使用更具表现力的策略，并展示了与基于似然的方法相比，在从模拟到真实世界的迁移中得到改进的性能。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Brent Yi, Hongsuk Choi, Himanshu Gaurav Singh, Xiaoyu Huang, Takara E. Truong, Carmelo Sferrazza, Yi Ma, Rocky Duan, Pieter Abbeel, Guanya Shi, Karen Liu, Angjoo Kanazawa

HumanX: Toward Agile and Generalizable Humanoid Interaction Skills from Human Videos

Abstract (English): Enabling humanoid robots to perform agile and adaptive interactive tasks has long been a core challenge in robotics. Current approaches are bottlenecked by either the scarcity of realistic interaction data or the need for meticulous, task-specific reward engineering, which limits their scalability. To narrow this gap, we present HumanX, a full-stack framework that compiles human video into generalizable, real-world interaction skills for humanoids, without task-specific rewards. HumanX integrates two co-designed components: XGen, a data generation pipeline that synthesizes diverse and physically plausible robot interaction data from video while supporting scalable data augmentation; and XMimic, a unified imitation learning framework that learns generalizable interaction skills. Evaluated across five distinct domains--basketball, football, badminton, cargo pickup, and reactive fighting--HumanX successfully acquires 10 different skills and transfers them zero-shot to a physical Unitree G1 humanoid. The learned capabilities include complex maneuvers such as pump-fake turnaround fadeaway jumpshots without any external perception, as well as interactive tasks like sustained human-robot passing sequences over 10 consecutive cycles--learned from a single video demonstration. Our experiments show that HumanX achieves over 8 times higher generalization success than prior methods, demonstrating a scalable and task-agnostic pathway for learning versatile, real-world robot interactive skills.

摘要（中文）: 使人形机器人能够执行敏捷且自适应的交互任务一直是机器人领域的核心挑战。目前的方法要么受限于真实交互数据的稀缺性，要么需要细致的、特定于任务的奖励工程，这限制了它们的可扩展性。为了缩小这一差距，我们提出了HumanX，一个完整的框架，可以将人类视频编译成适用于人形机器人的、可泛化的、真实世界的交互技能，而无需特定于任务的奖励。HumanX集成了两个协同设计的组件：XGen，一个数据生成管道，可以从视频中合成多样且物理上合理的机器人交互数据，同时支持可扩展的数据增强；以及XMimic，一个统一的模仿学习框架，可以学习可泛化的交互技能。在五个不同的领域（篮球、足球、羽毛球、货物拾取和反应式格斗）中进行评估，HumanX成功地获得了10种不同的技能，并将它们零样本迁移到真实的宇树G1人形机器人上。所学习的能力包括复杂的动作，如无需任何外部感知就能完成的虚晃转身后仰跳投，以及交互式任务，如持续超过10个连续周期的机器人与人类传球序列——这些都是从单个视频演示中学习到的。我们的实验表明，HumanX实现了比现有方法高8倍以上的泛化成功率，展示了一种可扩展且与任务无关的途径，用于学习通用、真实的机器人交互技能。

TLDR: HumanX is a framework for training humanoid robots to perform various interactive tasks by learning from human videos without task-specific rewards. It achieves state-of-the-art generalization performance across multiple domains.

简要说明: HumanX是一个框架，通过从人类视频中学习，无需特定于任务的奖励，从而训练人形机器人执行各种交互任务。它在多个领域实现了最先进的泛化性能。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Yinhuai Wang, Qihan Zhao, Yuen Fui Lau, Runyi Yu, Hok Wai Tsui, Qifeng Chen, Jingbo Wang, Jiangmiao Pang, Ping Tan

Relationship-Aware Hierarchical 3D Scene Graph for Task Reasoning

Abstract (English): Representing and understanding 3D environments in a structured manner is crucial for autonomous agents to navigate and reason about their surroundings. While traditional Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) methods generate metric reconstructions and can be extended to metric-semantic mapping, they lack a higher level of abstraction and relational reasoning. To address this gap, 3D scene graphs have emerged as a powerful representation for capturing hierarchical structures and object relationships. In this work, we propose an enhanced hierarchical 3D scene graph that integrates open-vocabulary features across multiple abstraction levels and supports object-relational reasoning. Our approach leverages a Vision Language Model (VLM) to infer semantic relationships. Notably, we introduce a task reasoning module that combines Large Language Models (LLM) and a VLM to interpret the scene graph's semantic and relational information, enabling agents to reason about tasks and interact with their environment more intelligently. We validate our method by deploying it on a quadruped robot in multiple environments and tasks, highlighting its ability to reason about them.

摘要（中文）: 以结构化的方式表示和理解3D环境对于自主智能体导航和推理周围环境至关重要。虽然传统的同步定位与地图构建（SLAM）方法能够生成度量重建，并且可以扩展到度量语义地图构建，但它们缺乏更高层次的抽象和关系推理能力。 为解决这一问题，3D场景图作为一种强大的表示形式应运而生，用于捕捉层次结构和对象关系。 在这项工作中，我们提出了一种增强的层次化3D场景图，它整合了跨多个抽象层次的开放词汇特征，并支持对象关系推理。 我们的方法利用视觉语言模型（VLM）来推断语义关系。 值得注意的是，我们引入了一个任务推理模块，它结合了大型语言模型（LLM）和VLM来解释场景图的语义和关系信息，从而使智能体能够推理任务并更智能地与其环境进行交互。 我们通过在多个环境和任务中将我们的方法部署在四足机器人上进行了验证，突出了其推理能力。

TLDR: This paper introduces a hierarchical 3D scene graph that leverages VLMs and LLMs for task reasoning in robotic environments, enabling intelligent interaction and understanding of complex scenes.

简要说明: 本文提出了一种分层3D场景图，利用VLMs和LLMs进行机器人环境中的任务推理，能够智能地交互和理解复杂场景。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (8/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Albert Gassol Puigjaner, Angelos Zacharia, Kostas Alexis

3D Foundation Model-Based Loop Closing for Decentralized Collaborative SLAM

Abstract (English): Decentralized Collaborative Simultaneous Localization And Mapping (C-SLAM) techniques often struggle to identify map overlaps due to significant viewpoint variations among robots. Motivated by recent advancements in 3D foundation models, which can register images despite large viewpoint differences, we propose a robust loop closing approach that leverages these models to establish inter-robot measurements. In contrast to resource-intensive methods requiring full 3D reconstruction within a centralized map, our approach integrates foundation models into existing SLAM pipelines, yielding scalable and robust multi-robot mapping. Our contributions include: (1) integrating 3D foundation models to reliably estimate relative poses from monocular image pairs within decentralized C-SLAM; (2) introducing robust outlier mitigation techniques critical to the use of these relative poses; and (3) developing specialized pose graph optimization formulations that efficiently resolve scale ambiguities. We evaluate our method against state-of-the-art approaches, demonstrating improvements in localization and mapping accuracy, alongside significant gains in computational and memory efficiency. These results highlight the potential of our approach for deployment in large-scale multi-robot scenarios.

摘要（中文）: 摘要： 去中心化协同同步定位与地图构建(C-SLAM)技术常因机器人之间视角差异巨大而难以识别地图重叠区域。受近期3D基础模型进展的启发，该模型即使在视角差异很大的情况下也能进行图像配准，我们提出了一种鲁棒的闭环检测方法，利用这些模型来建立机器人间的测量。与需要在中心化地图中进行完整3D重建的资源密集型方法不同，我们的方法将基础模型集成到现有的SLAM流程中，从而实现可扩展且鲁棒的多机器人地图构建。我们的贡献包括：（1）集成3D基础模型，以在去中心化C-SLAM中可靠地估计单目图像对之间的相对位姿；（2）引入鲁棒的离群值抑制技术，这对于使用这些相对位姿至关重要；（3）开发专门的位姿图优化公式，可以有效地解决尺度模糊。我们针对最先进的方法评估了我们的方法，证明了在定位和地图构建精度方面的改进，以及在计算和内存效率方面的显著提高。这些结果突显了我们的方法在大型多机器人场景中部署的潜力。

TLDR: This paper proposes a decentralized collaborative SLAM approach leveraging 3D foundation models for robust loop closing and inter-robot pose estimation, achieving improved accuracy and efficiency in multi-robot mapping.

简要说明: 本文提出了一种分散式协作SLAM方法，利用3D基础模型实现鲁棒的闭环检测和机器人间姿态估计，从而在多机器人地图构建中实现更高的精度和效率。

Relevance: (7/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)

Authors: Pierre-Yves Lajoie, Benjamin Ramtoula, Daniele De Martini, Giovanni Beltrame

Multi-Agent Monte Carlo Tree Search for Makespan-Efficient Object Rearrangement in Cluttered Spaces

Abstract (English): Object rearrangement planning in complex, cluttered environments is a common challenge in warehouses, households, and rescue sites. Prior studies largely address monotone instances, whereas real-world tasks are often non-monotone-objects block one another and must be temporarily relocated to intermediate positions before reaching their final goals. In such settings, effective multi-agent collaboration can substantially reduce the time required to complete tasks. This paper introduces Centralized, Asynchronous, Multi-agent Monte Carlo Tree Search (CAM-MCTS), a novel framework for general-purpose makespan-efficient object rearrangement planning in challenging environments. CAM-MCTS combines centralized task assignment-where agents remain aware of each other's intended actions to facilitate globally optimized planning-with an asynchronous task execution strategy that enables agents to take on new tasks at appropriate time steps, rather than waiting for others, guided by a one-step look-ahead cost estimate. This design minimizes idle time, prevents unnecessary synchronization delays, and enhances overall system efficiency. We evaluate CAM-MCTS across a diverse set of monotone and non-monotone tasks in cluttered environments, demonstrating consistent reductions in makespan compared to strong baselines. Finally, we validate our approach on a real-world multi-agent system under different configurations, further confirming its effectiveness and robustness.

摘要（中文）: 在复杂、拥挤的环境中进行物体重排规划是仓库、家庭和救援现场面临的常见挑战。先前研究主要集中在单调实例上，然而现实世界的任务通常是非单调的——物体之间相互阻挡，必须临时重新定位到中间位置才能到达最终目标。在这种情况下，有效的多智能体协作可以显著减少完成任务所需的时间。本文介绍了一种集中式、异步、多智能体蒙特卡洛树搜索 (CAM-MCTS)，这是一个用于在具有挑战性的环境中进行通用、最大完工时间高效的物体重排规划的新型框架。CAM-MCTS将集中式任务分配——智能体始终感知彼此的预期动作，以促进全局优化规划——与异步任务执行策略相结合。该策略使智能体能够在适当的时间步接受新任务，而不是等待其他智能体，且受一步前瞻成本估计的指导。这种设计最大限度地减少了空闲时间，避免了不必要的同步延迟，并提高了整体系统效率。我们在拥挤环境中对各种单调和非单调任务进行了CAM-MCTS的评估，证明与强大的基线相比，最大完工时间始终有所减少。最后，我们在不同配置下的真实多智能体系统上验证了我们的方法，进一步证实了其有效性和鲁棒性。

TLDR: The paper introduces CAM-MCTS, a novel multi-agent Monte Carlo Tree Search framework for makespan-efficient object rearrangement in cluttered environments, demonstrating its effectiveness in both simulated and real-world scenarios compared to existing baselines.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了一种新颖的名为CAM-MCTS的多智能体蒙特卡洛树搜索框架，用于在拥挤环境中实现makespan高效的物体重排。实验表明，与现有基线相比，该方法在模拟和真实场景中均表现出有效性。
