Authors: Hanwen Ren, Junyong Kim, Aathman Tharmasanthiran, Ahmed H. Qureshi

Zero-shot large vision-language model prompting for automated bone identification in paleoradiology x-ray archives

Abstract (English): Paleoradiology, the use of modern imaging technologies to study archaeological and anthropological remains, offers new windows on millennial scale patterns of human health. Unfortunately, the radiographs collected during field campaigns are heterogeneous: bones are disarticulated, positioning is ad hoc, and laterality markers are often absent. Additionally, factors such as age at death, age of bone, sex, and imaging equipment introduce high variability. Thus, content navigation, such as identifying a subset of images with a specific projection view, can be time consuming and difficult, making efficient triaging a bottleneck for expert analysis. We report a zero shot prompting strategy that leverages a state of the art Large Vision Language Model (LVLM) to automatically identify the main bone, projection view, and laterality in such images. Our pipeline converts raw DICOM files to bone windowed PNGs, submits them to the LVLM with a carefully engineered prompt, and receives structured JSON outputs, which are extracted and formatted onto a spreadsheet in preparation for validation. On a random sample of 100 images reviewed by an expert board certified paleoradiologist, the system achieved 92% main bone accuracy, 80% projection view accuracy, and 100% laterality accuracy, with low or medium confidence flags for ambiguous cases. These results suggest that LVLMs can substantially accelerate code word development for large paleoradiology datasets, allowing for efficient content navigation in future anthropology workflows.

摘要（中文）: 古辐射影像学，即利用现代影像技术研究考古学和人类学遗骸，为理解千年尺度上的人类健康模式提供了新的视角。然而，在野外考察中收集的X光片往往是异质的：骨骼是分离的，摆位是临时的，并且通常缺乏侧位标记。此外，死亡年龄、骨骼年龄、性别和成像设备等因素引入了高度的变异性。因此，诸如识别具有特定投照体位的图像子集等内容导航，可能非常耗时且困难，使得高效的分诊成为专家分析的瓶颈。我们提出了一种零样本提示策略，利用最先进的大型视觉语言模型(LVLM)来自动识别此类图像中的主要骨骼、投照体位和侧位。我们的流程将原始DICOM文件转换为骨窗PNG图像，使用精心设计的提示将其提交给LVLM，并接收结构化的JSON输出，这些输出被提取并格式化到电子表格中，以便进行验证。在一个由专家委员会认证的古辐射影像学家审查的100张图像的随机样本上，该系统实现了92%的主要骨骼准确率、80%的投照体位准确率和100%的侧位准确率，对模糊案例设置了低或中等置信度标志。这些结果表明，LVLM可以显著加速大型古辐射影像数据集的编码字开发，从而在未来的人类学工作流程中实现高效的内容导航。

TLDR: This paper explores using a zero-shot prompting strategy with a Large Vision Language Model (LVLM) to automate bone identification and view classification in paleoradiology X-ray images, achieving promising accuracy for bone, projection, and laterality identification.

简要说明: 本文探讨了使用大型视觉语言模型(LVLM)的零样本提示策略，以自动识别古放射学X射线图像中的骨骼并进行视图分类，在骨骼、投影和侧面的鉴定方面取得了可喜的准确性。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Owen Dong, Lily Gao, Manish Kota, Bennett A. Landmana, Jelena Bekvalac, Gaynor Western, Katherine D. Van Schaik

TodyComm: Task-Oriented Dynamic Communication for Multi-Round LLM-based Multi-Agent System

Abstract (English): Multi-round LLM-based multi-agent systems rely on effective communication structures to support collaboration across rounds. However, most existing methods employ a fixed communication topology during inference, which falls short in many realistic applications where the agents' roles may change \textit{across rounds} due to dynamic adversary, task progression, or time-varying constraints such as communication bandwidth. In this paper, we propose addressing this issue through TodyComm, a \textbf{t}ask-\textbf{o}riented \textbf{dy}namic \textbf{comm}unication algorithm. It produces behavior-driven collaboration topologies that adapt to the dynamics at each round, optimizing the utility for the task through policy gradient. Experiments on five benchmarks demonstrate that under both dynamic adversary and communications budgets, TodyComm delivers superior task effectiveness while retaining token efficiency and scalability.

摘要（中文）: 基于多轮大型语言模型的多智能体系统依赖于有效的通信结构来支持跨轮次的协作。然而，大多数现有方法在推理过程中采用固定的通信拓扑，这在许多实际应用中存在不足，因为在这些应用中，智能体的角色可能由于动态对抗、任务进展或时变约束（如通信带宽）而 \textit{跨轮次} 发生变化。在本文中，我们提出通过 TodyComm 来解决这个问题，这是一种 \textbf{任}务导向的 \textbf{动}态 \textbf{通}信算法。它产生行为驱动的协作拓扑，以适应每个轮次的动态变化，并通过策略梯度优化任务的效用。在五个基准测试上的实验表明，在动态对抗和通信预算的限制下，TodyComm 在保持令牌效率和可扩展性的同时，提供了更优越的任务有效性。

TLDR: The paper introduces TodyComm, a dynamic communication algorithm for multi-agent LLM systems that adapts communication topologies across rounds based on task dynamics, demonstrating improved effectiveness and efficiency in experiments.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了一种名为TodyComm的动态通信算法，用于多智能体LLM系统。该算法可以根据任务动态调整跨轮次的通信拓扑，实验结果表明其有效性和效率均有所提高。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)

Authors: Wenzhe Fan, Tommaso Tognoli, Henry Peng Zou, Chunyu Miao, Yibo Wang, Xinhua Zhang

Mitigating Conversational Inertia in Multi-Turn Agents

Abstract (English): Large language models excel as few-shot learners when provided with appropriate demonstrations, yet this strength becomes problematic in multiturn agent scenarios, where LLMs erroneously mimic their own previous responses as few-shot examples. Through attention analysis, we identify conversational inertia, a phenomenon where models exhibit strong diagonal attention to previous responses, which is associated with imitation bias that constrains exploration. This reveals a tension when transforming few-shot LLMs into agents: longer context enriches environmental feedback for exploitation, yet also amplifies conversational inertia that undermines exploration. Our key insight is that for identical states, actions generated with longer contexts exhibit stronger inertia than those with shorter contexts, enabling construction of preference pairs without environment rewards. Based on this, we propose Context Preference Learning to calibrate model preferences to favor low-inertia responses over highinertia ones. We further provide context management strategies at inference time to balance exploration and exploitation. Experimental results across eight agentic environments and one deep research scenario validate that our framework reduces conversational inertia and achieves performance improvements.

摘要（中文）: 当提供合适的演示时，大型语言模型作为少样本学习者表现出色，然而，这种优势在多轮代理场景中会成为问题，因为大型语言模型会错误地模仿自己先前的响应作为少样本示例。通过注意力分析，我们识别出对话惯性，一种模型对先前响应表现出强烈对角注意力的现象，这与限制探索的模仿偏差相关。这揭示了将少样本大型语言模型转化为代理时存在的张力：更长的上下文会丰富强化利用的环境反馈，但也放大了破坏探索的对话惯性。我们的关键见解是，对于相同的状态，使用更长上下文生成的动作比使用更短上下文生成的动作表现出更强的惯性，从而能够在没有环境奖励的情况下构建偏好对。基于此，我们提出了上下文偏好学习，以校准模型偏好，使其偏向低惯性响应而不是高惯性响应。我们进一步提供了推理时的上下文管理策略，以平衡探索和利用。在八个代理环境和一个深度研究场景中的实验结果验证了我们的框架能够减少对话惯性并实现性能提升。

TLDR: This paper addresses conversational inertia in multi-turn LLM agents, where models overly imitate their own previous responses, by introducing Context Preference Learning to calibrate model preferences and context management strategies to balance exploration and exploitation.

简要说明: 本文通过引入上下文偏好学习来校准模型偏好，并通过上下文管理策略来平衡探索与利用，从而解决多轮LLM代理中模型过度模仿自身先前响应的会话惯性问题。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Yang Wan, Zheng Cao, Zhenhao Zhang, Zhengwen Zeng, Shuheng Shen, Changhua Meng, Linchao Zhu

A Lightweight Library for Energy-Based Joint-Embedding Predictive Architectures

Abstract (English): We present EB-JEPA, an open-source library for learning representations and world models using Joint-Embedding Predictive Architectures (JEPAs). JEPAs learn to predict in representation space rather than pixel space, avoiding the pitfalls of generative modeling while capturing semantically meaningful features suitable for downstream tasks. Our library provides modular, self-contained implementations that illustrate how representation learning techniques developed for image-level self-supervised learning can transfer to video, where temporal dynamics add complexity, and ultimately to action-conditioned world models, where the model must additionally learn to predict the effects of control inputs. Each example is designed for single-GPU training within a few hours, making energy-based self-supervised learning accessible for research and education. We provide ablations of JEA components on CIFAR-10. Probing these representations yields 91% accuracy, indicating that the model learns useful features. Extending to video, we include a multi-step prediction example on Moving MNIST that demonstrates how the same principles scale to temporal modeling. Finally, we show how these representations can drive action-conditioned world models, achieving a 97% planning success rate on the Two Rooms navigation task. Comprehensive ablations reveal the critical importance of each regularization component for preventing representation collapse. Code is available at https://github.com/facebookresearch/eb_jepa.

摘要（中文）: 我们提出了EB-JEPA，一个开源库，用于使用联合嵌入预测架构（JEPAs）学习表征和世界模型。JEPAs学习在表征空间而非像素空间中进行预测，从而避免了生成式建模的缺陷，同时捕获语义上有意义的特征，适用于下游任务。我们的库提供模块化、自包含的实现，展示了为图像级别自监督学习开发的表征学习技术如何迁移到视频领域，其中时间动态增加了复杂性，并最终迁移到动作条件世界模型，其中模型必须额外学习预测控制输入的影响。每个示例都设计为在几个小时内完成单GPU训练，使得基于能量的自监督学习可用于研究和教育。我们提供了JЕPA组件在CIFAR-10上的消融实验。对这些表征的探测产生了91%的准确率，表明该模型学习了有用的特征。扩展到视频领域，我们包含了一个Moving MNIST上的多步预测示例，展示了相同的原理如何扩展到时间建模。最后，我们展示了这些表征如何驱动动作条件世界模型，在Two Rooms导航任务中实现了97%的规划成功率。全面的消融实验揭示了每个正则化组件对于防止表征崩溃的关键重要性。代码可在 https://github.com/facebookresearch/eb_jepa 获取。

TLDR: The paper introduces EB-JEPA, a lightweight, open-source library for learning representations and energy-based world models using Joint-Embedding Predictive Architectures, demonstrating its applicability to image, video, and action-conditioned world models with promising results and thorough ablations.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了 EB-JEPA，一个轻量级的开源库，用于使用联合嵌入预测架构学习表示和基于能量的世界模型，展示了其在图像、视频和动作条件世界模型中的适用性，并取得了有希望的结果和彻底的消融研究。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)

Authors: Basile Terver, Randall Balestriero, Megi Dervishi, David Fan, Quentin Garrido, Tushar Nagarajan, Koustuv Sinha, Wancong Zhang, Mike Rabbat, Yann LeCun, Amir Bar

HY3D-Bench: Generation of 3D Assets

Abstract (English): While recent advances in neural representations and generative models have revolutionized 3D content creation, the field remains constrained by significant data processing bottlenecks. To address this, we introduce HY3D-Bench, an open-source ecosystem designed to establish a unified, high-quality foundation for 3D generation. Our contributions are threefold: (1) We curate a library of 250k high-fidelity 3D objects distilled from large-scale repositories, employing a rigorous pipeline to deliver training-ready artifacts, including watertight meshes and multi-view renderings; (2) We introduce structured part-level decomposition, providing the granularity essential for fine-grained perception and controllable editing; and (3) We bridge real-world distribution gaps via a scalable AIGC synthesis pipeline, contributing 125k synthetic assets to enhance diversity in long-tail categories. Validated empirically through the training of Hunyuan3D-2.1-Small, HY3D-Bench democratizes access to robust data resources, aiming to catalyze innovation across 3D perception, robotics, and digital content creation.

摘要（中文）: 最近在神经表示和生成模型方面的进展已经彻底改变了3D内容创建，但该领域仍然受到显著的数据处理瓶颈的限制。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了HY3D-Bench，这是一个开源生态系统，旨在为3D生成建立一个统一、高质量的基础。我们的贡献有三方面：（1）我们精心策划了一个包含25万个高保真3D对象的库，这些对象来自大规模存储库，并采用严格的流程来提供可用于训练的工件，包括水密网格和多视图渲染；（2）我们引入了结构化的部件级分解，提供了细粒度感知和可控编辑所需的粒度；（3）我们通过可扩展的AIGC合成流程弥合了真实世界分布的差距，贡献了12.5万个合成资产，以增强长尾类别中的多样性。通过Hunyuan3D-2.1-Small的训练进行了经验验证，HY3D-Bench普及了对强大的数据资源的访问，旨在促进3D感知、机器人和数字内容创作领域的创新。

TLDR: HY3D-Bench introduces a large-scale, high-quality 3D asset dataset with part-level decomposition and synthetic data augmentation to facilitate research in 3D perception, robotics, and digital content creation.

简要说明: HY3D-Bench 提出了一个大规模、高质量的 3D 资产数据集，具有零件级分解和合成数据增强功能，旨在促进 3D 感知、机器人技术和数字内容创作领域的研究。

Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Team Hunyuan3D, :, Bowen Zhang, Chunchao Guo, Dongyuan Guo, Haolin Liu, Hongyu Yan, Huiwen Shi, Jiaao Yu, Jiachen Xu, Jingwei Huang, Kunhong Li, Lifu Wang, Linus, Penghao Wang, Qingxiang Lin, Ruining Tang, Xianghui Yang, Yang Li, Yirui Guan, Yunfei Zhao, Yunhan Yang, Zeqiang Lai, Zhihao Liang, Zibo Zhao

Reparameterization Flow Policy Optimization

Abstract (English): Reparameterization Policy Gradient (RPG) has emerged as a powerful paradigm for model-based reinforcement learning, enabling high sample efficiency by backpropagating gradients through differentiable dynamics. However, prior RPG approaches have been predominantly restricted to Gaussian policies, limiting their performance and failing to leverage recent advances in generative models. In this work, we identify that flow policies, which generate actions via differentiable ODE integration, naturally align with the RPG framework, a connection not established in prior work. However, naively exploiting this synergy proves ineffective, often suffering from training instability and a lack of exploration. We propose Reparameterization Flow Policy Optimization (RFO). RFO computes policy gradients by backpropagating jointly through the flow generation process and system dynamics, unlocking high sample efficiency without requiring intractable log-likelihood calculations. RFO includes two tailored regularization terms for stability and exploration. We also propose a variant of RFO with action chunking. Extensive experiments on diverse locomotion and manipulation tasks, involving both rigid and soft bodies with state or visual inputs, demonstrate the effectiveness of RFO. Notably, on a challenging locomotion task controlling a soft-body quadruped, RFO achieves almost $2\times$ the reward of the state-of-the-art baseline.

摘要（中文）: 重参数化策略梯度(RPG)已成为一种强大的基于模型的强化学习范例，通过可微动力学进行梯度反向传播，实现了高样本效率。然而，以往的RPG方法主要局限于高斯策略，限制了它们的性能，并且未能利用生成模型的最新进展。在这项工作中，我们发现流式策略通过可微ODE积分生成动作，与RPG框架天然契合，而这一联系在以往的研究中并未建立。然而，天真地利用这种协同作用证明是无效的，经常会遭受训练不稳定和缺乏探索的问题。我们提出了重参数化流式策略优化(RFO)。RFO通过联合反向传播流式生成过程和系统动力学来计算策略梯度，在不需要难以处理的对数似然计算的情况下解锁高样本效率。RFO包含两个针对稳定性和探索的定制正则化项。我们还提出了RFO的一个变体，具有动作分块功能。包括刚性和柔性物体，以及状态或视觉输入的，在各种运动和操纵任务上的大量实验证明了RFO的有效性。值得注意的是，在控制柔性四足动物的具有挑战性的运动任务中，RFO获得了几乎是当前最佳基线算法两倍的奖励。

TLDR: This paper introduces Reparameterization Flow Policy Optimization (RFO), a novel reinforcement learning method leveraging flow policies and reparameterization gradients for improved sample efficiency and performance, especially on challenging robotics tasks.

简要说明: 本文介绍了重参数化流策略优化（RFO），一种新的强化学习方法，利用流策略和重参数化梯度来提高样本效率和性能，尤其是在具有挑战性的机器人任务中。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (9/10)
Clarity: (8/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)

Authors: Hai Zhong, Zhuoran Li, Xun Wang, Longbo Huang

DeepDFA: Injecting Temporal Logic in Deep Learning for Sequential Subsymbolic Applications

Abstract (English): Integrating logical knowledge into deep neural network training is still a hard challenge, especially for sequential or temporally extended domains involving subsymbolic observations. To address this problem, we propose DeepDFA, a neurosymbolic framework that integrates high-level temporal logic - expressed as Deterministic Finite Automata (DFA) or Moore Machines - into neural architectures. DeepDFA models temporal rules as continuous, differentiable layers, enabling symbolic knowledge injection into subsymbolic domains. We demonstrate how DeepDFA can be used in two key settings: (i) static image sequence classification, and (ii) policy learning in interactive non-Markovian environments. Across extensive experiments, DeepDFA outperforms traditional deep learning models (e.g., LSTMs, GRUs, Transformers) and novel neuro-symbolic systems, achieving state-of-the-art results in temporal knowledge integration. These results highlight the potential of DeepDFA to bridge subsymbolic learning and symbolic reasoning in sequential tasks.

摘要（中文）: 将逻辑知识集成到深度神经网络训练中仍然是一个艰巨的挑战，尤其是在涉及亚符号观察的序列或时间扩展领域。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了DeepDFA，一个神经符号框架，它将高层时间逻辑（表达为确定性有限自动机（DFA）或摩尔机）集成到神经架构中。DeepDFA将时间规则建模为连续的、可微分的层，从而能够将符号知识注入到亚符号领域中。我们展示了DeepDFA如何应用于两个关键场景：（i）静态图像序列分类，以及（ii）交互式的非马尔可夫环境中的策略学习。通过广泛的实验，DeepDFA优于传统的深度学习模型（例如，LSTMs、GRUs、Transformers）和新型神经符号系统，在时间知识集成方面取得了最先进的结果。这些结果突出了DeepDFA在序列任务中桥接亚符号学习和符号推理的潜力。

TLDR: DeepDFA injects temporal logic (DFAs) into neural networks for sequential tasks, outperforming standard deep learning and neuro-symbolic methods in image sequence classification and policy learning.

简要说明: DeepDFA将时序逻辑（DFAs）注入到神经网络中，用于处理序列任务，并在图像序列分类和策略学习方面优于标准深度学习和神经符号方法。

Relevance: (7/10)

Authors: Elena Umili, Francesco Argenziano, Roberto Capobianco

IntentRL: Training Proactive User-intent Agents for Open-ended Deep Research via Reinforcement Learning

Abstract (English): Deep Research (DR) agents extend Large Language Models (LLMs) beyond parametric knowledge by autonomously retrieving and synthesizing evidence from large web corpora into long-form reports, enabling a long-horizon agentic paradigm. However, unlike real-time conversational assistants, DR is computationally expensive and time-consuming, creating an autonomy-interaction dilemma: high autonomy on ambiguous user queries often leads to prolonged execution with unsatisfactory outcomes. To address this, we propose IntentRL, a framework that trains proactive agents to clarify latent user intents before starting long-horizon research. To overcome the scarcity of open-ended research data, we introduce a scalable pipeline that expands a few seed samples into high-quality dialogue turns via a shallow-to-deep intent refinement graph. We further adopt a two-stage reinforcement learning (RL) strategy: Stage I applies RL on offline dialogues to efficiently learn general user-interaction behavior, while Stage II uses the trained agent and a user simulator for online rollouts to strengthen adaptation to diverse user feedback. Extensive experiments show that IntentRL significantly improves both intent hit rate and downstream task performance, outperforming the built-in clarify modules of closed-source DR agents and proactive LLM baselines.

摘要（中文）: 深度研究（DR）代理通过自主地从大型网络语料库中检索和合成证据，并将其汇集成长篇报告，从而将大型语言模型（LLMs）的能力扩展到参数知识之外，从而实现了一种长程代理范式。然而，与实时对话助手不同，DR在计算上开销巨大且耗时，这造成了一种自主性与交互之间的困境：在模糊的用户查询上高度自主通常会导致长时间的执行，但结果却不尽如人意。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了IntentRL，一个训练主动代理在启动长程研究之前澄清潜在用户意图的框架。为了克服开放式研究数据的稀缺性，我们引入了一个可扩展的流水线，通过浅层到深层意图细化图将少量种子样本扩展为高质量的对话回合。我们进一步采用了一种两阶段强化学习（RL）策略：第一阶段在离线对话上应用RL，以有效学习一般的用户交互行为，而第二阶段则使用训练好的代理和一个用户模拟器进行在线rollout，以加强对多样化用户反馈的适应性。大量实验表明，IntentRL显著提高了意图命中率和下游任务性能，优于封闭源DR代理的内置澄清模块和主动LLM基线。

TLDR: The paper introduces IntentRL, a reinforcement learning framework for training proactive agents that clarify user intent in open-ended deep research tasks, improving performance and reducing computational cost.

简要说明: 本文介绍了IntentRL，一个强化学习框架，用于训练主动型智能体，在开放式深度研究任务中澄清用户意图，从而提高性能并降低计算成本。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)

Authors: Haohao Luo, Zexi Li, Yuexiang Xie, Wenhao Zhang, Yaliang Li, Ying Shen

Chain-of-Goals Hierarchical Policy for Long-Horizon Offline Goal-Conditioned RL

Abstract (English): Offline goal-conditioned reinforcement learning remains challenging for long-horizon tasks. While hierarchical approaches mitigate this issue by decomposing tasks, most existing methods rely on separate high- and low-level networks and generate only a single intermediate subgoal, making them inadequate for complex tasks that require coordinating multiple intermediate decisions. To address this limitation, we draw inspiration from the chain-of-thought paradigm and propose the Chain-of-Goals Hierarchical Policy (CoGHP), a novel framework that reformulates hierarchical decision-making as autoregressive sequence modeling within a unified architecture. Given a state and a final goal, CoGHP autoregressively generates a sequence of latent subgoals followed by the primitive action, where each latent subgoal acts as a reasoning step that conditions subsequent predictions. To implement this efficiently, we pioneer the use of an MLP-Mixer backbone, which supports cross-token communication and captures structural relationships among state, goal, latent subgoals, and action. Across challenging navigation and manipulation benchmarks, CoGHP consistently outperforms strong offline baselines, demonstrating improved performance on long-horizon tasks.

摘要（中文）: 离线目标条件强化学习在处理长时程任务时仍然具有挑战性。分层方法通过分解任务来缓解这个问题，但大多数现有方法依赖于分离的高层和低层网络，并且仅生成单个中间子目标，这使得它们不足以应对需要协调多个中间决策的复杂任务。为了解决这一局限性，我们从思维链范式中汲取灵感，并提出了目标链式分层策略（CoGHP），这是一种新颖的框架，它将分层决策制定重新定义为统一架构中的自回归序列建模。给定一个状态和一个最终目标，CoGHP自回归地生成一个潜在子目标序列，然后生成原始动作，其中每个潜在子目标都充当一个推理步骤，用以调节后续预测。为了有效地实现这一点，我们率先使用了MLP-Mixer骨干网络，它支持跨token通信并捕获状态、目标、潜在子目标和动作之间的结构关系。在具有挑战性的导航和操作基准测试中，CoGHP始终优于强大的离线基线，证明了其在长时程任务上的性能提升。

TLDR: The paper introduces Chain-of-Goals Hierarchical Policy (CoGHP), a novel offline goal-conditioned RL framework using autoregressive sequence modeling with an MLP-Mixer backbone for long-horizon tasks, showing strong performance on navigation and manipulation benchmarks.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了目标链式分层策略 (CoGHP)，这是一种新颖的离线目标条件强化学习框架，它采用自回归序列建模和 MLP-Mixer 主干网络来处理长时域任务，并在导航和操作基准测试中表现出色。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (8/10)

Authors: Jinwoo Choi, Sang-Hyun Lee, Seung-Woo Seo

MedSAM-Agent: Empowering Interactive Medical Image Segmentation with Multi-turn Agentic Reinforcement Learning

Abstract (English): Medical image segmentation is evolving from task-specific models toward generalizable frameworks. Recent research leverages Multi-modal Large Language Models (MLLMs) as autonomous agents, employing reinforcement learning with verifiable reward (RLVR) to orchestrate specialized tools like the Segment Anything Model (SAM). However, these approaches often rely on single-turn, rigid interaction strategies and lack process-level supervision during training, which hinders their ability to fully exploit the dynamic potential of interactive tools and leads to redundant actions. To bridge this gap, we propose MedSAM-Agent, a framework that reformulates interactive segmentation as a multi-step autonomous decision-making process. First, we introduce a hybrid prompting strategy for expert-curated trajectory generation, enabling the model to internalize human-like decision heuristics and adaptive refinement strategies. Furthermore, we develop a two-stage training pipeline that integrates multi-turn, end-to-end outcome verification with a clinical-fidelity process reward design to promote interaction parsimony and decision efficiency. Extensive experiments across 6 medical modalities and 21 datasets demonstrate that MedSAM-Agent achieves state-of-the-art performance, effectively unifying autonomous medical reasoning with robust, iterative optimization. Code is available \href{https://github.com/CUHK-AIM-Group/MedSAM-Agent}{here}.

摘要（中文）: 医学图像分割正从特定任务模型向通用化框架演进。近期的研究利用多模态大型语言模型（MLLMs）作为自主代理，采用具有可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）来协调像分割一切模型（SAM）这样的专业工具。然而，这些方法通常依赖于单轮、僵化的交互策略，并且在训练期间缺乏过程层面的监督，这阻碍了它们充分利用交互工具的动态潜力，并导致冗余动作。为了弥合这一差距，我们提出了MedSAM-Agent，一个将交互式分割重构为多步骤自主决策过程的框架。首先，我们引入了一种混合提示策略，用于专家策划的轨迹生成，使模型能够内化类人决策启发式和自适应优化策略。此外，我们开发了一个两阶段训练管道，集成了多轮、端到端的结果验证和临床保真度的过程奖励设计，以促进交互的简约性和决策效率。在6种医学模态和21个数据集上的广泛实验表明，MedSAM-Agent实现了最先进的性能，有效地统一了自主医学推理与鲁棒的迭代优化。代码可在\href{https://github.com/CUHK-AIM-Group/MedSAM-Agent}{此处}获取。

TLDR: MedSAM-Agent improves interactive medical image segmentation by using multi-turn reinforcement learning with a novel prompting strategy and training pipeline to achieve state-of-the-art performance across various medical modalities.

简要说明: MedSAM-Agent 通过使用多轮强化学习、一种新颖的提示策略和训练流程，改进了交互式医学图像分割，并在各种医学模式中实现了最先进的性能。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)

Authors: Shengyuan Liu, Liuxin Bao, Qi Yang, Wanting Geng, Boyun Zheng, Chenxin Li, Wenting Chen, Houwen Peng, Yixuan Yuan

Hand3R: Online 4D Hand-Scene Reconstruction in the Wild

Abstract (English): For Embodied AI, jointly reconstructing dynamic hands and the dense scene context is crucial for understanding physical interaction. However, most existing methods recover isolated hands in local coordinates, overlooking the surrounding 3D environment. To address this, we present Hand3R, the first online framework for joint 4D hand-scene reconstruction from monocular video. Hand3R synergizes a pre-trained hand expert with a 4D scene foundation model via a scene-aware visual prompting mechanism. By injecting high-fidelity hand priors into a persistent scene memory, our approach enables simultaneous reconstruction of accurate hand meshes and dense metric-scale scene geometry in a single forward pass. Experiments demonstrate that Hand3R bypasses the reliance on offline optimization and delivers competitive performance in both local hand reconstruction and global positioning.

摘要（中文）: 对于具身人工智能而言，联合重建动态手部和稠密场景上下文对于理解物理交互至关重要。然而，大多数现有方法恢复的是局部坐标系下孤立的手部，忽略了周围的三维环境。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 Hand3R，这是第一个从单目视频中进行联合四维手部-场景重建的在线框架。Hand3R 通过场景感知视觉提示机制，将预训练的手部专家与四维场景基础模型协同工作。通过将高保真手部先验注入到持久的场景记忆中，我们的方法能够在一次前向传递中同时重建精确的手部网格和稠密的度量尺度场景几何。实验表明，Hand3R 绕过了对离线优化的依赖，并在局部手部重建和全局定位方面都具有竞争力的性能。

TLDR: Hand3R is a novel online framework for jointly reconstructing dynamic hands and dense 3D scenes from monocular video, addressing the limitations of existing methods that focus on isolated hand reconstruction.

简要说明: Hand3R 是一个新颖的在线框架，用于从单目视频中联合重建动态手部和稠密3D场景，解决了现有方法侧重于孤立手部重建的局限性。

Relevance: (7/10)
Novelty: (9/10)
Clarity: (8/10)

Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Wendi Hu, Haonan Zhou, Wenhao Hu, Gaoang Wang

Reinforcement Learning with Promising Tokens for Large Language Models

Abstract (English): Reinforcement learning (RL) has emerged as a key paradigm for aligning and optimizing large language models (LLMs). Standard approaches treat the LLM as the policy and apply RL directly over the full vocabulary space. However, this formulation includes the massive tail of contextually irrelevant tokens in the action space, which could distract the policy from focusing on decision-making among the truly reasonable tokens. In this work, we verify that valid reasoning paths could inherently concentrate within a low-rank subspace. Based on this insight, we introduce Reinforcement Learning with Promising Tokens (RLPT), a framework that mitigates the action space issue by decoupling strategic decision-making from token generation. Specifically, RLPT leverages the semantic priors of the base model to identify a dynamic set of \emph{promising tokens} and constrains policy optimization exclusively to this refined subset via masking. Theoretical analysis and empirical results demonstrate that RLPT effectively reduces gradient variance, stabilizes the training process, and improves sample efficiency. Experiment results on math, coding, and telecom reasoning show that RLPT outperforms standard RL baselines and integrates effectively across various model sizes (4B and 8B) and RL algorithms (GRPO and DAPO).

摘要（中文）: 强化学习（RL）已成为对齐和优化大型语言模型（LLMs）的关键范式。标准方法将LLM视为策略，并在完整的词汇空间上直接应用RL。然而，这种表述包含了大量在上下文中不相关的token，这些token可能会分散策略的注意力，使其无法专注于在真正合理的token之间进行决策。在这项工作中，我们验证了有效的推理路径本质上可以集中在低秩子空间内。基于这一洞察，我们提出了基于有希望的Token的强化学习（RLPT），该框架通过将战略决策与token生成解耦来缓解行动空间问题。具体来说，RLPT利用基础模型的语义先验来识别一组动态的\emph{有希望的Token}，并通过掩码将策略优化专门限制在这个精炼的子集上。理论分析和实验结果表明，RLPT能有效降低梯度方差，稳定训练过程，并提高样本效率。在数学、代码和电信推理上的实验结果表明，RLPT优于标准RL基线，并能有效地集成到各种模型规模（4B和8B）和RL算法（GRPO和DAPO）中。

TLDR: The paper introduces Reinforcement Learning with Promising Tokens (RLPT), a method that improves RL for LLMs by focusing the action space on a dynamic set of relevant tokens, leading to better performance and stability.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了具有前景令牌的强化学习（RLPT），这是一种通过将动作空间集中在一组动态的相关令牌上来改进LLM的RL的方法，从而带来更好的性能和稳定性。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Jing-Cheng Pang, Liang Lu, Xian Tang, Kun Jiang, Sijie Wu, Kai Zhang, Xubin Li

General Agents Contain World Models, even under Partial Observability and Stochasticity

Abstract (English): Deciding whether an agent possesses a model of its surrounding world is a fundamental step toward understanding its capabilities and limitations. In [10], it was shown that, within a particular framework, every almost optimal and general agent necessarily contains sufficient knowledge of its environment to allow an approximate reconstruction of it by querying the agent as a black box. This result relied on the assumptions that the agent is deterministic and that the environment is fully observable. In this work, we remove both assumptions by extending the theorem to stochastic agents operating in partially observable environments. Fundamentally, this shows that stochastic agents cannot avoid learning their environment through the usage of randomization. We also strengthen the result by weakening the notion of generality, proving that less powerful agents already contain a model of the world in which they operate.

摘要（中文）: 判断一个智能体是否拥有其周围世界模型的认知，是理解其能力和局限性的基础步骤。[10]中证明，在特定框架下，每个近乎最优且通用的智能体必然包含对其环境的足够知识，从而允许通过将该智能体作为黑盒进行查询来近似地重构该环境。该结果依赖于智能体是确定性的以及环境是完全可观测的假设。 在这项工作中，我们移除了这两个假设，将该定理扩展到在部分可观测环境中运行的随机智能体。从根本上说，这表明随机智能体无法通过随机化来避免学习其环境。我们还通过弱化通用性的概念来加强该结果，证明能力较弱的智能体也已经包含其所运行的世界的模型。

TLDR: This paper extends the theorem that general agents contain world models to stochastic and partially observable environments, strengthening the result by weakening the notion of generality. It proves that even less powerful stochastic agents learn a model of their environment through randomization.

简要说明: 本文将通用智能体包含世界模型的定理扩展到随机和部分可观察的环境，并通过弱化通用性的概念来加强结果。它证明即使是能力较弱的随机智能体也通过随机化学习其环境模型。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Santiago Cifuentes

Internet of Agentic AI: Incentive-Compatible Distributed Teaming and Workflow

Abstract (English): Large language models (LLMs) have enabled a new class of agentic AI systems that reason, plan, and act by invoking external tools. However, most existing agentic architectures remain centralized and monolithic, limiting scalability, specialization, and interoperability. This paper proposes a framework for scalable agentic intelligence, termed the Internet of Agentic AI, in which autonomous, heterogeneous agents distributed across cloud and edge infrastructure dynamically form coalitions to execute task-driven workflows. We formalize a network-native model of agentic collaboration and introduce an incentive-compatible workflow-coalition feasibility framework that integrates capability coverage, network locality, and economic implementability. To enable scalable coordination, we formulate a minimum-effort coalition selection problem and propose a decentralized coalition formation algorithm. The proposed framework can operate as a coordination layer above the Model Context Protocol (MCP). A healthcare case study demonstrates how domain specialization, cloud-edge heterogeneity, and dynamic coalition formation enable scalable, resilient, and economically viable agentic workflows. This work lays the foundation for principled coordination and scalability in the emerging era of Internet of Agentic AI.

摘要（中文）: 大型语言模型（LLMs）催生了新型的智能体式人工智能系统，它们通过调用外部工具进行推理、规划和行动。然而，目前大多数的智能体式架构依然是中心化和单片的，限制了可扩展性、专业化和互操作性。本文提出了一个可扩展的智能体式人工智能框架，称为智能体式AI互联网，其中分布在云和边缘基础设施上的自治、异构智能体动态地形成联盟，以执行任务驱动的工作流程。我们形式化了网络原生的智能体协作模型，并引入了一个激励兼容的工作流程-联盟可行性框架，该框架集成了能力覆盖、网络局部性和经济可实施性。 为了实现可扩展的协调，我们制定了一个最小努力联盟选择问题，并提出了一种去中心化的联盟形成算法。所提出的框架可以作为模型上下文协议（MCP）之上的协调层运行。一个医疗保健案例研究表明，领域专业化、云边异构性以及动态联盟形成如何实现可扩展、弹性且经济可行的智能体式工作流程。这项工作为新兴的智能体式AI互联网时代中原则性的协调和可扩展性奠定了基础。

TLDR: This paper proposes a decentralized framework for agentic AI systems, termed the Internet of Agentic AI, enabling scalable and incentive-compatible collaboration among heterogeneous agents to execute task-driven workflows across cloud and edge infrastructures.

简要说明: 该论文提出了一个分散的 Agentic AI 系统框架，名为 Agentic AI 互联网，旨在实现异构 Agent 之间可扩展且符合激励机制的协作，以在云和边缘基础设施上执行任务驱动的工作流程。

Relevance: (7/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Ya-Ting Yang, Quanyan Zhu

Self-Hinting Language Models Enhance Reinforcement Learning

Abstract (English): Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) has recently emerged as a practical recipe for aligning large language models with verifiable objectives. However, under sparse terminal rewards, GRPO often stalls because rollouts within a group frequently receive identical rewards, causing relative advantages to collapse and updates to vanish. We propose self-hint aligned GRPO with privileged supervision (SAGE), an on-policy reinforcement learning framework that injects privileged hints during training to reshape the rollout distribution under the same terminal verifier reward. For each prompt $x$, the model samples a compact hint $h$ (e.g., a plan or decomposition) and then generates a solution $τ$ conditioned on $(x,h)$. Crucially, the task reward $R(x,τ)$ is unchanged; hints only increase within-group outcome diversity under finite sampling, preventing GRPO advantages from collapsing under sparse rewards. At test time, we set $h=\varnothing$ and deploy the no-hint policy without any privileged information. Moreover, sampling diverse self-hints serves as an adaptive curriculum that tracks the learner's bottlenecks more effectively than fixed hints from an initial policy or a stronger external model. Experiments over 6 benchmarks with 3 LLMs show that SAGE consistently outperforms GRPO, on average +2.0 on Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct, +1.2 on Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct and +1.3 on Qwen3-4B-Instruct. The code is available at https://github.com/BaohaoLiao/SAGE.

摘要（中文）: 群体相对策略优化（GRPO）最近崭露头角，成为一种将大型语言模型与可验证目标对齐的实用方法。然而，在稀疏终端奖励下，GRPO经常停滞不前，因为一个群体内的展开轨迹通常会收到相同的奖励，导致相对优势崩塌，更新消失。我们提出了带有特权监督的自提示对齐群体相对策略优化（SAGE），一个在线强化学习框架，在训练期间注入特权提示，以在相同的终端验证器奖励下重塑展开轨迹分布。对于每个提示词 $x$，模型采样一个紧凑的提示 $h$（例如，计划或分解），然后生成一个以 $(x,h)$ 为条件的解决方案 $τ$。关键的是，任务奖励 $R(x,τ)$ 保持不变；提示仅在有限采样下增加群体内的结果多样性，从而防止 GRPO 优势在稀疏奖励下崩塌。在测试阶段，我们设置 $h=\varnothing$，并部署无提示策略，无需任何特权信息。此外，采样多样化的自提示充当自适应课程，比来自初始策略或更强大的外部模型的固定提示更有效地追踪学习者的瓶颈。在 6 个基准测试和 3 个大型语言模型上的实验表明，SAGE 始终优于 GRPO，平均在 Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct 上提升 +2.0，在 Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct 上提升 +1.2，在 Qwen3-4B-Instruct 上提升 +1.3。 代码可在 https://github.com/BaohaoLiao/SAGE 获取。

TLDR: The paper introduces SAGE, a reinforcement learning framework that uses self-generated hints during training to improve the performance of GRPO in sparse reward environments, particularly for aligning large language models.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了SAGE，一个强化学习框架，它在训练期间使用自我生成的提示来提高GRPO在稀疏奖励环境中的性能，特别是在对齐大型语言模型方面。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Baohao Liao, Hanze Dong, Xinxing Xu, Christof Monz, Jiang Bian

Risky-Bench: Probing Agentic Safety Risks under Real-World Deployment

Abstract (English): Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as agents that operate in real-world environments, introducing safety risks beyond linguistic harm. Existing agent safety evaluations rely on risk-oriented tasks tailored to specific agent settings, resulting in limited coverage of safety risk space and failing to assess agent safety behavior during long-horizon, interactive task execution in complex real-world deployments. Moreover, their specialization to particular agent settings limits adaptability across diverse agent configurations. To address these limitations, we propose Risky-Bench, a framework that enables systematic agent safety evaluation grounded in real-world deployment. Risky-Bench organizes evaluation around domain-agnostic safety principles to derive context-aware safety rubrics that delineate safety space, and systematically evaluates safety risks across this space through realistic task execution under varying threat assumptions. When applied to life-assist agent settings, Risky-Bench uncovers substantial safety risks in state-of-the-art agents under realistic execution conditions. Moreover, as a well-structured evaluation pipeline, Risky-Bench is not confined to life-assist scenarios and can be adapted to other deployment settings to construct environment-specific safety evaluations, providing an extensible methodology for agent safety assessment.

摘要（中文）: 大型语言模型（LLMs）越来越多地被部署为在真实世界环境中运行的智能体，这引入了超出语言危害的安全风险。现有的智能体安全评估依赖于针对特定智能体设置量身定制的风险导向任务，导致对安全风险空间的覆盖范围有限，并且未能评估智能体在复杂真实世界部署中进行的长程、交互式任务执行期间的安全行为。此外，它们对特定智能体设置的专门化限制了在不同智能体配置中的适应性。为了解决这些局限性，我们提出了 Risky-Bench，一个支持基于真实世界部署进行系统性智能体安全评估的框架。Risky-Bench 围绕领域无关安全原则组织评估，以推导出情境感知的安全准则，这些准则划定安全空间，并通过在不同威胁假设下执行现实任务来系统地评估该空间中的安全风险。当应用于生命辅助智能体设置时，Risky-Bench 在现实执行条件下揭示了最先进智能体中存在的重大安全风险。此外，作为一个结构良好的评估流程，Risky-Bench 不仅限于生命辅助场景，还可以适应于其他部署设置，以构建特定于环境的安全评估，从而为智能体安全评估提供了一种可扩展的方法。

TLDR: The paper introduces Risky-Bench, a novel framework for systematically evaluating the safety of LLM agents in real-world deployments across various domains, revealing substantial safety risks in state-of-the-art agents.

简要说明: 本文介绍了Risky-Bench，一个用于系统评估LLM智能体在真实世界部署中的安全性的新框架，该框架适用于多个领域，并揭示了当前最佳智能体中存在的重大安全风险。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (9/10)

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Jingnan Zheng, Yanzhen Luo, Jingjun Xu, Bingnan Liu, Yuxin Chen, Chenhang Cui, Gelei Deng, Chaochao Lu, Xiang Wang, An Zhang, Tat-Seng Chua

JRDB-Pose3D: A Multi-person 3D Human Pose and Shape Estimation Dataset for Robotics

Abstract (English): Real-world scenes are inherently crowded. Hence, estimating 3D poses of all nearby humans, tracking their movements over time, and understanding their activities within social and environmental contexts are essential for many applications, such as autonomous driving, robot perception, robot navigation, and human-robot interaction. However, most existing 3D human pose estimation datasets primarily focus on single-person scenes or are collected in controlled laboratory environments, which restricts their relevance to real-world applications. To bridge this gap, we introduce JRDB-Pose3D, which captures multi-human indoor and outdoor environments from a mobile robotic platform. JRDB-Pose3D provides rich 3D human pose annotations for such complex and dynamic scenes, including SMPL-based pose annotations with consistent body-shape parameters and track IDs for each individual over time. JRDB-Pose3D contains, on average, 5-10 human poses per frame, with some scenes featuring up to 35 individuals simultaneously. The proposed dataset presents unique challenges, including frequent occlusions, truncated bodies, and out-of-frame body parts, which closely reflect real-world environments. Moreover, JRDB-Pose3D inherits all available annotations from the JRDB dataset, such as 2D pose, information about social grouping, activities, and interactions, full-scene semantic masks with consistent human- and object-level tracking, and detailed annotations for each individual, such as age, gender, and race, making it a holistic dataset for a wide range of downstream perception and human-centric understanding tasks.

摘要（中文）: 现实世界场景本质上是拥挤的。因此，估计所有附近人类的3D姿态、随时间追踪他们的运动、以及理解他们在社会和环境背景下的活动，对于许多应用至关重要，例如自动驾驶、机器人感知、机器人导航和人机交互。然而，大多数现有的3D人体姿态估计数据集主要侧重于单人场景或在受控的实验室环境中收集数据，这限制了它们在现实世界应用中的相关性。为了弥合这一差距，我们引入了JRDB-Pose3D，它从移动机器人平台捕获了多人室内和室外环境。JRDB-Pose3D为这种复杂和动态的场景提供了丰富的3D人体姿态标注，包括基于SMPL的姿态标注，其中包含一致的身体形状参数，以及每个人随时间的轨迹ID。JRDB-Pose3D平均每帧包含5-10个人体姿态，在某些场景中同时出现多达35个人。所提出的数据集提出了独特的挑战，包括频繁的遮挡、截断的身体和超出画面范围的身体部位，这些都密切反映了现实世界环境。此外，JRDB-Pose3D继承了来自JRDB数据集的所有可用标注，例如2D姿态、有关社会群体、活动和互动的信息、具有一致的人和对象级别跟踪的完整场景语义掩码，以及每个个体的详细标注，例如年龄、性别和种族，使其成为一个用于各种下游感知和以人为中心的理解任务的整体数据集。

TLDR: JRDB-Pose3D is a new multi-person 3D human pose dataset captured from a robotic platform in complex indoor/outdoor environments, designed to address the limitations of existing datasets for robotics and human-robot interaction.

简要说明: JRDB-Pose3D是一个新的多人3D人体姿态数据集，它是在复杂的室内/室外环境中从机器人平台上捕获的，旨在解决现有数据集在机器人和人机交互方面的局限性。

Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Sandika Biswas, Kian Izadpanah, Hamid Rezatofighi

CoBA-RL: Capability-Oriented Budget Allocation for Reinforcement Learning in LLMs

Abstract (English): Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has emerged as a key approach for enhancing LLM reasoning. However, standard frameworks like Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) typically employ a uniform rollout budget, leading to resource inefficiency. Moreover, existing adaptive methods often rely on instance-level metrics, such as task pass rates, failing to capture the model's dynamic learning state. To address these limitations, we propose CoBA-RL, a reinforcement learning algorithm designed to adaptively allocate rollout budgets based on the model's evolving capability. Specifically, CoBA-RL utilizes a Capability-Oriented Value function to map tasks to their potential training gains and employs a heap-based greedy strategy to efficiently self-calibrate the distribution of computational resources to samples with high training value. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach effectively orchestrates the trade-off between exploration and exploitation, delivering consistent generalization improvements across multiple challenging benchmarks. These findings underscore that quantifying sample training value and optimizing budget allocation are pivotal for advancing LLM post-training efficiency.

摘要（中文）: 具有可验证奖励的强化学习 (RLVR) 已经成为增强LLM推理的关键方法。然而，诸如组相对策略优化 (GRPO) 等标准框架通常采用统一的 rollout 预算，导致资源效率低下。此外，现有的自适应方法通常依赖于实例级别的指标，如任务通过率，未能捕捉模型动态的学习状态。为了解决这些限制，我们提出了 CoBA-RL，一种强化学习算法，旨在根据模型不断演进的能力自适应地分配 rollout 预算。具体而言，CoBA-RL 利用面向能力的价值函数来将任务映射到其潜在的训练收益，并采用基于堆的贪婪策略来有效自校准计算资源在具有高训练价值的样本上的分布。大量实验表明，我们的方法有效地协调了探索和利用之间的权衡，在多个具有挑战性的基准测试中实现了持续的泛化改进。这些发现强调，量化样本训练价值和优化预算分配对于提升 LLM 后训练效率至关重要。

TLDR: The paper introduces CoBA-RL, a reinforcement learning algorithm that adaptively allocates the rollout budget in RL for LLMs based on the model's evolving capability, leading to improved training efficiency and generalization.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了CoBA-RL，一种强化学习算法，可以基于模型不断发展的能力，自适应地为LLM中的RL分配 rollout 预算，从而提高训练效率和泛化能力。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (8/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Zhiyuan Yao, Yi-Kai Zhang, Yuxin Chen, Yueqing Sun, Zishan Xu, Yu Yang, Tianhao Hu, Qi Gu, Hui Su, Xunliang Cai

Bongards at the Boundary of Perception and Reasoning: Programs or Language?

Abstract (English): Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have made great strides in everyday visual tasks, such as captioning a natural image, or answering commonsense questions about such images. But humans possess the puzzling ability to deploy their visual reasoning abilities in radically new situations, a skill rigorously tested by the classic set of visual reasoning challenges known as the Bongard problems. We present a neurosymbolic approach to solving these problems: given a hypothesized solution rule for a Bongard problem, we leverage LLMs to generate parameterized programmatic representations for the rule and perform parameter fitting using Bayesian optimization. We evaluate our method on classifying Bongard problem images given the ground truth rule, as well as on solving the problems from scratch.

摘要（中文）: 视觉语言模型(VLMs)在日常视觉任务中取得了显著进展，例如为自然图像生成标题或回答关于这些图像的常识性问题。但人类拥有一种令人费解的能力，能够在全新的情境中运用他们的视觉推理能力，这种能力受到了经典视觉推理挑战——邦加德问题——的严格测试。我们提出了一种神经符号方法来解决这些问题：给定一个邦加德问题的假设解规则，我们利用LLM来生成规则的参数化程序化表示，并使用贝叶斯优化执行参数拟合。我们在给定真实规则的情况下对邦加德问题图像进行分类，以及从头开始解决问题方面评估了我们的方法。

TLDR: This paper introduces a neurosymbolic method leveraging LLMs to solve Bongard problems, a classic visual reasoning challenge, by generating programmatic representations of hypothesized solution rules and using Bayesian optimization for parameter fitting.

简要说明: 本文介绍了一种神经符号方法，利用大型语言模型解决 Bongard 问题（一种经典的视觉推理挑战），通过生成假设解规则的程序化表示，并使用贝叶斯优化进行参数拟合。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Cassidy Langenfeld, Claas Beger, Gloria Geng, Wasu Top Piriyakulkij, Keya Hu, Yewen Pu, Kevin Ellis

STAR: Similarity-guided Teacher-Assisted Refinement for Super-Tiny Function Calling Models

Abstract (English): The proliferation of Large Language Models (LLMs) in function calling is pivotal for creating advanced AI agents, yet their large scale hinders widespread adoption, necessitating transferring their capabilities into smaller ones. However, existing paradigms are often plagued by overfitting, training instability, ineffective binary rewards for multi-solution tasks, and the difficulty of synergizing techniques. We introduce STAR: Similarity-guided Teacher-Assisted Refinement, a novel holistic framework that effectively transfers LLMs' capabilities to super-tiny models. STAR consists of two core technical innovations: (1) Constrained Knowledge Distillation (CKD), a training objective that augments top-k forward KL divergence to suppress confidently incorrect predictions, ensuring training stability while preserving exploration capacity for downstream RL. STAR holistically synergizes these strategies within a cohesive training curriculum, enabling super-tiny models to achieve exceptional performance on complex function calling tasks; (2) Similarity-guided RL (Sim-RL), a RL mechanism that introduces a fine-grained, similarity-based reward. This provides a robust, continuous, and rich signal for better policy optimization by evaluating the similarity between generated outputs and the ground truth. Extensive experiments on challenging and renowned benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of our method. Our STAR models establish SOTA in their size classes, significantly outperforming baselines. Remarkably, our 0.6B STAR model achieves the best performance among all open models under 1B, surpassing even several well-known open models at a larger scale. STAR demonstrates a training framework that distills capabilities of LLMs into super-tiny models, paving the way for powerful, accessible, and efficient AI agents.

摘要（中文）: 函数调用中大型语言模型（LLMs）的激增对于创建高级AI智能体至关重要，但其庞大的规模阻碍了广泛应用，因此需要将其能力转移到较小的模型。然而，现有的范例通常受到过拟合、训练不稳定、多解任务的无效二元奖励以及协同技术的困难等问题的困扰。我们引入了STAR：相似性引导的教师辅助精炼，这是一种新颖的整体框架，可以有效地将LLMs的能力转移到超小型模型。STAR由两个核心技术创新组成：（1）约束知识蒸馏（CKD），这是一种训练目标，它增强了top-k前向KL散度，以抑制置信度高的错误预测，从而在保留下游强化学习探索能力的同时确保训练稳定性。STAR在一个有凝聚力的训练课程中全面协同这些策略，使超小型模型能够在复杂的函数调用任务上实现卓越的性能；（2）相似性引导强化学习（Sim-RL），这是一种强化学习机制，引入了细粒度的、基于相似性的奖励。这通过评估生成输出与真实值之间的相似性，为更好的策略优化提供了稳健、连续且丰富的信号。在具有挑战性和著名的基准测试上进行的大量实验证明了我们方法的有效性。我们的STAR模型在其规模级别中建立了SOTA，显著优于基线模型。值得注意的是，我们的0.6B STAR模型在所有小于1B的开放模型中实现了最佳性能，甚至超过了一些更大规模的知名开放模型。STAR展示了一种训练框架，可以将LLMs的能力提炼到超小型模型中，为强大、可访问且高效的AI智能体铺平了道路。

TLDR: The paper introduces STAR, a framework for distilling LLM function calling capabilities into super-tiny models using constrained knowledge distillation and similarity-guided reinforcement learning, achieving state-of-the-art performance for models under 1B parameters.

简要说明: 本文介绍STAR，一个将LLM函数调用能力提炼到超小型模型中的框架，它采用约束知识蒸馏和相似性引导的强化学习，在小于10亿参数的模型中实现了最先进的性能。

Relevance: (7/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Jiliang Ni, Jiachen Pu, Zhongyi Yang, Jingfeng Luo, Conggang Hu

Agent Alpha: Tree Search Unifying Generation, Exploration and Evaluation for Computer-Use Agents

Abstract (English): While scaling test-time compute through trajectory-level sampling has significantly improved Graphical User Interface (GUI) agents, the lack of regressive ability prevents the reuse of partial successes and the recovery from early missteps. In this paper, we introduce Agent Alpha, a unified framework that synergizes generation, exploration, and evaluation through step-level Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS). It enables active modeling or exploiting structures of the planning space. By integrating alpha-UCT guided search into the interaction loop, Agent Alpha enables deliberate planning, facilitating early pruning of suboptimal branches and efficient prefix reuse. We also employ comparison-driven evaluation to mitigate absolute scoring biases and diversity-constrained expansion to maintain a compact, informative search space. Regret bound of alpha-UCT is analyzed. On the OSWorld benchmark, Agent Alpha achieves a state-of-the-art success rate of $\sim 77\%$, significantly outperforming trajectory-level baselines under equivalent compute.

摘要（中文）: 虽然通过轨迹级采样扩展测试时算力显著提升了图形用户界面（GUI）智能体，但缺乏回归能力阻碍了部分成功的复用和早期失误的恢复。在本文中，我们提出了Agent Alpha，一个统一的框架，通过步级蒙特卡洛树搜索（MCTS）协同生成、探索和评估，从而能够主动建模或利用规划空间的结构。通过将 alpha-UCT 指导的搜索集成到交互循环中，Agent Alpha 实现了深思熟虑的规划，便于尽早剪枝次优分支和高效地复用前缀。我们还采用比较驱动的评估来减轻绝对评分偏差，并采用多样性约束的扩展来维持一个紧凑且信息丰富的搜索空间。我们分析了 alpha-UCT 的遗憾界。在OSWorld基准测试中，Agent Alpha 达到了约 77% 的最先进成功率，在等效算力下显著优于轨迹级基线。

TLDR: Agent Alpha uses step-level Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) to improve GUI agents by enabling better planning, pruning, and prefix reuse, achieving state-of-the-art results on the OSWorld benchmark.

简要说明: Agent Alpha采用步进式蒙特卡洛树搜索（MCTS）来改进GUI代理，通过实现更好的规划、剪枝和前缀重用，在OSWorld基准测试中取得了最先进的成果。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Sizhe Tang, Rongqian Chen, Tian Lan

Structuring Value Representations via Geometric Coherence in Markov Decision Processes

Abstract (English): Geometric properties can be leveraged to stabilize and speed reinforcement learning. Existing examples include encoding symmetry structure, geometry-aware data augmentation, and enforcing structural restrictions. In this paper, we take a novel view of RL through the lens of order theory and recast value function estimates into learning a desired poset (partially ordered set). We propose \emph{GCR-RL} (Geometric Coherence Regularized Reinforcement Learning) that computes a sequence of super-poset refinements -- by refining posets in previous steps and learning additional order relationships from temporal difference signals -- thus ensuring geometric coherence across the sequence of posets underpinning the learned value functions. Two novel algorithms by Q-learning and by actor--critic are developed to efficiently realize these super-poset refinements. Their theoretical properties and convergence rates are analyzed. We empirically evaluate GCR-RL in a range of tasks and demonstrate significant improvements in sample efficiency and stable performance over strong baselines.

摘要（中文）: 几何性质可以用于稳定并加速强化学习。现有的例子包括编码对称结构、几何感知数据增强以及强制结构约束。在本文中，我们从序理论的角度对强化学习进行重新审视，并将价值函数估计重塑为学习一个期望的偏序集（poset）。我们提出了\emph{GCR-RL}（几何相干正则化强化学习），它通过细化先前步骤中的偏序集并从时序差分信号中学习额外的序关系，计算一系列超偏序集精化，从而确保支撑学习价值函数的偏序集序列具有几何相干性。我们开发了基于Q学习和基于Actor-Critic的两种新型算法来高效地实现这些超偏序集精化。分析了它们的理论性质和收敛速度。我们在各种任务中对GCR-RL进行了实验评估，并展示了相对于强大基线在样本效率和稳定性能方面的显著改进。

TLDR: The paper introduces GCR-RL, a novel reinforcement learning approach that leverages order theory and geometric coherence to improve sample efficiency and stability by learning a sequence of super-poset refinements for value function estimation.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了 GCR-RL，一种新颖的强化学习方法，它利用序理论和几何一致性，通过学习一系列用于价值函数估计的超偏序集细化来提高样本效率和稳定性。

Relevance: (7/10)
Novelty: (9/10)
Clarity: (8/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Zuyuan Zhang, Zeyu Fang, Tian Lan

Where Norms and References Collide: Evaluating LLMs on Normative Reasoning

Abstract (English): Embodied agents, such as robots, will need to interact in situated environments where successful communication often depends on reasoning over social norms: shared expectations that constrain what actions are appropriate in context. A key capability in such settings is norm-based reference resolution (NBRR), where interpreting referential expressions requires inferring implicit normative expectations grounded in physical and social context. Yet it remains unclear whether Large Language Models (LLMs) can support this kind of reasoning. In this work, we introduce SNIC (Situated Norms in Context), a human-validated diagnostic testbed designed to probe how well state-of-the-art LLMs can extract and utilize normative principles relevant to NBRR. SNIC emphasizes physically grounded norms that arise in everyday tasks such as cleaning, tidying, and serving. Across a range of controlled evaluations, we find that even the strongest LLMs struggle to consistently identify and apply social norms, particularly when norms are implicit, underspecified, or in conflict. These findings reveal a blind spot in current LLMs and highlight a key challenge for deploying language-based systems in socially situated, embodied settings.

摘要（中文）: 具体化身代理，如机器人，需要在情境化的环境中进行交互，其中成功的沟通通常依赖于对社会规范的推理：即约束情境中适当行为的共享期望。在这种情况下，一项关键能力是基于规范的指称消解（NBRR），其中解释指称表达式需要推断出隐含的、扎根于物理和社会背景中的规范性期望。然而，大型语言模型（LLMs）是否能够支持这种推理仍然不清楚。在这项工作中，我们引入了SNIC（情境中的具身规范），这是一个经过人工验证的诊断测试平台，旨在探究最先进的LLM能够多好地提取和利用与NBRR相关的规范原则。SNIC强调在日常任务（如清洁、整理和服务）中产生的物理层面上的规范。通过一系列受控评估，我们发现即使是最强大的LLM也难以始终如一地识别和应用社会规范，尤其是在规范是隐式的、欠明确的或冲突的情况下。这些发现揭示了当前LLM的一个盲点，并突出了在社会情境化、具体化身环境中部署基于语言的系统所面临的一个关键挑战。

TLDR: This paper introduces SNIC, a benchmark for evaluating LLMs' ability to reason about social norms in situated environments for norm-based reference resolution, finding that current LLMs struggle with this task, particularly when norms are implicit or conflicting.

简要说明: 本文介绍了SNIC，一个用于评估大型语言模型在情境环境中基于规范的参照解析中推理社会规范能力的基准。研究发现，当前的大型语言模型难以胜任这项任务，尤其是在规范是隐含的或冲突的情况下。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Mitchell Abrams, Kaveh Eskandari Miandoab, Felix Gervits, Vasanth Sarathy, Matthias Scheutz

FIRE-Bench: Evaluating Agents on the Rediscovery of Scientific Insights

Abstract (English): Autonomous agents powered by large language models (LLMs) promise to accelerate scientific discovery end-to-end, but rigorously evaluating their capacity for verifiable discovery remains a central challenge. Existing benchmarks face a trade-off: they either heavily rely on LLM-as-judge evaluations of automatically generated research outputs or optimize convenient yet isolated performance metrics that provide coarse proxies for scientific insight. To address this gap, we introduce FIRE-Bench (Full-cycle Insight Rediscovery Evaluation), a benchmark that evaluates agents through the rediscovery of established findings from recent, high-impact machine learning research. Agents are given only a high-level research question extracted from a published, verified study and must autonomously explore ideas, design experiments, implement code, execute their plans, and derive conclusions supported by empirical evidence. We evaluate a range of state-of-the-art agents with frontier LLMs backbones like gpt-5 on FIRE-Bench. Our results show that full-cycle scientific research remains challenging for current agent systems: even the strongest agents achieve limited rediscovery success (<50 F1), exhibit high variance across runs, and display recurring failure modes in experimental design, execution, and evidence-based reasoning. FIRE-Bench provides a rigorous and diagnostic framework for measuring progress toward reliable agent-driven scientific discovery.

摘要（中文）: 由大型语言模型(LLMs)驱动的自主智能体有望加速端到端的科学发现，但严格评估其可验证发现能力仍然是一项核心挑战。现有基准面临着一种权衡：它们要么严重依赖于LLM作为裁判对自动生成的研究成果进行评估，要么优化便捷但孤立的性能指标，这些指标为科学洞察力提供了粗略的代理。为了解决这一差距，我们引入了FIRE-Bench（全周期洞察重新发现评估）基准，它通过重新发现近期、高影响力机器学习研究中的已确立发现来评估智能体。智能体仅被提供从已发表、经过验证的研究中提取的高级研究问题，并且必须自主地探索想法，设计实验，实现代码，执行他们的计划，并得出由经验证据支持的结论。我们使用诸如gpt-5等前沿LLM主干评估了一系列最先进的智能体在FIRE-Bench上的表现。我们的结果表明，全周期的科学研究对当前的智能体系统仍然具有挑战性：即使是最强大的智能体也只能实现有限的重新发现成功（<50 F1），在运行过程中表现出高方差，并且在实验设计、执行和基于证据的推理中表现出反复出现的失效模式。FIRE-Bench提供了一个严谨且具有诊断意义的框架，用于衡量在可靠的智能体驱动的科学发现方面取得的进展。

TLDR: The paper introduces FIRE-Bench, a new benchmark for evaluating AI agents on their ability to rediscover scientific insights, revealing that current agents struggle with full-cycle scientific research.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了FIRE-Bench，一个新的基准测试，用于评估AI智能体在重新发现科学见解方面的能力。结果表明，目前的智能体在全周期科学研究方面表现不佳。

Authors: Zhen Wang, Fan Bai, Zhongyan Luo, Jinyan Su, Kaiser Sun, Xinle Yu, Jieyuan Liu, Kun Zhou, Claire Cardie, Mark Dredze, Eric P. Xing, Zhiting Hu

Manifold-Constrained Energy-Based Transition Models for Offline Reinforcement Learning

Abstract (English): Model-based offline reinforcement learning is brittle under distribution shift: policy improvement drives rollouts into state--action regions weakly supported by the dataset, where compounding model error yields severe value overestimation. We propose Manifold-Constrained Energy-based Transition Models (MC-ETM), which train conditional energy-based transition models using a manifold projection--diffusion negative sampler. MC-ETM learns a latent manifold of next states and generates near-manifold hard negatives by perturbing latent codes and running Langevin dynamics in latent space with the learned conditional energy, sharpening the energy landscape around the dataset support and improving sensitivity to subtle out-of-distribution deviations. For policy optimization, the learned energy provides a single reliability signal: rollouts are truncated when the minimum energy over sampled next states exceeds a threshold, and Bellman backups are stabilized via pessimistic penalties based on Q-value-level dispersion across energy-guided samples. We formalize MC-ETM through a hybrid pessimistic MDP formulation and derive a conservative performance bound separating in-support evaluation error from truncation risk. Empirically, MC-ETM improves multi-step dynamics fidelity and yields higher normalized returns on standard offline control benchmarks, particularly under irregular dynamics and sparse data coverage.

摘要（中文）: 基于模型的离线强化学习在分布偏移下表现脆弱：策略改进驱动rollout进入数据集弱支持的状态-动作区域，导致模型误差的累积效应产生严重的价值高估。我们提出流形约束的基于能量的转移模型 (MC-ETM)，该模型采用流形投影-扩散负采样器训练条件基于能量的转移模型。MC-ETM学习下一个状态的潜在流形，并通过扰动潜在编码并在潜在空间中利用学习到的条件能量运行朗之万动力学来生成近流形的硬负样本，从而锐化数据集支持周围的能量场，并提高对细微的分布外偏差的敏感性。对于策略优化，学习到的能量提供了一个单一的可靠性信号：当采样得到的下一个状态的最小能量超过阈值时，rollout将被截断，并通过基于能量引导的样本的Q值水平离差的悲观惩罚来稳定贝尔曼回溯。我们通过一种混合悲观MDP公式来形式化MC-ETM，并推导出将支持内评估误差与截断风险分离的保守性能边界。在经验上，MC-ETM提高了多步动态保真度，并在标准离线控制基准测试中产生了更高的规范化回报，尤其是在不规则动态和稀疏数据覆盖的情况下。

TLDR: The paper introduces Manifold-Constrained Energy-based Transition Models (MC-ETM) for offline reinforcement learning that addresses distribution shift by learning a latent manifold of next states and using energy-guided sampling for pessimistic policy optimization, leading to improved performance on offline control benchmarks.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了用于离线强化学习的流形约束能量转移模型（MC-ETM），通过学习下一个状态的潜在流形并使用能量引导的采样进行悲观策略优化，解决了分布偏移问题，从而提高了离线控制基准的性能。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Zeyu Fang, Zuyuan Zhang, Mahdi Imani, Tian Lan

Joint Learning of Hierarchical Neural Options and Abstract World Model

Abstract (English): Building agents that can perform new skills by composing existing skills is a long-standing goal of AI agent research. Towards this end, we investigate how to efficiently acquire a sequence of skills, formalized as hierarchical neural options. However, existing model-free hierarchical reinforcement algorithms need a lot of data. We propose a novel method, which we call AgentOWL (Option and World model Learning Agent), that jointly learns -- in a sample efficient way -- an abstract world model (abstracting across both states and time) and a set of hierarchical neural options. We show, on a subset of Object-Centric Atari games, that our method can learn more skills using much less data than baseline methods.

摘要（中文）: 构建能够通过组合现有技能来执行新技能的智能体是人工智能智能体研究的一个长期目标。为了实现这个目标，我们研究如何高效地获取一系列技能，这些技能形式化为分层神经选项。 然而，现有的无模型分层强化学习算法需要大量数据。我们提出了一种新颖的方法，我们称之为 AgentOWL（选项与世界模型学习智能体），该方法以样本高效的方式联合学习一个抽象世界模型（在状态和时间上进行抽象）以及一组分层神经选项。 我们在对象中心化的Atari游戏的一个子集上展示了我们的方法能够使用比基线方法少得多的数据来学习更多的技能。

TLDR: The paper introduces AgentOWL, a method for sample-efficiently learning hierarchical neural options and abstract world models, demonstrating improved skill acquisition on Object-Centric Atari games.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了AgentOWL，一种用于高效学习分层神经options和抽象世界模型的方法，并在以对象为中心的Atari游戏中展示了改进的技能获取。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Wasu Top Piriyakulkij, Wolfgang Lehrach, Kevin Ellis, Kevin Murphy

Dynamic Mix Precision Routing for Efficient Multi-step LLM Interaction

Abstract (English): Large language models (LLM) achieve strong performance in long-horizon decision-making tasks through multi-step interaction and reasoning at test time. While practitioners commonly believe a higher task success rate necessitates the use of a larger and stronger LLM model, multi-step interaction with a large LLM incurs prohibitive inference cost. To address this problem, we explore the use of low-precision quantized LLM in the long-horizon decision-making process. Based on the observation of diverse sensitivities among interaction steps, we propose a dynamic mix-precision routing framework that adaptively selects between high-precision and low-precision LLMs at each decision step. The router is trained via a two-stage pipeline, consisting of KL-divergence-based supervised learning that identifies precision-sensitive steps, followed by Group-Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to further improve task success rates. Experiments on ALFWorld demonstrate that our approach achieves a great improvement on accuracy-cost trade-off over single-precision baselines and heuristic routing methods.

摘要（中文）: 大型语言模型（LLM）通过测试时多步交互和推理，在长程决策任务中实现了强大的性能。从业者通常认为更高的任务成功率需要使用更大、更强的 LLM 模型，但是与大型 LLM 的多步交互会产生过高的推理成本。为了解决这个问题，我们探索在长程决策过程中使用低精度量化的 LLM。基于对交互步骤间不同敏感性的观察，我们提出了一种动态混合精度路由框架，该框架在每个决策步骤自适应地选择高精度和低精度 LLM。该路由器通过两阶段流程进行训练，首先是基于 KL 散度的监督学习，用于识别精度敏感步骤，然后是群体相对策略优化 (GRPO)，以进一步提高任务成功率。ALFWorld 上的实验表明，与单精度基线和启发式路由方法相比，我们的方法在准确率-成本权衡方面取得了显著提升。

TLDR: The paper proposes a dynamic mix-precision routing framework for multi-step LLM interaction in long-horizon decision making, which adaptively selects between high-precision and low-precision LLMs at each step to improve accuracy-cost trade-off.

简要说明: 该论文提出了一种用于长时域决策中多步LLM交互的动态混合精度路由框架，该框架在每个步骤中自适应地选择高精度和低精度LLM，以提高准确性-成本的权衡。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Yuanzhe Li, Jianing Deng, Jingtong Hu, Tianlong Chen, Song Wang, Huanrui Yang

MARS: Modular Agent with Reflective Search for Automated AI Research

Abstract (English): Automating AI research differs from general software engineering due to computationally expensive evaluation (e.g., model training) and opaque performance attribution. Current LLM-based agents struggle here, often generating monolithic scripts that ignore execution costs and causal factors. We introduce MARS (Modular Agent with Reflective Search), a framework optimized for autonomous AI research. MARS relies on three pillars: (1) Budget-Aware Planning via cost-constrained Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) to explicitly balance performance with execution expense; (2) Modular Construction, employing a "Design-Decompose-Implement" pipeline to manage complex research repositories; and (3) Comparative Reflective Memory, which addresses credit assignment by analyzing solution differences to distill high-signal insights. MARS achieves state-of-the-art performance among open-source frameworks on MLE-Bench under comparable settings, maintaining competitiveness with the global leaderboard's top methods. Furthermore, the system exhibits qualitative "Aha!" moments, where 63% of all utilized lessons originate from cross-branch transfer, demonstrating that the agent effectively generalizes insights across search paths.

摘要（中文）: 自动化人工智能研究与通用软件工程不同，其原因在于计算开销巨大的评估（例如，模型训练）和不透明的性能归因。当前基于大语言模型的智能体在此方面表现不佳，通常生成忽略执行成本和因果关系的单体脚本。我们引入了 MARS（具有反思性搜索的模块化智能体），这是一个专为自主人工智能研究优化的框架。MARS 依赖于三大支柱：（1）预算感知规划，通过成本约束的蒙特卡洛树搜索（MCTS）来显式平衡性能和执行开销；（2）模块化构建，采用“设计-分解-实现”流水线来管理复杂的科研知识库；（3）比较性反思记忆，通过分析解决方案差异来提炼高价值的见解，从而解决信用分配问题。在可比的环境下，MARS 在 MLE-Bench 上实现了开源框架中最先进的性能，并与全球排行榜上的顶尖方法保持了竞争力。此外，该系统展现出定性“Aha！”时刻，其中 63% 的所有利用的经验教训都来自跨分支转移，表明该智能体能够有效地概括搜索路径中的见解。

TLDR: The paper introduces MARS, a modular agent for automating AI research using budget-aware planning, modular construction, and comparative reflective memory, achieving state-of-the-art performance on MLE-Bench.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了MARS，一种用于自动化AI研究的模块化代理，它使用预算感知规划、模块化结构和比较反射记忆，并在MLE-Bench上实现了最先进的性能。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Jiefeng Chen, Bhavana Dalvi Mishra, Jaehyun Nam, Rui Meng, Tomas Pfister, Jinsung Yoon

RLAnything: Forge Environment, Policy, and Reward Model in Completely Dynamic RL System

Abstract (English): We propose RLAnything, a reinforcement learning framework that dynamically forges environment, policy, and reward models through closed-loop optimization, amplifying learning signals and strengthening the overall RL system for any LLM or agentic scenarios. Specifically, the policy is trained with integrated feedback from step-wise and outcome signals, while the reward model is jointly optimized via consistency feedback, which in turn further improves policy training. Moreover, our theory-motivated automatic environment adaptation improves training for both the reward and policy models by leveraging critic feedback from each, enabling learning from experience. Empirically, each added component consistently improves the overall system, and RLAnything yields substantial gains across various representative LLM and agentic tasks, boosting Qwen3-VL-8B-Thinking by 9.1% on OSWorld and Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct by 18.7% and 11.9% on AlfWorld and LiveBench, respectively. We also that optimized reward-model signals outperform outcomes that rely on human labels. Code: https://github.com/Gen-Verse/Open-AgentRL

摘要（中文）: 我们提出 RLAnything，一种强化学习框架，它通过闭环优化动态构建环境、策略和奖励模型，从而放大学习信号并增强整体 RL 系统，适用于任何 LLM 或自主代理场景。具体而言，策略在逐步和结果信号的综合反馈下进行训练，同时奖励模型通过一致性反馈进行联合优化，进而进一步改进策略训练。此外，我们由理论驱动的自动环境适应，利用来自每个模型的评论家反馈，改进了奖励和策略模型的训练，从而实现从经验中学习。实验结果表明，每个新增组件都能持续改进整体系统，并且 RLAnything 在各种代表性的 LLM 和自主代理任务上都取得了显著的提升，在 OSWorld 上使 Qwen3-VL-8B-Thinking 提高了 9.1%，在 AlfWorld 和 LiveBench 上分别使 Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct 提高了 18.7% 和 11.9%。我们还证明了优化的奖励模型信号优于依赖于人工标注的结果。代码：https://github.com/Gen-Verse/Open-AgentRL

TLDR: RLAnything is a reinforcement learning framework that dynamically optimizes environment, policy, and reward models in a closed-loop manner, demonstrating significant performance improvements on LLM and agentic tasks. The system leverages integrated feedback and automatic environment adaptation for improved learning.

简要说明: RLAnything是一个强化学习框架，它以闭环方式动态优化环境、策略和奖励模型，并在LLM和智能体任务上表现出显著的性能提升。该系统利用综合反馈和自动环境适应来改善学习效果。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (8/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Yinjie Wang, Tianbao Xie, Ke Shen, Mengdi Wang, Ling Yang

RE-TRAC: REcursive TRAjectory Compression for Deep Search Agents

Abstract (English): LLM-based deep research agents are largely built on the ReAct framework. This linear design makes it difficult to revisit earlier states, branch into alternative search directions, or maintain global awareness under long contexts, often leading to local optima, redundant exploration, and inefficient search. We propose Re-TRAC, an agentic framework that performs cross-trajectory exploration by generating a structured state representation after each trajectory to summarize evidence, uncertainties, failures, and future plans, and conditioning subsequent trajectories on this state representation. This enables iterative reflection and globally informed planning, reframing research as a progressive process. Empirical results show that Re-TRAC consistently outperforms ReAct by 15-20% on BrowseComp with frontier LLMs. For smaller models, we introduce Re-TRAC-aware supervised fine-tuning, achieving state-of-the-art performance at comparable scales. Notably, Re-TRAC shows a monotonic reduction in tool calls and token usage across rounds, indicating progressively targeted exploration driven by cross-trajectory reflection rather than redundant search.

摘要（中文）: 基于LLM的深度研究智能体很大程度上构建于ReAct框架之上。这种线性设计使得难以回顾先前的状态、分叉到其他的搜索方向，或者在长上下文中保持全局感知，通常导致局部最优、冗余探索和低效搜索。我们提出Re-TRAC，一个智能体框架，通过在每个轨迹后生成结构化的状态表示来执行跨轨迹探索，以总结证据、不确定性、失败情况和未来计划，并基于此状态表示来调节后续的轨迹。这实现了迭代反思和全局知情的规划，将研究重构为一个渐进的过程。实验结果表明，在使用前沿LLM时，Re-TRAC在BrowseComp上始终优于ReAct 15-20%。对于较小的模型，我们引入了感知Re-TRAC的监督式微调，在相当的规模下实现了最先进的性能。值得注意的是，Re-TRAC显示出工具调用次数和token使用量随轮次的单调递减，表明跨轨迹反思驱动的渐进式定向探索，而不是冗余搜索。

TLDR: The paper introduces Re-TRAC, a novel framework for LLM-based research agents that improves search efficiency by incorporating cross-trajectory reflection and structured state representation, outperforming ReAct in BrowseComp benchmarks.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了Re-TRAC，一种新颖的基于LLM的研究代理框架，通过结合跨轨迹反思和结构化状态表示来提高搜索效率，在BrowseComp基准测试中优于ReAct。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Jialiang Zhu, Gongrui Zhang, Xiaolong Ma, Lin Xu, Miaosen Zhang, Ruiqi Yang, Song Wang, Kai Qiu, Zhirong Wu, Qi Dai, Ruichun Ma, Bei Liu, Yifan Yang, Chong Luo, Zhengyuan Yang, Linjie Li, Lijuan Wang, Weizhu Chen, Xin Geng, Baining Guo

MemSkill: Learning and Evolving Memory Skills for Self-Evolving Agents

Abstract (English): Most Large Language Model (LLM) agent memory systems rely on a small set of static, hand-designed operations for extracting memory. These fixed procedures hard-code human priors about what to store and how to revise memory, making them rigid under diverse interaction patterns and inefficient on long histories. To this end, we present \textbf{MemSkill}, which reframes these operations as learnable and evolvable memory skills, structured and reusable routines for extracting, consolidating, and pruning information from interaction traces. Inspired by the design philosophy of agent skills, MemSkill employs a \emph{controller} that learns to select a small set of relevant skills, paired with an LLM-based \emph{executor} that produces skill-guided memories. Beyond learning skill selection, MemSkill introduces a \emph{designer} that periodically reviews hard cases where selected skills yield incorrect or incomplete memories, and evolves the skill set by proposing refinements and new skills. Together, MemSkill forms a closed-loop procedure that improves both the skill-selection policy and the skill set itself. Experiments on LoCoMo, LongMemEval, HotpotQA, and ALFWorld demonstrate that MemSkill improves task performance over strong baselines and generalizes well across settings. Further analyses shed light on how skills evolve, offering insights toward more adaptive, self-evolving memory management for LLM agents.

摘要（中文）: 大多数大型语言模型（LLM）智能体记忆系统依赖于一小组静态的、手工设计的记忆提取操作。这些固定的程序硬编码了关于存储什么以及如何修订记忆的人为先验知识，这使得它们在不同的交互模式下显得僵化，并且在长时历史记录上效率低下。为此，我们提出了 \textbf{MemSkill}，它将这些操作重构为可学习和可演进的记忆技能，这些技能是结构化的、可重复使用的例程，用于从交互轨迹中提取、整合和剪枝信息。受智能体技能设计理念的启发，MemSkill 采用一个 \emph{控制器} 来学习选择一小部分相关的技能，并与一个基于 LLM 的 \emph{执行器} 配对，该执行器产生技能引导的记忆。除了学习技能选择外，MemSkill 还引入了一个 \emph{设计器}，它定期审查所选技能产生不正确或不完整记忆的困难案例，并通过提出改进意见和新技能来演进技能集。总之，MemSkill 形成了一个闭环程序，可以改进技能选择策略和技能集本身。在 LoCoMo、LongMemEval、HotpotQA 和 ALFWorld 上的实验表明，MemSkill 提高了任务性能，优于强大的基线，并且在不同设置中具有良好的泛化能力。进一步的分析阐明了技能是如何演变的，为 LLM 智能体提供更具适应性的、自我演进的记忆管理提供了见解。

TLDR: MemSkill introduces a framework for learning and evolving memory skills in LLM agents, enabling them to adapt to diverse interaction patterns and improve long-term memory management through a closed-loop system that refines skill selection and the skill set itself.

简要说明: MemSkill 引入了一个框架，用于学习和发展 LLM 代理中的记忆技能，使其能够适应多样化的交互模式，并通过一个闭环系统来改进技能选择和技能集本身，从而提高长期记忆管理能力。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (9/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)

Authors: Haozhen Zhang, Quanyu Long, Jianzhu Bao, Tao Feng, Weizhi Zhang, Haodong Yue, Wenya Wang

Avenir-Web: Human-Experience-Imitating Multimodal Web Agents with Mixture of Grounding Experts

Abstract (English): Despite advances in multimodal large language models, autonomous web agents still struggle to reliably execute long-horizon tasks on complex and dynamic web interfaces. Existing agents often suffer from inaccurate element grounding, the absence of site-specific procedural knowledge, and unstable long-term task tracking and memory, particularly when operating over complex Document Object Model structures. To address these limitations, we introduce Avenir-Web, a web agent that achieves a new open-source state of the art on the Online-Mind2Web benchmark in real-world deployment. Avenir-Web leverages a Mixture of Grounding Experts, Experience-Imitation Planning for incorporating procedural priors, and a task-tracking checklist combined with adaptive memory to enable robust and seamless interaction across diverse user interface paradigms. We evaluate Avenir-Web on Online-Mind2Web, a rigorous benchmark of live and user-centered web tasks. Our results demonstrate that Avenir-Web significantly surpasses prior open-source agents and attains performance parity with top-tier proprietary models, thereby establishing a new open-source state of the art for reliable web agents on live websites.

摘要（中文）: 尽管多模态大型语言模型取得了进展，但自主网络智能体在复杂和动态的Web界面上可靠地执行长程任务仍然面临挑战。现有的智能体常常受到不准确的元素定位、缺乏特定站点的程序性知识以及不稳定的长期任务跟踪和记忆的影响，尤其是在处理复杂的文档对象模型结构时。为了解决这些局限性，我们推出了Avenir-Web，这是一种网络智能体，在真实部署的Online-Mind2Web基准测试中实现了新的开源最佳性能。Avenir-Web利用了混合定位专家、用于融合程序性先验的经验模仿规划，以及结合自适应记忆的任务跟踪清单，从而能够在各种用户界面范例中实现稳健和无缝的交互。我们在Online-Mind2Web（一个针对实时和以用户为中心的Web任务的严格基准测试）上评估了Avenir-Web。我们的结果表明，Avenir-Web显著超越了之前的开源智能体，并达到了与顶级专有模型相当的性能，从而为实时网站上可靠的网络智能体建立了新的开源最佳水平。

TLDR: Avenir-Web, a novel web agent, achieves state-of-the-art performance on the Online-Mind2Web benchmark by using a Mixture of Grounding Experts, Experience-Imitation Planning, and a task-tracking checklist with adaptive memory.

简要说明: Avenir-Web是一种新型网络代理，通过使用接地专家混合、经验模仿规划和带有自适应记忆的任务跟踪清单，在Online-Mind2Web基准测试中实现了最先进的性能。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
