Authors: Aiden Yiliu Li, Xinyue Hao, Shilong Liu, Mengdi Wang

Drift-Bench: Diagnosing Cooperative Breakdowns in LLM Agents under Input Faults via Multi-Turn Interaction

Abstract (English): As Large Language Models transition to autonomous agents, user inputs frequently violate cooperative assumptions (e.g., implicit intent, missing parameters, false presuppositions, or ambiguous expressions), creating execution risks that text-only evaluations do not capture. Existing benchmarks typically assume well-specified instructions or restrict evaluation to text-only, single-turn clarification, and thus do not measure multi-turn disambiguation under grounded execution risk. We introduce \textbf{Drift-Bench}, the first diagnostic benchmark that evaluates agentic pragmatics under input faults through multi-turn clarification across state-oriented and service-oriented execution environments. Grounded in classical theories of communication, \textbf{Drift-Bench} provides a unified taxonomy of cooperative breakdowns and employs a persona-driven user simulator with the \textbf{Rise} evaluation protocol. Experiments show substantial performance drops under these faults, with clarification effectiveness varying across user personas and fault types. \MethodName bridges clarification research and agent safety evaluation, enabling systematic diagnosis of failures that can lead to unsafe executions.

摘要（中文）: 随着大型语言模型向自主代理转型，用户输入经常违反合作性假设（例如，隐式意图、缺失参数、错误前提或模糊表达），从而产生文本评估无法捕捉的执行风险。现有的基准测试通常假设指令指定明确，或将评估限制在纯文本的单轮澄清，因此无法衡量在实际执行风险下的多轮歧义消除能力。我们推出了\textbf{Drift-Bench}，这是首个诊断性基准，它通过跨状态导向和面向服务的执行环境的多轮澄清，评估在输入错误下的代理语用学。\textbf{Drift-Bench}扎根于经典沟通理论，提供了一个统一的合作性崩溃分类法，并采用带有\textbf{Rise}评估协议的角色驱动型用户模拟器。实验表明，在这些错误下性能显著下降，且澄清效果随用户角色和错误类型的不同而变化。\MethodName 连接了澄清研究和代理安全评估，能够系统地诊断可能导致不安全执行的故障。

TLDR: The paper introduces Drift-Bench, a new benchmark for evaluating the ability of LLM agents to handle faulty user inputs in multi-turn interactions, revealing significant performance drops and variations based on user personas and fault types.

简要说明: 本文介绍了Drift-Bench，一个新的基准测试，用于评估大型语言模型智能体在多轮交互中处理错误用户输入的能力，揭示了基于用户角色和错误类型的显著性能下降和变化。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (9/10)
Clarity: (8/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Han Bao, Zheyuan Zhang, Pengcheng Jing, Zhengqing Yuan, Kaiwen Shi, Yanfang Ye

SafeGround: Know When to Trust GUI Grounding Models via Uncertainty Calibration

Abstract (English): Graphical User Interface (GUI) grounding aims to translate natural language instructions into executable screen coordinates, enabling automated GUI interaction. Nevertheless, incorrect grounding can result in costly, hard-to-reverse actions (e.g., erroneous payment approvals), raising concerns about model reliability. In this paper, we introduce SafeGround, an uncertainty-aware framework for GUI grounding models that enables risk-aware predictions through calibrations before testing. SafeGround leverages a distribution-aware uncertainty quantification method to capture the spatial dispersion of stochastic samples from outputs of any given model. Then, through the calibration process, SafeGround derives a test-time decision threshold with statistically guaranteed false discovery rate (FDR) control. We apply SafeGround on multiple GUI grounding models for the challenging ScreenSpot-Pro benchmark. Experimental results show that our uncertainty measure consistently outperforms existing baselines in distinguishing correct from incorrect predictions, while the calibrated threshold reliably enables rigorous risk control and potentials of substantial system-level accuracy improvements. Across multiple GUI grounding models, SafeGround improves system-level accuracy by up to 5.38% percentage points over Gemini-only inference.

摘要（中文）: 图形用户界面 (GUI) 基础旨在将自然语言指令转换为可执行的屏幕坐标，从而实现自动化的 GUI 交互。然而，不正确的基础可能导致代价高昂且难以逆转的操作（例如，错误的支付批准），从而引发对模型可靠性的担忧。在本文中，我们介绍 SafeGround，这是一个面向 GUI 基础模型的不确定性感知框架，通过测试前的校准来实现风险感知预测。SafeGround 利用一种分布感知的不确定性量化方法来捕获来自任何给定模型输出的随机样本的空间离散性。然后，通过校准过程，SafeGround 推导出具有统计保证的错误发现率 (FDR) 控制的测试时决策阈值。我们将 SafeGround 应用于具有挑战性的 ScreenSpot-Pro 基准测试的多个 GUI 基础模型。实验结果表明，我们的不确定性度量在区分正确预测和错误预测方面始终优于现有基线，而校准后的阈值能够可靠地实现严格的风险控制，并具有实质性的系统级精度改进潜力。在多个GUI基础模型中，SafeGround 将系统级精度提高了最多 5.38 个百分点，优于仅使用 Gemini 的推断。

TLDR: SafeGround introduces an uncertainty-aware framework for GUI grounding models, enabling risk-aware predictions and improving system-level accuracy through calibration and FDR control, especially in scenarios where incorrect actions can be costly.

简要说明: SafeGround 引入了一个面向 GUI grounding 模型的不确定性感知框架，通过校准和 FDR 控制实现风险感知的预测并提高系统级的准确性，特别是在错误操作代价高昂的情况下。

Relevance: (7/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Qingni Wang, Yue Fan, Xin Eric Wang

ReasonEdit: Editing Vision-Language Models using Human Reasoning

Abstract (English): Model editing aims to correct errors in large, pretrained models without altering unrelated behaviors. While some recent works have edited vision-language models (VLMs), no existing editors tackle reasoning-heavy tasks, which typically require humans and models to reason about images. We therefore propose ReasonEdit, the first VLM editor to let users explain their reasoning during editing, introducing a new, practical model editing setup. ReasonEdit continuously stores human reasoning in a codebook, and retrieves only relevant facts during inference using a novel topology-balanced multimodal embedding method inspired by network science. Across four VLMs on multiple rationale-based visual question answering datasets, ReasonEdit achieves state-of-the-art editing performance, ultimately showing that using human reasoning during editing greatly improves edit generalization.

摘要（中文）: 模型编辑旨在纠正大型预训练模型中的错误，同时不改变不相关的行为。尽管最近的一些工作已经编辑了视觉-语言模型（VLMs），但目前还没有编辑器能够处理推理密集型任务，这些任务通常需要人类和模型对图像进行推理。因此，我们提出了 ReasonEdit，这是第一个允许用户在编辑过程中解释推理过程的 VLM 编辑器，引入了一种新的实用模型编辑设置。ReasonEdit 将人类推理持续存储在一个代码本中，并在推理过程中使用一种受网络科学启发的、新颖的拓扑平衡多模态嵌入方法来检索相关的知识事实。在多个基于原理的视觉问答数据集上对四个 VLM 进行的实验表明，ReasonEdit 实现了最先进的编辑性能，最终表明在编辑过程中使用人类推理可以极大地提高编辑的泛化能力。

TLDR: ReasonEdit is a new VLM editing method that leverages human reasoning during the editing process, storing and retrieving relevant facts using a novel topology-balanced multimodal embedding, demonstrating SOTA performance on rationale-based VQA tasks.

简要说明: ReasonEdit是一种新的VLM编辑方法，它在编辑过程中利用人类推理，使用一种新颖的拓扑平衡多模态嵌入来存储和检索相关事实，并在基于理由的VQA任务上展示了SOTA性能。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Jiaxing Qiu, Kaihua Hou, Roxana Daneshjou, Ahmed Alaa, Thomas Hartvigsen

LIVE: Long-horizon Interactive Video World Modeling

Abstract (English): Autoregressive video world models predict future visual observations conditioned on actions. While effective over short horizons, these models often struggle with long-horizon generation, as small prediction errors accumulate over time. Prior methods alleviate this by introducing pre-trained teacher models and sequence-level distribution matching, which incur additional computational cost and fail to prevent error propagation beyond the training horizon. In this work, we propose LIVE, a Long-horizon Interactive Video world modEl that enforces bounded error accumulation via a novel cycle-consistency objective, thereby eliminating the need for teacher-based distillation. Specifically, LIVE first performs a forward rollout from ground-truth frames and then applies a reverse generation process to reconstruct the initial state. The diffusion loss is subsequently computed on the reconstructed terminal state, providing an explicit constraint on long-horizon error propagation. Moreover, we provide an unified view that encompasses different approaches and introduce progressive training curriculum to stabilize training. Experiments demonstrate that LIVE achieves state-of-the-art performance on long-horizon benchmarks, generating stable, high-quality videos far beyond training rollout lengths.

摘要（中文）: 自回归视频世界模型在动作的条件下预测未来的视觉观察。虽然在短时间范围内有效，但这些模型通常难以处理长时间的生成，因为微小的预测误差会随着时间累积。先前的方法通过引入预训练的教师模型和序列级分布匹配来缓解这个问题，但这会带来额外的计算成本，并且无法阻止误差传播超出训练范围。在这项工作中，我们提出了LIVE，一种长时交互视频世界模型，它通过一种新颖的循环一致性目标来强制实施有界的误差累积，从而消除了对基于教师的知识蒸馏的需求。具体来说，LIVE首先从真实帧执行前向展开，然后应用反向生成过程来重建初始状态。随后，在重建的终端状态上计算扩散损失，为长时间的误差传播提供了一个明确的约束。此外，我们提供了一个统一的视角，涵盖了不同的方法，并引入了渐进式训练课程以稳定训练。实验表明，LIVE在长时基准测试中实现了最先进的性能，生成了远远超出训练展开长度的稳定、高质量的视频。

TLDR: The paper introduces LIVE, a long-horizon video world model that uses a cycle-consistency objective to limit error accumulation, achieving state-of-the-art performance in generating stable, high-quality videos beyond training rollout lengths.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了LIVE，一种长程视频世界模型，它使用循环一致性目标来限制误差累积，从而在生成超出训练长度的稳定、高质量视频方面实现了最先进的性能。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Junchao Huang, Ziyang Ye, Xinting Hu, Tianyu He, Guiyu Zhang, Shaoshuai Shi, Jiang Bian, Li Jiang

Edge-Optimized Vision-Language Models for Underground Infrastructure Assessment

Abstract (English): Autonomous inspection of underground infrastructure, such as sewer and culvert systems, is critical to public safety and urban sustainability. Although robotic platforms equipped with visual sensors can efficiently detect structural deficiencies, the automated generation of human-readable summaries from these detections remains a significant challenge, especially on resource-constrained edge devices. This paper presents a novel two-stage pipeline for end-to-end summarization of underground deficiencies, combining our lightweight RAPID-SCAN segmentation model with a fine-tuned Vision-Language Model (VLM) deployed on an edge computing platform. The first stage employs RAPID-SCAN (Resource-Aware Pipeline Inspection and Defect Segmentation using Compact Adaptive Network), achieving 0.834 F1-score with only 0.64M parameters for efficient defect segmentation. The second stage utilizes a fine-tuned Phi-3.5 VLM that generates concise, domain-specific summaries in natural language from the segmentation outputs. We introduce a curated dataset of inspection images with manually verified descriptions for VLM fine-tuning and evaluation. To enable real-time performance, we employ post-training quantization with hardware-specific optimization, achieving significant reductions in model size and inference latency without compromising summarization quality. We deploy and evaluate our complete pipeline on a mobile robotic platform, demonstrating its effectiveness in real-world inspection scenarios. Our results show the potential of edge-deployable integrated AI systems to bridge the gap between automated defect detection and actionable insights for infrastructure maintenance, paving the way for more scalable and autonomous inspection solutions.

摘要（中文）: 地下基础设施（如污水管道和涵洞系统）的自主检测对公共安全和城市可持续性至关重要。尽管配备视觉传感器的机器人平台可以有效地检测结构缺陷，但从这些检测结果自动生成人类可读的摘要仍然是一个重大挑战，尤其是在资源受限的边缘设备上。本文提出了一种新颖的两阶段流程，用于地下缺陷的端到端摘要生成，该流程结合了我们的轻量级 RAPID-SCAN 分割模型和部署在边缘计算平台上的微调 Vision-Language Model (VLM)。第一阶段采用 RAPID-SCAN（使用紧凑自适应网络的资源感知管道检测和缺陷分割），以仅 0.64M 参数实现了 0.834 的 F1 值，从而实现高效的缺陷分割。第二阶段利用微调后的 Phi-3.5 VLM，从分割输出以自然语言生成简洁的、特定领域的摘要。我们引入了一个精心策划的检测图像数据集，其中包含人工验证的描述，用于 VLM 微调和评估。为了实现实时性能，我们采用后训练量化和硬件特定的优化，在不影响摘要质量的前提下，显著减小了模型大小并降低了推理延迟。我们将完整的流程部署在移动机器人平台上并进行评估，展示了其在现实检测场景中的有效性。我们的结果表明，边缘可部署的集成人工智能系统有潜力弥合自动化缺陷检测和基础设施维护的可操作见解之间的差距，为更具可扩展性和自主性的检测解决方案铺平道路。

TLDR: This paper presents a two-stage edge-optimized pipeline combining a lightweight segmentation model (RAPID-SCAN) and a fine-tuned VLM (Phi-3.5) for generating human-readable summaries of underground infrastructure defects, demonstrating real-time performance on a mobile robotic platform.

简要说明: 本文提出了一种双阶段边缘优化流程，结合轻量级分割模型（RAPID-SCAN）和一个微调的VLM（Phi-3.5），用于生成地下基础设施缺陷的人类可读摘要，并在移动机器人平台上展示了实时性能。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Johny J. Lopez, Md Meftahul Ferdaus, Mahdi Abdelguerfi

RegionReasoner: Region-Grounded Multi-Round Visual Reasoning

Abstract (English): Large vision-language models have achieved remarkable progress in visual reasoning, yet most existing systems rely on single-step or text-only reasoning, limiting their ability to iteratively refine understanding across multiple visual contexts. To address this limitation, we introduce a new multi-round visual reasoning benchmark with training and test sets spanning both detection and segmentation tasks, enabling systematic evaluation under iterative reasoning scenarios. We further propose RegionReasoner, a reinforcement learning framework that enforces grounded reasoning by requiring each reasoning trace to explicitly cite the corresponding reference bounding boxes, while maintaining semantic coherence via a global-local consistency reward. This reward extracts key objects and nouns from both global scene captions and region-level captions, aligning them with the reasoning trace to ensure consistency across reasoning steps. RegionReasoner is optimized with structured rewards combining grounding fidelity and global-local semantic alignment. Experiments on detection and segmentation tasks show that RegionReasoner-7B, together with our newly introduced benchmark RegionDial-Bench, considerably improves multi-round reasoning accuracy, spatial grounding precision, and global-local consistency, establishing a strong baseline for this emerging research direction.

摘要（中文）: 大型视觉语言模型在视觉推理方面取得了显著进展，但大多数现有系统依赖于单步或纯文本推理，限制了其在多个视觉上下文中迭代优化理解的能力。为了解决这一局限性，我们引入了一个新的多轮视觉推理基准，其训练集和测试集涵盖了检测和分割任务，从而能够在迭代推理场景下进行系统评估。我们进一步提出了RegionReasoner，一个强化学习框架，该框架通过要求每个推理轨迹显式地引用相应的参考边界框来强制执行基于定位的推理，同时通过全局-局部一致性奖励来保持语义连贯性。此奖励从全局场景描述和区域级描述中提取关键对象和名词，将其与推理轨迹对齐，以确保推理步骤之间的一致性。RegionReasoner通过结合定位保真度和全局-局部语义对齐的结构化奖励进行优化。在检测和分割任务上的实验表明，RegionReasoner-7B，连同我们新提出的基准RegionDial-Bench，显著提高了多轮推理准确性、空间定位精度以及全局-局部一致性，为这一新兴研究方向建立了一个强有力的基线。

TLDR: The paper introduces a new multi-round visual reasoning benchmark and a reinforcement learning framework called RegionReasoner that enforces grounded reasoning using bounding box references and global-local consistency rewards, achieving improved accuracy, grounding, and consistency on detection and segmentation tasks.

简要说明: 本文介绍了一个新的多轮视觉推理基准测试，以及一个名为RegionReasoner的强化学习框架。该框架通过边界框引用和全局-局部一致性奖励来强化基础推理，并在检测和分割任务上实现了更高的准确性、基础性和一致性。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Wenfang Sun, Hao Chen, Yingjun Du, Yefeng Zheng, Cees G. M. Snoek

Refer-Agent: A Collaborative Multi-Agent System with Reasoning and Reflection for Referring Video Object Segmentation

Abstract (English): Referring Video Object Segmentation (RVOS) aims to segment objects in videos based on textual queries. Current methods mainly rely on large-scale supervised fine-tuning (SFT) of Multi-modal Large Language Models (MLLMs). However, this paradigm suffers from heavy data dependence and limited scalability against the rapid evolution of MLLMs. Although recent zero-shot approaches offer a flexible alternative, their performance remains significantly behind SFT-based methods, due to the straightforward workflow designs. To address these limitations, we propose \textbf{Refer-Agent}, a collaborative multi-agent system with alternating reasoning-reflection mechanisms. This system decomposes RVOS into step-by-step reasoning process. During reasoning, we introduce a Coarse-to-Fine frame selection strategy to ensure the frame diversity and textual relevance, along with a Dynamic Focus Layout that adaptively adjusts the agent's visual focus. Furthermore, we propose a Chain-of-Reflection mechanism, which employs a Questioner-Responder pair to generate a self-reflection chain, enabling the system to verify intermediate results and generates feedback for next-round reasoning refinement. Extensive experiments on five challenging benchmarks demonstrate that Refer-Agent significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods, including both SFT-based models and zero-shot approaches. Moreover, Refer-Agent is flexible and enables fast integration of new MLLMs without any additional fine-tuning costs. Code will be released.

摘要（中文）: 指代视频目标分割（RVOS）旨在根据文本查询分割视频中的对象。目前的方法主要依赖于多模态大型语言模型（MLLM）的大规模监督微调（SFT）。然而，这种模式存在严重的数据依赖性，并且面对 MLLM 的快速发展，可扩展性有限。尽管最近的零样本方法提供了一种灵活的替代方案，但由于其直接的工作流程设计，其性能仍远落后于基于 SFT 的方法。为了解决这些局限性，我们提出了 \textbf{Refer-Agent}，一种具有交替推理-反思机制的协同多智能体系统。该系统将 RVOS 分解为逐步推理过程。在推理过程中，我们引入了一种由粗到精的帧选择策略，以确保帧的多样性和文本相关性，并结合一种动态焦点布局，自适应地调整智能体的视觉焦点。此外，我们提出了一种链式反思机制，该机制采用提问者-回答者对来生成自我反思链，使系统能够验证中间结果并为下一轮推理改进生成反馈。在五个具有挑战性的基准测试上的大量实验表明，Refer-Agent 的性能显著优于最先进的方法，包括基于 SFT 的模型和零样本方法。此外，Refer-Agent 具有灵活性，可以快速集成新的 MLLM，而无需任何额外的微调成本。代码将会开源。

TLDR: The paper introduces Refer-Agent, a collaborative multi-agent system with reasoning and reflection for referring video object segmentation. It outperforms existing SFT-based and zero-shot methods on RVOS tasks and allows fast integration of new MLLMs without fine-tuning.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了Refer-Agent，一个用于指代视频对象分割的协作多智能体系统，具有推理和反思能力。它在RVOS任务上优于现有的SFT和零样本方法，并且能够快速集成新的MLLM，无需微调。

Relevance: (9/10)

Authors: Haichao Jiang, Tianming Liang, Wei-Shi Zheng, Jian-Fang Hu

PnP-U3D: Plug-and-Play 3D Framework Bridging Autoregression and Diffusion for Unified Understanding and Generation

Abstract (English): The rapid progress of large multimodal models has inspired efforts toward unified frameworks that couple understanding and generation. While such paradigms have shown remarkable success in 2D, extending them to 3D remains largely underexplored. Existing attempts to unify 3D tasks under a single autoregressive (AR) paradigm lead to significant performance degradation due to forced signal quantization and prohibitive training cost. Our key insight is that the essential challenge lies not in enforcing a unified autoregressive paradigm, but in enabling effective information interaction between generation and understanding while minimally compromising their inherent capabilities and leveraging pretrained models to reduce training cost. Guided by this perspective, we present the first unified framework for 3D understanding and generation that combines autoregression with diffusion. Specifically, we adopt an autoregressive next-token prediction paradigm for 3D understanding, and a continuous diffusion paradigm for 3D generation. A lightweight transformer bridges the feature space of large language models and the conditional space of 3D diffusion models, enabling effective cross-modal information exchange while preserving the priors learned by standalone models. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our framework achieves state-of-the-art performance across diverse 3D understanding and generation benchmarks, while also excelling in 3D editing tasks. These results highlight the potential of unified AR+diffusion models as a promising direction for building more general-purpose 3D intelligence.

摘要（中文）: 大型多模态模型的快速发展激发了人们对耦合理解和生成的统一框架的探索。虽然这种范式在二维领域取得了显著成功，但将其扩展到三维领域在很大程度上仍未被探索。现有尝试在单个自回归（AR）范式下统一三维任务的方法，由于强制信号量化和过高的训练成本导致了显著的性能下降。我们的关键见解是，本质上的挑战并非在于强制实施统一的自回归范式，而在于实现生成和理解之间有效的信息交互，同时尽可能减少对其固有能力的compromised，并利用预训练模型来降低训练成本。在这一观点的指导下，我们提出了第一个结合自回归和扩散的三维理解和生成统一框架。具体而言，我们采用自回归下一令牌预测范式进行三维理解，采用连续扩散范式进行三维生成。轻量级Transformer桥接了大型语言模型的特征空间和三维扩散模型的条件空间，从而能够实现有效的跨模态信息交换，同时保留了独立模型学习到的先验知识。大量实验表明，我们的框架在各种三维理解和生成基准测试中都取得了最先进的性能，同时在三维编辑任务中也表现出色。这些结果突出了统一的AR+扩散模型作为构建更通用的三维智能的有前景的方向的潜力。

TLDR: The paper introduces PnP-U3D, a unified framework for 3D understanding and generation that combines autoregressive and diffusion models, achieving state-of-the-art results in various 3D tasks.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了PnP-U3D，一个统一的3D理解和生成框架，结合了自回归和扩散模型，并在各种3D任务中取得了最先进的结果。

Relevance: (7/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Yongwei Chen, Tianyi Wei, Yushi Lan, Zhaoyang Lyu, Shangchen Zhou, Xudong Xu, Xingang Pan

Contextualized Visual Personalization in Vision-Language Models

Abstract (English): Despite recent progress in vision-language models (VLMs), existing approaches often fail to generate personalized responses based on the user's specific experiences, as they lack the ability to associate visual inputs with a user's accumulated visual-textual context. We newly formalize this challenge as contextualized visual personalization, which requires the visual recognition and textual retrieval of personalized visual experiences by VLMs when interpreting new images. To address this issue, we propose CoViP, a unified framework that treats personalized image captioning as a core task for contextualized visual personalization and improves this capability through reinforcement-learning-based post-training and caption-augmented generation. We further introduce diagnostic evaluations that explicitly rule out textual shortcut solutions and verify whether VLMs truly leverage visual context. Extensive experiments demonstrate that existing open-source and proprietary VLMs exhibit substantial limitations, while CoViP not only improves personalized image captioning but also yields holistic gains across downstream personalization tasks. These results highlight CoViP as a crucial stage for enabling robust and generalizable contextualized visual personalization.

摘要（中文）: 尽管视觉-语言模型（VLMs）近期取得了进展，但由于缺乏将视觉输入与用户积累的视觉-文本上下文相关联的能力，现有方法常常无法根据用户的特定经验生成个性化响应。我们将这一挑战新形式化为上下文相关的视觉个性化，这需要在解释新图像时，VLM能够对个性化的视觉体验进行视觉识别和文本检索。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了CoViP，一个统一的框架，该框架将个性化图像描述生成作为上下文相关的视觉个性化的核心任务，并通过基于强化学习的后训练和描述增强生成来提高这一能力。我们进一步引入了诊断性评估，明确排除了文本捷径解决方案，并验证了VLM是否真正利用了视觉上下文。大量实验表明，现有的开源和专有VLM表现出明显的局限性，而CoViP不仅提高了个性化图像描述生成能力，还在下游个性化任务中产生了整体收益。这些结果表明，CoViP是实现稳健和可泛化的上下文相关的视觉个性化的关键阶段。

TLDR: The paper introduces Contextualized Visual Personalization, a new challenge for VLMs, and proposes CoViP, a reinforcement-learning-based framework to address it, demonstrating improved personalized image captioning and downstream task performance.

简要说明: 该论文提出了情境化视觉个性化这一新的VLM挑战，并提出了CoViP，一个基于强化学习的框架来解决这个问题，展示了改进的个性化图像描述和下游任务性能。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Yeongtak Oh, Sangwon Yu, Junsung Park, Han Cheol Moon, Jisoo Mok, Sungroh Yoon

Z3D: Zero-Shot 3D Visual Grounding from Images

Abstract (English): 3D visual grounding (3DVG) aims to localize objects in a 3D scene based on natural language queries. In this work, we explore zero-shot 3DVG from multi-view images alone, without requiring any geometric supervision or object priors. We introduce Z3D, a universal grounding pipeline that flexibly operates on multi-view images while optionally incorporating camera poses and depth maps. We identify key bottlenecks in prior zero-shot methods causing significant performance degradation and address them with (i) a state-of-the-art zero-shot 3D instance segmentation method to generate high-quality 3D bounding box proposals and (ii) advanced reasoning via prompt-based segmentation, which utilizes full capabilities of modern VLMs. Extensive experiments on the ScanRefer and Nr3D benchmarks demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance among zero-shot methods. Code is available at https://github.com/col14m/z3d .

摘要（中文）: 3D视觉定位旨在根据自然语言查询在3D场景中定位物体。 本文探讨仅使用多视角图像进行零样本3D视觉定位，无需任何几何监督或物体先验。我们提出了Z3D，一种通用的定位流程，其灵活地处理多视角图像，并可选择性地结合相机姿态和深度图。我们识别出先前零样本方法中导致性能显著下降的关键瓶颈，并通过以下方法解决这些问题：（i）一种最先进的零样本3D实例分割方法，用于生成高质量的3D边界框提议；（ii）通过基于提示的分割进行高级推理，充分利用现代视觉语言模型（VLM）的全部能力。 在ScanRefer和Nr3D基准测试上的大量实验表明，我们的方法在零样本方法中实现了最先进的性能。 代码可在https://github.com/col14m/z3d 获取。

TLDR: The paper introduces Z3D, a zero-shot 3D visual grounding pipeline that achieves state-of-the-art performance on ScanRefer and Nr3D benchmarks by using a high-quality 3D instance segmentation method and prompt-based segmentation with VLMs.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了Z3D，一个零样本3D视觉定位流程，通过使用高质量的3D实例分割方法和基于提示的分割与VLM，在ScanRefer和Nr3D基准测试中实现了最先进的性能。

Relevance: (7/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Nikita Drozdov, Andrey Lemeshko, Nikita Gavrilov, Anton Konushin, Danila Rukhovich, Maksim Kolodiazhnyi

LaVPR: Benchmarking Language and Vision for Place Recognition

Abstract (English): Visual Place Recognition (VPR) often fails under extreme environmental changes and perceptual aliasing. Furthermore, standard systems cannot perform "blind" localization from verbal descriptions alone, a capability needed for applications such as emergency response. To address these challenges, we introduce LaVPR, a large-scale benchmark that extends existing VPR datasets with over 650,000 rich natural-language descriptions. Using LaVPR, we investigate two paradigms: Multi-Modal Fusion for enhanced robustness and Cross-Modal Retrieval for language-based localization. Our results show that language descriptions yield consistent gains in visually degraded conditions, with the most significant impact on smaller backbones. Notably, adding language allows compact models to rival the performance of much larger vision-only architectures. For cross-modal retrieval, we establish a baseline using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) and Multi-Similarity loss, which substantially outperforms standard contrastive methods across vision-language models. Ultimately, LaVPR enables a new class of localization systems that are both resilient to real-world stochasticity and practical for resource-constrained deployment. Our dataset and code are available at https://github.com/oferidan1/LaVPR.

摘要（中文）: 视觉场景识别（VPR）在极端环境变化和感知混淆下经常失效。此外，标准系统无法仅凭口头描述进行“盲”定位，而这种能力对于诸如应急响应等应用至关重要。为了应对这些挑战，我们引入了LaVPR，一个大型基准测试数据集，它通过超过65万条丰富的自然语言描述扩展了现有的VPR数据集。利用LaVPR，我们研究了两种范式：用于增强鲁棒性的多模态融合，以及用于基于语言的定位的跨模态检索。我们的结果表明，语言描述在视觉退化条件下产生了持续的增益，对较小的骨干网络影响最为显著。值得注意的是，添加语言使得紧凑的模型能够与更大的纯视觉架构相媲美。对于跨模态检索，我们建立了使用低秩自适应（LoRA）和多相似性损失的基线，该基线在各种视觉-语言模型上显著优于标准的对比方法。最终，LaVPR实现了新一类定位系统，这些系统既能抵抗现实世界的随机性，又适用于资源受限的部署。我们的数据集和代码可在https://github.com/oferidan1/LaVPR获取。

TLDR: The paper introduces LaVPR, a large-scale benchmark for visual place recognition using language descriptions, enhancing robustness in challenging conditions and enabling language-based localization, especially for resource-constrained applications.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了LaVPR，一个大规模的视觉地点识别基准，它利用语言描述增强了在具有挑战性条件下的鲁棒性，并实现了基于语言的定位，尤其适用于资源受限的应用。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Ofer Idan, Dan Badur, Yosi Keller, Yoli Shavit

InstaDrive: Instance-Aware Driving World Models for Realistic and Consistent Video Generation

Abstract (English): Autonomous driving relies on robust models trained on high-quality, large-scale multi-view driving videos. While world models offer a cost-effective solution for generating realistic driving videos, they struggle to maintain instance-level temporal consistency and spatial geometric fidelity. To address these challenges, we propose InstaDrive, a novel framework that enhances driving video realism through two key advancements: (1) Instance Flow Guider, which extracts and propagates instance features across frames to enforce temporal consistency, preserving instance identity over time. (2) Spatial Geometric Aligner, which improves spatial reasoning, ensures precise instance positioning, and explicitly models occlusion hierarchies. By incorporating these instance-aware mechanisms, InstaDrive achieves state-of-the-art video generation quality and enhances downstream autonomous driving tasks on the nuScenes dataset. Additionally, we utilize CARLA's autopilot to procedurally and stochastically simulate rare but safety-critical driving scenarios across diverse maps and regions, enabling rigorous safety evaluation for autonomous systems. Our project page is https://shanpoyang654.github.io/InstaDrive/page.html.

摘要（中文）: 自动驾驶依赖于基于高质量、大规模多视角驾驶视频训练的鲁棒模型。尽管世界模型为生成逼真驾驶视频提供了一种具有成本效益的解决方案，但它们难以维持实例级的时序一致性和空间几何保真度。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了一种名为InstaDrive的新框架，它通过两个关键进展来增强驾驶视频的真实性：（1）实例流引导器，它提取并在帧之间传播实例特征以增强时序一致性，在时间推移中保持实例身份。（2）空间几何对齐器，它改进了空间推理，确保了精确的实例定位，并显式地对遮挡层级结构进行建模。通过结合这些实例感知机制，InstaDrive实现了最先进的视频生成质量，并增强了nuScenes数据集上的下游自动驾驶任务。此外，我们利用CARLA的自动驾驶仪，在不同的地图和区域中以程序化和随机的方式模拟罕见但对安全至关重要的驾驶场景，从而能够对自动驾驶系统进行严格的安全评估。我们的项目主页是 https://shanpoyang654.github.io/InstaDrive/page.html。

TLDR: InstaDrive improves driving video generation by enforcing instance-level temporal consistency and spatial geometric fidelity, achieving state-of-the-art results and enabling safety evaluation.

简要说明: InstaDrive通过增强实例级别的时间一致性和空间几何保真度来改进驾驶视频生成，实现了最先进的结果，并可用于安全评估。

Authors: Zhuoran Yang, Xi Guo, Chenjing Ding, Chiyu Wang, Wei Wu, Yanyong Zhang

EventFlash: Towards Efficient MLLMs for Event-Based Vision

Abstract (English): Event-based multimodal large language models (MLLMs) enable robust perception in high-speed and low-light scenarios, addressing key limitations of frame-based MLLMs. However, current event-based MLLMs often rely on dense image-like processing paradigms, overlooking the spatiotemporal sparsity of event streams and resulting in high computational cost. In this paper, we propose EventFlash, a novel and efficient MLLM to explore spatiotemporal token sparsification for reducing data redundancy and accelerating inference. Technically, we build EventMind, a large-scale and scene-diverse dataset with over 500k instruction sets, providing both short and long event stream sequences to support our curriculum training strategy. We then present an adaptive temporal window aggregation module for efficient temporal sampling, which adaptively compresses temporal tokens while retaining key temporal cues. Finally, a sparse density-guided attention module is designed to improve spatial token efficiency by selecting informative regions and suppressing empty or sparse areas. Experimental results show that EventFlash achieves a $12.4\times$ throughput improvement over the baseline (EventFlash-Zero) while maintaining comparable performance. It supports long-range event stream processing with up to 1,000 bins, significantly outperforming the 5-bin limit of EventGPT. We believe EventFlash serves as an efficient foundation model for event-based vision.

摘要（中文）: 基于事件的多模态大型语言模型（MLLM）能够在高速和低光场景中实现稳健的感知，解决了基于帧的MLLM的关键局限性。然而，当前的基于事件的MLLM通常依赖于密集的类图像处理范式，忽略了事件流的时空稀疏性，导致高昂的计算成本。本文提出EventFlash，一种新颖且高效的MLLM，旨在探索时空token稀疏化，以减少数据冗余并加速推理。在技术上，我们构建了EventMind，一个大规模且场景多样的数据集，包含超过50万条指令集，提供短的和长的事件流序列，以支持我们的课程训练策略。然后，我们提出了一种自适应时间窗口聚合模块，用于高效的时间采样，该模块能够自适应地压缩时间token，同时保留关键的时间线索。最后，设计了一种稀疏密度引导的注意力模块，通过选择信息丰富的区域并抑制空或稀疏区域，来提高空间token的效率。实验结果表明，EventFlash在保持相当性能的同时，比基线模型（EventFlash-Zero）实现了12.4倍的吞吐量提升。它支持长程事件流处理，最多可达1000个bin，显著优于EventGPT的5-bin限制。我们相信EventFlash可以作为基于事件视觉的高效基础模型。

TLDR: EventFlash introduces an efficient MLLM for event-based vision by employing spatiotemporal token sparsification, achieving significant throughput improvements on a newly created dataset.

简要说明: EventFlash 提出了一种高效的事件视觉多模态大语言模型，通过时空 token 稀疏化实现，并在新创建的数据集上取得了显著的吞吐量提升。

Authors: Shaoyu Liu, Jianing Li, Guanghui Zhao, Yunjian Zhang, Wen Jiang, Ming Li, Xiangyang Ji

VIRAL: Visual In-Context Reasoning via Analogy in Diffusion Transformers

Abstract (English): Replicating In-Context Learning (ICL) in computer vision remains challenging due to task heterogeneity. We propose \textbf{VIRAL}, a framework that elicits visual reasoning from a pre-trained image editing model by formulating ICL as conditional generation via visual analogy ($x_s : x_t :: x_q : y_q$). We adapt a frozen Diffusion Transformer (DiT) using role-aware multi-image conditioning and introduce a Mixture-of-Experts LoRA to mitigate gradient interference across diverse tasks. Additionally, to bridge the gaps in current visual context datasets, we curate a large-scale dataset spanning perception, restoration, and editing. Experiments demonstrate that VIRAL outperforms existing methods, validating that a unified V-ICL paradigm can handle the majority of visual tasks, including open-domain editing. Our code is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/VIRAL-744A

摘要（中文）: 由于任务异构性，在计算机视觉中复现上下文学习(ICL)仍然具有挑战性。我们提出了一个框架\textbf{VIRAL}，它通过将ICL公式化为基于视觉类比的条件生成 ($x_s : x_t :: x_q : y_q$)，从而从预训练的图像编辑模型中激发视觉推理能力。我们使用角色感知的多图像条件作用来适配一个冻结的扩散Transformer (DiT)，并引入一个混合专家LoRA来缓解不同任务之间的梯度干扰。此外，为了弥合当前视觉上下文数据集的差距，我们精心策划了一个大规模数据集，涵盖感知、恢复和编辑。实验表明，VIRAL优于现有方法，验证了统一的V-ICL范式可以处理大多数视觉任务，包括开放域编辑。我们的代码可在https://anonymous.4open.science/r/VIRAL-744A找到。

TLDR: The paper presents VIRAL, a framework enabling visual in-context learning (V-ICL) in diffusion transformers via visual analogy. It introduces a new dataset and achieves state-of-the-art performance in various visual tasks, including open-domain editing.

简要说明: 该论文提出了VIRAL，一个通过视觉类比实现扩散Transformer中的视觉上下文学习（V-ICL）的框架。它引入了一个新的数据集，并在各种视觉任务（包括开放域编辑）中实现了最先进的性能。

Authors: Zhiwen Li, Zhongjie Duan, Jinyan Ye, Cen Chen, Daoyuan Chen, Yaliang Li, Yingda Chen

4DPC$^2$hat: Towards Dynamic Point Cloud Understanding with Failure-Aware Bootstrapping

Abstract (English): Point clouds provide a compact and expressive representation of 3D objects, and have recently been integrated into multimodal large language models (MLLMs). However, existing methods primarily focus on static objects, while understanding dynamic point cloud sequences remains largely unexplored. This limitation is mainly caused by the lack of large-scale cross-modal datasets and the difficulty of modeling motions in spatio-temporal contexts. To bridge this gap, we present 4DPC$^2$hat, the first MLLM tailored for dynamic point cloud understanding. To this end, we construct a large-scale cross-modal dataset 4DPC$^2$hat-200K via a meticulous two-stage pipeline consisting of topology-consistent 4D point construction and two-level captioning. The dataset contains over 44K dynamic object sequences, 700K point cloud frames, and 200K curated question-answer (QA) pairs, supporting inquiries about counting, temporal relationship, action, spatial relationship, and appearance. At the core of the framework, we introduce a Mamba-enhanced temporal reasoning MLLM to capture long-range dependencies and dynamic patterns among a point cloud sequence. Furthermore, we propose a failure-aware bootstrapping learning strategy that iteratively identifies model deficiencies and generates targeted QA supervision to continuously strengthen corresponding reasoning capabilities. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our 4DPC$^2$hat significantly improves action understanding and temporal reasoning compared with existing models, establishing a strong foundation for 4D dynamic point cloud understanding.

摘要（中文）: 点云为3D对象提供了一种紧凑而富有表现力的表示形式，并且最近已被集成到多模态大型语言模型（MLLM）中。然而，现有方法主要集中在静态对象上，而对动态点云序列的理解在很大程度上仍未被探索。这种限制主要是由于缺乏大规模跨模态数据集以及在时空环境中建模运动的难度所致。为了弥合这一差距，我们提出了4DPC$^2$hat，这是首个专为动态点云理解而定制的MLLM。为此，我们通过精心的两阶段流程构建了一个大规模跨模态数据集4DPC$^2$hat-200K，该流程包括拓扑一致的4D点云构建和两级标注。该数据集包含超过4.4万个动态对象序列、70万个点云帧和20万个精心策划的问答(QA)对，支持关于计数、时间关系、动作、空间关系和外观的查询。在该框架的核心，我们引入了一个Mamba增强的时序推理MLLM，以捕获点云序列中的长程依赖关系和动态模式。此外，我们提出了一种故障感知引导的自举学习策略，该策略迭代地识别模型缺陷并生成有针对性的QA监督，以持续加强相应的推理能力。大量实验表明，与现有模型相比，我们的4DPC$^2$hat显著提高了动作理解和时间推理能力，为4D动态点云理解奠定了坚实的基础。

TLDR: This paper introduces 4DPC$^2$hat, a novel MLLM and dataset for dynamic point cloud understanding, featuring a temporal reasoning MLLM and failure-aware bootstrapping to significantly improve action understanding and temporal reasoning.

简要说明: 本文介绍了 4DPC$^2$hat，一种用于动态点云理解的新型MLLM和数据集。它包含一个时序推理MLLM和一个故障感知自举学习策略，显著提升了动作理解和时序推理能力。

Authors: Xindan Zhang, Weilong Yan, Yufei Shi, Xuerui Qiu, Tao He, Ying Li, Ming Li, Hehe Fan

IVC-Prune: Revealing the Implicit Visual Coordinates in LVLMs for Vision Token Pruning

Abstract (English): Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) achieve impressive performance across multiple tasks. A significant challenge, however, is their prohibitive inference cost when processing high-resolution visual inputs. While visual token pruning has emerged as a promising solution, existing methods that primarily focus on semantic relevance often discard tokens that are crucial for spatial reasoning. We address this gap through a novel insight into \emph{how LVLMs process spatial reasoning}. Specifically, we reveal that LVLMs implicitly establish visual coordinate systems through Rotary Position Embeddings (RoPE), where specific token positions serve as \textbf{implicit visual coordinates} (IVC tokens) that are essential for spatial reasoning. Based on this insight, we propose \textbf{IVC-Prune}, a training-free, prompt-aware pruning strategy that retains both IVC tokens and semantically relevant foreground tokens. IVC tokens are identified by theoretically analyzing the mathematical properties of RoPE, targeting positions at which its rotation matrices approximate identity matrix or the $90^\circ$ rotation matrix. Foreground tokens are identified through a robust two-stage process: semantic seed discovery followed by contextual refinement via value-vector similarity. Extensive evaluations across four representative LVLMs and twenty diverse benchmarks show that IVC-Prune reduces visual tokens by approximately 50\% while maintaining $\geq$ 99\% of the original performance and even achieving improvements on several benchmarks. Source codes are available at https://github.com/FireRedTeam/IVC-Prune.

摘要（中文）: 大型视觉语言模型（LVLMs）在多个任务中取得了令人印象深刻的性能。然而，一个显著的挑战是在处理高分辨率视觉输入时，其高昂的推理成本。虽然视觉Token剪枝已经成为一种有前景的解决方案，但现有的主要关注语义相关性的方法通常会丢弃对空间推理至关重要的Token。我们通过探究 \emph{LVLMs如何处理空间推理}的全新视角来解决这一差距。具体而言，我们揭示了LVLMs通过旋转位置嵌入（RoPE）隐式地建立视觉坐标系，其中特定的Token位置充当对空间推理至关重要的\textbf{隐式视觉坐标}（IVC Token）。基于此洞察，我们提出\textbf{IVC-Prune}，一种免训练、提示感知的剪枝策略，它保留了IVC Token和语义相关的前景Token。IVC Token是通过理论分析RoPE的数学特性来识别的，目标是其旋转矩阵近似于单位矩阵或$90^\circ$旋转矩阵的位置。前景Token通过稳健的两阶段过程来识别：语义种子发现，然后通过值向量相似性进行上下文细化。在四个具有代表性的LVLMs和二十个不同的基准测试上的广泛评估表明，IVC-Prune减少了大约50%的视觉Token，同时保持了$\geq$ 99%的原始性能，甚至在几个基准测试上实现了改进。源代码可在https://github.com/FireRedTeam/IVC-Prune 上获取。

TLDR: The paper proposes IVC-Prune, a training-free visual token pruning method for LVLMs that leverages implicit visual coordinates established by RoPE to retain crucial spatial information, achieving significant token reduction with minimal performance loss.

简要说明: 本文提出了一种名为IVC-Prune的免训练视觉令牌剪枝方法，该方法利用RoPE建立的隐式视觉坐标来保留关键的空间信息，从而在最小性能损失下实现显著的令牌减少。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (9/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Zhichao Sun, Yidong Ma, Gang Liu, Yibo Chen, Xu Tang, Yao Hu, Yongchao Xu

Video-OPD: Efficient Post-Training of Multimodal Large Language Models for Temporal Video Grounding via On-Policy Distillation

Abstract (English): Reinforcement learning has emerged as a principled post-training paradigm for Temporal Video Grounding (TVG) due to its on-policy optimization, yet existing GRPO-based methods remain fundamentally constrained by sparse reward signals and substantial computational overhead. We propose Video-OPD, an efficient post-training framework for TVG inspired by recent advances in on-policy distillation. Video-OPD optimizes trajectories sampled directly from the current policy, thereby preserving alignment between training and inference distributions, while a frontier teacher supplies dense, token-level supervision via a reverse KL divergence objective. This formulation preserves the on-policy property critical for mitigating distributional shift, while converting sparse, episode-level feedback into fine-grained, step-wise learning signals. Building on Video-OPD, we introduce Teacher-Validated Disagreement Focusing (TVDF), a lightweight training curriculum that iteratively prioritizes trajectories that are both teacher-reliable and maximally informative for the student, thereby improving training efficiency. Empirical results demonstrate that Video-OPD consistently outperforms GRPO while achieving substantially faster convergence and lower computational cost, establishing on-policy distillation as an effective alternative to conventional reinforcement learning for TVG.

摘要（中文）: 强化学习已成为时序视频定位（TVG）的一种有原则的后训练范式，这归功于其在策略优化，但现有的基于GRPO的方法仍然受到稀疏奖励信号和大量计算开销的根本限制。我们提出Video-OPD，一种受近期在策略蒸馏进展启发的、高效的TVG后训练框架。Video-OPD优化直接从当前策略中采样的轨迹，从而保持训练和推理分布之间的一致性，同时前沿教师通过反向KL散度目标提供密集的、token级别的监督。这种公式保留了对缓解分布漂移至关重要的在策略属性，同时将稀疏的、episode级别的反馈转化为细粒度的、step级别的学习信号。在Video-OPD的基础上，我们引入了教师验证差异聚焦（TVDF），一种轻量级的训练课程，它迭代地优先考虑那些既具有教师可靠性又对学生最具信息量的轨迹，从而提高训练效率。实验结果表明，Video-OPD始终优于GRPO，同时实现了更快的收敛速度和更低的计算成本，证明了在策略蒸馏是TVG传统强化学习的一种有效替代方案。

TLDR: The paper presents Video-OPD, an efficient post-training method for Temporal Video Grounding (TVG) using on-policy distillation and a novel training curriculum (TVDF) to address limitations of reinforcement learning-based approaches.

简要说明: 该论文提出了Video-OPD，一种用于时序视频定位(TVG)的高效微调方法，它使用在策略蒸馏和一种新的训练课程(TVDF)来克服基于强化学习方法的局限性。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)

Authors: Jiaze Li, Hao Yin, Haoran Xu, Boshen Xu, Wenhui Tan, Zewen He, Jianzhong Ju, Zhenbo Luo, Jian Luan

ViThinker: Active Vision-Language Reasoning via Dynamic Perceptual Querying

Abstract (English): Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning excels in language models but struggles in vision-language models due to premature visual-to-text conversion that discards continuous information such as geometry and spatial layout. While recent methods enhance CoT through static enumeration or attention-based selection, they remain passive, i.e., processing pre-computed inputs rather than actively seeking task-relevant details. Inspired by human active perception, we introduce ViThinker, a framework that enables vision-language models to autonomously generate decision (query) tokens triggering the synthesis of expert-aligned visual features on demand. ViThinker internalizes vision-expert capabilities during training, performing generative mental simulation during inference without external tool calls. Through a two-stage curriculum: first distilling frozen experts into model parameters, then learning task-driven querying via sparsity penalties, i.e., ViThinker discovers minimal sufficient perception for each reasoning step. Evaluations across vision-centric benchmarks demonstrate consistent improvements, validating that active query generation outperforms passive approaches in both perceptual grounding and reasoning accuracy.

摘要（中文）: 链式思考 (CoT) 推理在语言模型中表现出色，但在视觉-语言模型中表现不佳，原因是过早的视觉到文本的转换丢弃了诸如几何形状和空间布局之类的连续信息。虽然最近的方法通过静态枚举或基于注意力的选择来增强 CoT，但它们仍然是被动的，即处理预先计算的输入，而不是主动寻找与任务相关的细节。受到人类主动感知的启发，我们引入了 ViThinker，这是一种框架，它使视觉-语言模型能够自主生成决策（查询）令牌，从而按需触发专家对齐的视觉特征的合成。ViThinker 在训练期间将视觉专家能力内在化，在推理期间执行生成式心理模拟，而无需外部工具调用。通过一个两阶段课程：首先将冻结的专家知识提炼到模型参数中，然后通过稀疏性惩罚学习任务驱动的查询，即 ViThinker 发现了每个推理步骤所需的最小充分感知。在以视觉为中心的基准测试中的评估表明，ViThinker 显示出持续的改进，验证了主动查询生成在感知基础和推理准确性方面均优于被动方法。

TLDR: ViThinker introduces a framework for vision-language models that enables active querying of visual features, improving reasoning accuracy by emulating human active perception and generating expert-aligned features on demand.

简要说明: ViThinker 提出了一个视觉-语言模型的框架，通过主动查询视觉特征，模拟人类的主动感知，并根据需求生成专家对齐的特征，从而提高推理准确性。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (8/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Weihang You, Qingchan Zhu, David Liu, Yi Pan, Geng Yuan, Hanqi Jiang

Reasoning Cache: Continual Improvement Over Long Horizons via Short-Horizon RL

Abstract (English): Large Language Models (LLMs) that can continually improve beyond their training budgets are able to solve increasingly difficult problems by adapting at test time, a property we refer to as extrapolation. However, standard reinforcement learning (RL) operates over fixed problem distributions and training budgets, which limits extrapolation amidst distribution shift at test time. To address this, we introduce RC, an iterative decoding algorithm that replaces standard autoregressive decoding during both training and inference. RC exploits an asymmetry between the response generation and summarization capabilities of LLMs to construct reasoning chains that consistently improve across iterations. Models trained to use RC can extrapolate and continually improve over reasoning horizons more than an order of magnitude longer than those seen during training. Empirically, training a 4B model with RC using a 16k-token training budget improves performance on HMMT 2025 from 40% to nearly 70% with 0.5m tokens at test time, outperforming both comparably sized models and many larger reasoning LLMs. Finally, we also show that models trained with RC can more effectively leverage existing scaffolds to further scale test-time performance, due to the improved summary-conditioned generation abilities learned through training.

摘要（中文）: 能够超越其训练预算进行持续改进的大型语言模型（LLMs）可以通过在测试时进行适应来解决日益复杂的问题，我们称之为外推能力。 然而，标准的强化学习（RL）是在固定的问题分布和训练预算下运行的，这限制了测试时分布偏移下的外推能力。 为了解决这个问题，我们引入了RC，一种迭代解码算法，它在训练和推理阶段都替代了标准的自回归解码。RC利用LLM在响应生成和总结能力之间的不对称性来构建推理链，使其在迭代过程中持续改进。 经训练能够使用RC的模型能够外推，并在推理时域上持续改进，其长度比训练期间所见的长度大一个数量级以上。 经验上，使用RC训练一个具有16k tokens训练预算的4B模型，在测试时使用0.5m tokens，将HMMT 2025上的性能从40%提高到近70%，优于规模相当的模型和许多更大的推理LLM。 最后，我们还表明，由于通过训练学习到的改进的总结条件生成能力，使用RC训练的模型可以更有效地利用现有的脚手架，从而进一步扩展测试时的性能。

TLDR: The paper introduces Reasoning Cache (RC), an iterative decoding algorithm for LLMs that enables continual improvement over long reasoning horizons by exploiting the asymmetry between generation and summarization, leading to improved performance on complex tasks.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了一种名为推理缓存（RC）的LLM迭代解码算法，它通过利用生成和总结之间的不对称性，在长时间的推理范围内实现持续改进，从而提高复杂任务的性能。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Ian Wu, Yuxiao Qu, Amrith Setlur, Aviral Kumar

TRE: Encouraging Exploration in the Trust Region

Abstract (English): Entropy regularization is a standard technique in reinforcement learning (RL) to enhance exploration, yet it yields negligible effects or even degrades performance in Large Language Models (LLMs). We attribute this failure to the cumulative tail risk inherent to LLMs with massive vocabularies and long generation horizons. In such environments, standard global entropy maximization indiscriminately dilutes probability mass into the vast tail of invalid tokens rather than focusing on plausible candidates, thereby disrupting coherent reasoning. To address this, we propose Trust Region Entropy (TRE), a method that encourages exploration strictly within the model's trust region. Extensive experiments across mathematical reasoning (MATH), combinatorial search (Countdown), and preference alignment (HH) tasks demonstrate that TRE consistently outperforms vanilla PPO, standard entropy regularization, and other exploration baselines. Our code is available at https://github.com/WhyChaos/TRE-Encouraging-Exploration-in-the-Trust-Region.

摘要（中文）: 熵正则化是强化学习（RL）中用于增强探索的标准技术，然而它在大语言模型（LLM）中几乎没有效果，甚至会降低性能。我们将此失败归因于LLM具有的大量词汇和长生成视野所固有的累积尾部风险。在这种环境中，标准全局熵最大化不加区分地将概率质量稀释到大量无效token的尾部，而不是集中在合理的候选token上，从而扰乱了连贯的推理。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了信任域熵（TRE），这是一种鼓励在模型信任域内严格进行探索的方法。在数学推理（MATH）、组合搜索（Countdown）和偏好对齐（HH）任务中进行的大量实验表明，TRE始终优于原始PPO、标准熵正则化和其他探索基线。我们的代码可在https://github.com/WhyChaos/TRE-Encouraging-Exploration-in-the-Trust-Region获取。

TLDR: The paper introduces Trust Region Entropy (TRE), a novel entropy regularization method for reinforcement learning with LLMs that encourages exploration within the model's trust region, addressing the issue of standard entropy regularization diluting probability mass into invalid tokens. TRE outperforms existing methods across mathematical reasoning, combinatorial search, and preference alignment tasks.

简要说明: 本文介绍了一种名为信任区域熵（TRE）的新型熵正则化方法，用于大型语言模型（LLM）的强化学习，该方法鼓励在模型的信任区域内进行探索，解决了标准熵正则化将概率质量稀释到无效 token 中的问题。TRE 在数学推理、组合搜索和偏好对齐等任务中优于现有方法。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Chao Huang, Yujing Lu, Quangang Li, Shenghe Wang, Yan Wang, Yueyang Zhang, Long Xia, Jiashu Zhao, Zhiyuan Sun, Daiting Shi, Tingwen Liu

Can Large Language Models Generalize Procedures Across Representations?

Abstract (English): Large language models (LLMs) are trained and tested extensively on symbolic representations such as code and graphs, yet real-world user tasks are often specified in natural language. To what extent can LLMs generalize across these representations? Here, we approach this question by studying isomorphic tasks involving procedures represented in code, graphs, and natural language (e.g., scheduling steps in planning). We find that training LLMs with popular post-training methods on graphs or code data alone does not reliably generalize to corresponding natural language tasks, while training solely on natural language can lead to inefficient performance gains. To address this gap, we propose a two-stage data curriculum that first trains on symbolic, then natural language data. The curriculum substantially improves model performance across model families and tasks. Remarkably, a 1.5B Qwen model trained by our method can closely match zero-shot GPT-4o in naturalistic planning. Finally, our analysis suggests that successful cross-representation generalization can be interpreted as a form of generative analogy, which our curriculum effectively encourages.

摘要（中文）: 大型语言模型(LLM)在代码和图等符号表示上进行了广泛的训练和测试，然而现实世界中的用户任务通常以自然语言指定。LLM在这些表示形式之间的泛化能力如何？为回答这个问题，我们研究了涉及以代码、图和自然语言表示的同构任务(例如，规划中的调度步骤)。我们发现，仅使用流行的后训练方法在图或代码数据上训练LLM，并不能可靠地泛化到相应的自然语言任务，而仅在自然语言上训练可能会导致效率低下的性能提升。为了弥补这一差距，我们提出了一种分阶段的数据课程，首先在符号数据上训练，然后在自然语言数据上训练。该课程显著提高了模型在不同模型家族和任务中的性能。值得注意的是，一个用我们方法训练的15亿参数的Qwen模型可以在自然主义的规划中与零样本GPT-4o相媲美。最后，我们的分析表明，成功的跨表示泛化可以被解释为一种生成类比，而我们的课程有效地鼓励了这种类比。

TLDR: The paper investigates LLMs' ability to generalize procedural knowledge across different representations (code, graphs, natural language), finds limitations in existing training methods, and proposes a curriculum learning approach to improve cross-representation generalization, achieving results comparable to GPT-4o in planning tasks.

简要说明: 该论文研究了大型语言模型在不同表示形式（代码、图、自然语言）之间泛化程序知识的能力，发现了现有训练方法的局限性，并提出了一种课程学习方法来提高跨表示泛化能力，从而在规划任务中实现了与 GPT-4o 相当的结果。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Fangru Lin, Valentin Hofmann, Xingchen Wan, Weixing Wang, Zifeng Ding, Anthony G. Cohn, Janet B. Pierrehumbert

A Minimal Task Reveals Emergent Path Integration and Object-Location Binding in a Predictive Sequence Model

Abstract (English): Adaptive cognition requires structured internal models representing objects and their relations. Predictive neural networks are often proposed to form such "world models", yet their underlying mechanisms remain unclear. One hypothesis is that action-conditioned sequential prediction suffices for learning such world models. In this work, we investigate this possibility in a minimal in-silico setting. Sequentially sampling tokens from 2D continuous token scenes, a recurrent neural network is trained to predict the upcoming token from current input and a saccade-like displacement. On novel scenes, prediction accuracy improves across the sequence, indicating in-context learning. Decoding analyses reveal path integration and dynamic binding of token identity to position. Interventional analyses show that new bindings can be learned late in sequence and that out-of-distribution bindings can be learned. Together, these results demonstrate how structured representations that rely on flexible binding emerge to support prediction, offering a mechanistic account of sequential world modeling relevant to cognitive science.

摘要（中文）: 自适应认知需要结构化的内部模型来表示物体及其关系。预测性神经网络经常被提出用于形成这种“世界模型”，但其潜在机制仍不清楚。一个假说是，基于动作调节的序列预测足以学习这种世界模型。 在这项工作中，我们研究了一个最小化的硅上环境中这种可能性。 通过从二维连续令牌场景中顺序采样令牌，一个循环神经网络被训练来根据当前输入和一个类似扫视的位移来预测即将到来的令牌。 在新的场景中，预测准确率在整个序列中得到提高，表明了语境学习。 解码分析揭示了路径积分和令牌身份与位置的动态绑定。 干预分析表明，新的绑定可以在序列后期学习，并且可以学习超出分布范围的绑定。 总之，这些结果表明，依赖于灵活绑定的结构化表示是如何涌现出来以支持预测的，为认知科学相关的序列世界建模提供了一种机制解释。

TLDR: The paper demonstrates that a recurrent neural network trained with sequential prediction in a minimal 2D environment can learn path integration and dynamic object-location binding, suggesting a mechanistic explanation for how world models emerge in neural networks.

简要说明: 该论文证明，在一个极简二维环境中接受序列预测训练的循环神经网络可以学习路径积分和动态物体-位置绑定，从而为神经网络中世界模型的涌现提供了一种机制解释。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Linda Ariel Ventura, Victoria Bosch, Tim C Kietzmann, Sushrut Thorat

Accurate Failure Prediction in Agents Does Not Imply Effective Failure Prevention

Abstract (English): Proactive interventions by LLM critic models are often assumed to improve reliability, yet their effects at deployment time are poorly understood. We show that a binary LLM critic with strong offline accuracy (AUROC 0.94) can nevertheless cause severe performance degradation, inducing a 26 percentage point (pp) collapse on one model while affecting another by near zero pp. This variability demonstrates that LLM critic accuracy alone is insufficient to determine whether intervention is safe. We identify a disruption-recovery tradeoff: interventions may recover failing trajectories but also disrupt trajectories that would have succeeded. Based on this insight, we propose a pre-deployment test that uses a small pilot of 50 tasks to estimate whether intervention is likely to help or harm, without requiring full deployment. Across benchmarks, the test correctly anticipates outcomes: intervention degrades performance on high-success tasks (0 to -26 pp), while yielding a modest improvement on the high-failure ALFWorld benchmark (+2.8 pp, p=0.014). The primary value of our framework is therefore identifying when not to intervene, preventing severe regressions before deployment.

摘要（中文）: 大型语言模型评论家模型的主动干预通常被认为可以提高可靠性，然而其在部署时的实际效果却鲜为人知。我们发现，一个具有强大离线准确率（AUROC 0.94）的二元大型语言模型评论家，仍然可能导致严重的性能下降，使一个模型的性能崩溃26个百分点(pp)，而对另一个模型的影响接近于零个百分点(pp)。这种差异性表明，仅凭大型语言模型评论家的准确性不足以判断干预是否安全。 我们识别出一种中断-恢复的权衡：干预可能恢复失败的轨迹，但也可能扰乱原本会成功的轨迹。基于这一洞察，我们提出了一种部署前测试，该测试使用一个由50个任务组成的小型试点来估计干预是否可能有所帮助或造成损害，而无需完全部署。在多个基准测试中，该测试能正确预测结果：干预降低了高成功率任务的性能（0到-26 pp），同时在高失败率的ALFWorld基准测试中产生 modest的改进（+2.8 pp，p=0.014）。因此，我们框架的主要价值在于识别何时不应该进行干预，从而在部署前防止严重的退化。

TLDR: This paper investigates the effectiveness of LLM critics for agent failure prediction and intervention, finding that accurate failure prediction doesn't guarantee effective failure prevention, and proposes a pre-deployment test to anticipate the impact of interventions.

简要说明: 本文研究了LLM评论员在代理失败预测和干预中的有效性，发现准确的失败预测并不能保证有效的失败预防，并提出了一种预部署测试来预测干预的影响。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Rakshith Vasudev, Melisa Russak, Dan Bikel, Waseem Alshikh

From Scalar Rewards to Potential Trends: Shaping Potential Landscapes for Model-Based Reinforcement Learning

Abstract (English): Model-based reinforcement learning (MBRL) achieves high sample efficiency by simulating future trajectories with learned dynamics and reward models. However, its effectiveness is severely compromised in sparse reward settings. The core limitation lies in the standard paradigm of regressing ground-truth scalar rewards: in sparse environments, this yields a flat, gradient-free landscape that fails to provide directional guidance for planning. To address this challenge, we propose Shaping Landscapes with Optimistic Potential Estimates (SLOPE), a novel framework that shifts reward modeling from predicting scalars to constructing informative potential landscapes. SLOPE employs optimistic distributional regression to estimate high-confidence upper bounds, which amplifies rare success signals and ensures sufficient exploration gradients. Evaluations on 30+ tasks across 5 benchmarks demonstrate that SLOPE consistently outperforms leading baselines in fully sparse, semi-sparse, and dense rewards.

摘要（中文）: 基于模型的强化学习（MBRL）通过模拟具有学习到的动力学和奖励模型的未来轨迹，实现了较高的样本效率。然而，其有效性在稀疏奖励场景中会受到严重影响。 核心局限性在于回归真实标量奖励的标准范式：在稀疏环境中，这会产生一个平坦、无梯度的地形，无法为规划提供方向性指导。 为了应对这一挑战，我们提出了一种名为“使用乐观潜力估计塑造地形（SLOPE）”的新框架，该框架将奖励建模从预测标量转变为构建信息丰富的潜力地形。 SLOPE采用乐观的分布回归来估计高置信度上限，从而放大罕见的成功信号并确保充分的探索梯度。 在跨5个基准的30多个任务上的评估表明，SLOPE在完全稀疏、半稀疏和密集奖励中始终优于领先的基线。

TLDR: This paper introduces SLOPE, a model-based reinforcement learning framework that learns potential landscapes instead of scalar rewards using optimistic distributional regression, improving performance in sparse reward environments.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了SLOPE，一个基于模型的强化学习框架，使用乐观分布回归学习潜在地形而不是标量奖励，从而提高了在稀疏奖励环境中的性能。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (9/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Yao-Hui Li, Zeyu Wang, Xin Li, Wei Pang, Yingfang Yuan, Zhengkun Chen, Boya Zhang, Riashat Islam, Alex Lamb, Yonggang Zhang

TMS: Trajectory-Mixed Supervision for Reward-Free, On-Policy SFT

Abstract (English): Reinforcement Learning (RL) and Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) are the two dominant paradigms for enhancing Large Language Model (LLM) performance on downstream tasks. While RL generally preserves broader model capabilities (retention) better than SFT, it comes with significant costs: complex reward engineering, instability, and expensive on-policy sampling. In contrast, SFT is efficient but brittle, often suffering from catastrophic forgetting due to $\textbf{Supervision Mismatch}$: the divergence between the model's evolving policy and static training labels. We address this trade-off with $\textbf{Trajectory-Mixed Supervision (TMS)}$, a reward-free framework that approximates the on-policy benefits of RL by creating a dynamic curriculum from the model's own historical checkpoints. TMS minimizes $\textit{Policy-Label Divergence (PLD)}$, preventing the mode collapse that drives forgetting in standard SFT. Experiments across reasoning (MATH, GSM8K) and instruction-following benchmarks demonstrate that TMS effectively shifts the accuracy--retention Pareto frontier. While RL remains the gold standard for retention, TMS significantly outperforms standard and iterative SFT, bridging the gap to RL without requiring reward models or verifiers. Mechanistic analysis confirms that PLD drift accurately predicts forgetting and that TMS successfully mitigates this drift.

摘要（中文）: 强化学习（RL）和监督式微调（SFT）是增强大型语言模型（LLM）在下游任务性能的两种主要范式。 虽然强化学习通常比监督式微调更好地保留更广泛的模型能力（保留），但它也带来了巨大的成本：复杂的奖励工程、不稳定性和昂贵的在线策略采样。 相比之下，监督式微调是高效的，但也很脆弱，常常由于$\textbf{监督不匹配}$而导致灾难性遗忘：即模型不断演变的策略与静态训练标签之间的差异。 我们通过$\textbf{轨迹混合监督（TMS）}$来解决这种权衡，这是一个无需奖励的框架，它通过从模型自身的历史检查点创建动态课程来近似强化学习的在线策略优势。 轨迹混合监督通过最小化$\textit{策略-标签差异（PLD）}$来防止模式崩溃，而模式崩溃是标准监督式微调中遗忘的驱动因素。在推理（MATH, GSM8K）和指令遵循基准测试上的实验表明，轨迹混合监督有效地改变了准确性-保留帕累托前沿。 虽然强化学习仍然是保留的金标准，但轨迹混合监督显著优于标准和迭代监督式微调，无需奖励模型或验证器即可弥合与强化学习之间的差距。 机制分析证实，策略-标签差异漂移能准确预测遗忘，并且轨迹混合监督成功地缓解了这种漂移。

TLDR: The paper introduces Trajectory-Mixed Supervision (TMS), a reward-free SFT method that addresses the trade-off between accuracy and retention in LLMs by creating a dynamic curriculum from the model's own historical checkpoints, mitigating policy-label divergence and improving retention compared to standard SFT.

简要说明: 该论文提出了一种名为轨迹混合监督 (TMS) 的无奖励 SFT 方法，该方法通过从模型自身的历史检查点创建动态的课程，解决了 LLM 中准确性和保留率之间的权衡。TMS 缓解了策略标签差异，与标准 SFT 相比，提高了保留率。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Rana Muhammad Shahroz Khan, Zijie Liu, Zhen Tan, Charles Fleming, Tianlong Chen

LatentMem: Customizing Latent Memory for Multi-Agent Systems

Abstract (English): Large language model (LLM)-powered multi-agent systems (MAS) demonstrate remarkable collective intelligence, wherein multi-agent memory serves as a pivotal mechanism for continual adaptation. However, existing multi-agent memory designs remain constrained by two fundamental bottlenecks: (i) memory homogenization arising from the absence of role-aware customization, and (ii) information overload induced by excessively fine-grained memory entries. To address these limitations, we propose LatentMem, a learnable multi-agent memory framework designed to customize agent-specific memories in a token-efficient manner. Specifically, LatentMem comprises an experience bank that stores raw interaction trajectories in a lightweight form, and a memory composer that synthesizes compact latent memories conditioned on retrieved experience and agent-specific contexts. Further, we introduce Latent Memory Policy Optimization (LMPO), which propagates task-level optimization signals through latent memories to the composer, encouraging it to produce compact and high-utility representations. Extensive experiments across diverse benchmarks and mainstream MAS frameworks show that LatentMem achieves a performance gain of up to $19.36$% over vanilla settings and consistently outperforms existing memory architectures, without requiring any modifications to the underlying frameworks.

摘要（中文）: 由大型语言模型驱动的多智能体系统（MAS）展现出卓越的集体智能，其中多智能体记忆是持续适应的关键机制。然而，现有的多智能体记忆设计仍然受到两个基本瓶颈的限制：（i）因缺乏角色感知定制而产生的记忆同质化，以及（ii）由过于细粒度的记忆条目引起的信息过载。为了解决这些问题，我们提出了LatentMem，一种可学习的多智能体记忆框架，旨在以高效的标记方式定制特定于智能体的记忆。具体而言，LatentMem包含一个以轻量级形式存储原始交互轨迹的经验库，以及一个根据检索到的经验和特定于智能体的上下文合成紧凑的潜在记忆的记忆合成器。此外，我们引入了潜在记忆策略优化（LMPO），它通过潜在记忆将任务级优化信号传播到合成器，从而鼓励其产生紧凑且高实用性的表示。在各种基准测试和主流MAS框架上的大量实验表明，LatentMem的性能比原始设置提高了高达19.36%，并且始终优于现有的记忆架构，而无需对底层框架进行任何修改。

TLDR: The paper introduces LatentMem, a framework that addresses memory homogenization and information overload in LLM-powered multi-agent systems by customizing agent-specific latent memories and optimizing memory composition, achieving performance gains over existing methods.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了LatentMem，一个通过定制agent特定的潜在记忆并优化记忆组成来解决LLM驱动的多智能体系统中记忆同质化和信息过载问题的框架，相比现有方法取得了性能提升。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Muxin Fu, Guibin Zhang, Xiangyuan Xue, Yafu Li, Zefeng He, Siyuan Huang, Xiaoye Qu, Yu Cheng, Yang Yang

From Task Solving to Robust Real-World Adaptation in LLM Agents

Abstract (English): Large language models are increasingly deployed as specialized agents that plan, call tools, and take actions over extended horizons. Yet many existing evaluations assume a "clean interface" where dynamics are specified and stable, tools and sensors are reliable, and success is captured by a single explicit objective-often overestimating real-world readiness. In practice, agents face underspecified rules, unreliable signals, shifting environments, and implicit, multi-stakeholder goals. The challenge is therefore not just solving tasks, but adapting while solving: deciding what to trust, what is wanted, when to verify, and when to fall back or escalate. We stress-test deployment-relevant robustness under four operational circumstances: partial observability, dynamic environments, noisy signals, and dynamic agent state. We benchmark agentic LLMs in a grid-based game with a simple goal but long-horizon execution. Episodes violate clean-interface assumptions yet remain solvable, forcing agents to infer rules, pay for information, adapt to environmental and internal shifts, and act cautiously under noise. Across five state-of-the-art LLM agents, we find large gaps between nominal task-solving and deployment-like robustness. Performance generally degrades as grid size and horizon increase, but rankings are unstable: weaker models can beat stronger ones when strategy matches the uncertainty regime. Despite no explicit instruction, agents trade off completion, efficiency, and penalty avoidance, suggesting partial objective inference. Ablations and feature analyses reveal model-specific sensitivities and failure drivers, motivating work on verification, safe action selection, and objective inference under partial observability, noise, and non-stationarity.

摘要（中文）: 大型语言模型正日益被部署为专业化代理，用于规划、调用工具并在较长的时间范围内执行行动。然而，许多现有的评估假设存在一个“干净的界面”，其中动力学是被明确指定且稳定的，工具和传感器是可靠的，并且成功是通过一个单一的明确目标来捕捉的——这往往高估了在现实世界中的准备程度。在实践中，代理面临着未明确的规则、不可靠的信号、不断变化的环境以及隐式的、多方利益相关者的目标。因此，挑战不仅仅是解决任务，而是在解决任务的同时进行适应：决定信任什么、需要什么、何时验证以及何时回退或升级。我们着重于在四种可操作的情况下对部署相关的鲁棒性进行压力测试：部分可观测性、动态环境、噪声信号和动态代理状态。我们在一个基于网格的游戏中对代理型 LLM 进行基准测试，该游戏具有简单的目标但执行时间长。剧集违反了干净界面的假设，但仍然是可解决的，迫使代理推断规则、付费获取信息、适应环境和内部变化，并在噪声下谨慎行事。在五个最先进的 LLM 代理中，我们发现名义上的任务解决能力和类部署鲁棒性之间存在很大差距。 性能通常随着网格大小和时间范围的增加而降低，但排名不稳定：当策略与不确定性状态相匹配时，较弱的模型可以击败较强的模型。尽管没有明确的指令，代理会在完成、效率和避免处罚之间进行权衡，这表明存在部分目标推断。消融研究和特征分析揭示了特定于模型的敏感性和失败驱动因素，从而推动了在部分可观测性、噪声和非平稳性下进行验证、安全行动选择以及目标推断方面的工作。

TLDR: This paper evaluates the robustness of LLM agents in a simulated grid-world environment by introducing challenges like partial observability, dynamic environments, and noisy signals, revealing a significant performance gap between nominal task-solving and real-world deployment robustness.

简要说明: 本文通过在模拟网格世界环境中引入部分可观测性、动态环境和噪声信号等挑战，评估了LLM智能体在现实世界部署中的鲁棒性，揭示了名义任务解决能力与现实世界部署鲁棒性之间的巨大性能差距。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Pouya Pezeshkpour, Estevam Hruschka

Maximum Likelihood Reinforcement Learning

Abstract (English): Reinforcement learning is the method of choice to train models in sampling-based setups with binary outcome feedback, such as navigation, code generation, and mathematical problem solving. In such settings, models implicitly induce a likelihood over correct rollouts. However, we observe that reinforcement learning does not maximize this likelihood, and instead optimizes only a lower-order approximation. Inspired by this observation, we introduce Maximum Likelihood Reinforcement Learning (MaxRL), a sampling-based framework to approximate maximum likelihood using reinforcement learning techniques. MaxRL addresses the challenges of non-differentiable sampling by defining a compute-indexed family of sample-based objectives that interpolate between standard reinforcement learning and exact maximum likelihood as additional sampling compute is allocated. The resulting objectives admit a simple, unbiased policy-gradient estimator and converge to maximum likelihood optimization in the infinite-compute limit. Empirically, we show that MaxRL Pareto-dominates existing methods in all models and tasks we tested, achieving up to 20x test-time scaling efficiency gains compared to its GRPO-trained counterpart. We also observe MaxRL to scale better with additional data and compute. Our results suggest MaxRL is a promising framework for scaling RL training in correctness based settings.

摘要（中文）: 强化学习是在基于采样的场景中训练具有二元结果反馈的模型（例如导航、代码生成和数学问题求解）的首选方法。在这些场景中，模型隐式地诱导出正确轨迹的似然。然而，我们观察到强化学习并没有最大化这个似然，而是仅优化了一个低阶近似。受此启发，我们引入了最大似然强化学习 (MaxRL)，这是一个基于采样的框架，利用强化学习技术来近似最大似然。MaxRL通过定义一个计算索引的基于采样的目标函数族来解决不可微分采样带来的挑战，该目标函数族在分配额外采样计算资源时，会在标准强化学习和精确最大似然之间进行插值。由此产生的目标函数允许一个简单、无偏的策略梯度估计器，并在无限计算的极限下收敛于最大似然优化。 经验上，我们表明，MaxRL在所有我们测试的模型和任务中都 Pareto 优于现有方法，与 GRPO 训练的对应方法相比，实现了高达 20 倍的测试时扩展效率提升。我们还观察到 MaxRL 在使用额外的数据和计算时表现出更好的扩展性。我们的结果表明，MaxRL 是在基于正确性的场景中扩展强化学习训练的一种有前景的框架。

TLDR: The paper introduces Maximum Likelihood Reinforcement Learning (MaxRL), a novel framework that approximates maximum likelihood using RL techniques in settings with binary outcome feedback, and shows it outperforms existing methods with significant efficiency gains.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了最大似然强化学习（MaxRL），这是一种新的框架，它在具有二元结果反馈的设置中使用强化学习技术来近似最大似然，并表明它优于现有方法，并具有显着的效率提升。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Fahim Tajwar, Guanning Zeng, Yueer Zhou, Yuda Song, Daman Arora, Yiding Jiang, Jeff Schneider, Ruslan Salakhutdinov, Haiwen Feng, Andrea Zanette

When Should Agents Coordinate in Differentiable Sequential Decision Problems?

Abstract (English): Multi-robot teams must coordinate to operate effectively. When a team operates in an uncoordinated manner, and agents choose actions that are only individually optimal, the team's outcome can suffer. However, in many domains, coordination requires costly communication. We explore the value of coordination in a broad class of differentiable motion-planning problems. In particular, we model coordinated behavior as a spectrum: at one extreme, agents jointly optimize a common team objective, and at the other, agents make unilaterally optimal decisions given their individual decision variables, i.e., they operate at Nash equilibria. We then demonstrate that reasoning about coordination in differentiable motion-planning problems reduces to reasoning about the second-order properties of agents' objectives, and we provide algorithms that use this second-order reasoning to determine at which times a team of agents should coordinate.

摘要（中文）: 多机器人团队必须进行协调才能有效地运作。当团队以不协调的方式运作，且智能体选择仅对自身最优的行动时，团队的整体结果可能会受到影响。然而，在许多领域，协调需要代价高昂的通信。我们探索了协调在一类广泛的可微运动规划问题中的价值。特别地，我们将协调行为建模为一个光谱：在一个极端，智能体共同优化一个共同的团队目标；而在另一个极端，智能体根据其个体的决策变量做出单方面最优的决策，即，它们在纳什均衡状态下运作。然后我们证明，在可微运动规划问题中对协调进行推理，可以简化为对智能体目标函数的二阶性质进行推理，并且我们提供了使用这种二阶推理来确定智能体团队应该在何时进行协调的算法。

TLDR: The paper investigates when multi-robot teams should coordinate in differentiable motion-planning problems, proposing algorithms based on second-order properties of agent objectives to determine optimal coordination times.

简要说明: 该论文研究了在可微运动规划问题中，多机器人团队何时应该进行协调，并提出了基于智能体目标函数的二阶属性的算法来确定最佳协调时间。

Relevance: (7/10)
Novelty: (7/10)

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Caleb Probine, Su Ann Low, David Fridovich-Keil, Ufuk Topcu

Variance-Reduced Model Predictive Path Integral via Quadratic Model Approximation

Abstract (English): Sampling-based controllers, such as Model Predictive Path Integral (MPPI) methods, offer substantial flexibility but often suffer from high variance and low sample efficiency. To address these challenges, we introduce a hybrid variance-reduced MPPI framework that integrates a prior model into the sampling process. Our key insight is to decompose the objective function into a known approximate model and a residual term. Since the residual captures only the discrepancy between the model and the objective, it typically exhibits a smaller magnitude and lower variance than the original objective. Although this principle applies to general modeling choices, we demonstrate that adopting a quadratic approximation enables the derivation of a closed-form, model-guided prior that effectively concentrates samples in informative regions. Crucially, the framework is agnostic to the source of geometric information, allowing the quadratic model to be constructed from exact derivatives, structural approximations (e.g., Gauss- or Quasi-Newton), or gradient-free randomized smoothing. We validate the approach on standard optimization benchmarks, a nonlinear, underactuated cart-pole control task, and a contact-rich manipulation problem with non-smooth dynamics. Across these domains, we achieve faster convergence and superior performance in low-sample regimes compared to standard MPPI. These results suggest that the method can make sample-based control strategies more practical in scenarios where obtaining samples is expensive or limited.

摘要（中文）: 基于采样的控制器，例如模型预测路径积分（MPPI）方法，提供了很大的灵活性，但通常存在高方差和低采样效率的问题。为了解决这些挑战，我们引入了一种混合方差缩减的MPPI框架，该框架将先验模型集成到采样过程中。我们的关键见解是将目标函数分解为一个已知的近似模型和一个残差项。由于残差仅捕获模型与目标之间的差异，因此它通常比原始目标函数表现出更小的量级和更低的方差。虽然此原理适用于一般的建模选择，但我们证明采用二次近似能够推导出闭合形式的模型引导先验，有效地将样本集中在信息丰富的区域。至关重要的是，该框架与几何信息的来源无关，允许从精确导数、结构近似（例如，高斯或拟牛顿）或无梯度随机平滑构造二次模型。我们在标准优化基准测试、非线性欠驱动倒立摆控制任务以及具有非光滑动力学的接触丰富的操纵问题上验证了该方法。在这些领域中，与标准MPPI相比，我们在低样本机制下实现了更快的收敛速度和卓越的性能。这些结果表明，该方法可以使基于采样的控制策略在获取样本成本高昂或受限的情况下更具实用性。

TLDR: This paper introduces a variance-reduced Model Predictive Path Integral (MPPI) method that integrates a prior model, particularly a quadratic approximation, to improve sample efficiency in control tasks, demonstrating superior performance over standard MPPI in various scenarios.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了一种方差缩减的模型预测路径积分(MPPI)方法，该方法集成了一个先验模型，特别是二次近似，以提高控制任务中的样本效率，并在各种情况下表现出优于标准MPPI的性能。

Relevance: (7/10)
Novelty: (8/10)

Authors: Fabian Schramm, Franki Nguimatsia Tiofack, Nicolas Perrin-Gilbert, Marc Toussaint, Justin Carpentier

Self-supervised Physics-Informed Manipulation of Deformable Linear Objects with Non-negligible Dynamics

Abstract (English): We address dynamic manipulation of deformable linear objects by presenting SPiD, a physics-informed self-supervised learning framework that couples an accurate deformable object model with an augmented self-supervised training strategy. On the modeling side, we extend a mass-spring model to more accurately capture object dynamics while remaining lightweight enough for high-throughput rollouts during self-supervised learning. On the learning side, we train a neural controller using a task-oriented cost, enabling end-to-end optimization through interaction with the differentiable object model. In addition, we propose a self-supervised DAgger variant that detects distribution shift during deployment and performs offline self-correction to further enhance robustness without expert supervision. We evaluate our method primarily on the rope stabilization task, where a robot must bring a swinging rope to rest as quickly and smoothly as possible. Extensive experiments in both simulation and the real world demonstrate that the proposed controller achieves fast and smooth rope stabilization, generalizing across unseen initial states, rope lengths, masses, non-uniform mass distributions, and external disturbances. Additionally, we develop an affordable markerless rope perception method and demonstrate that our controller maintains performance with noisy and low-frequency state updates. Furthermore, we demonstrate the generality of the framework by extending it to the rope trajectory tracking task. Overall, SPiD offers a data-efficient, robust, and physically grounded framework for dynamic manipulation of deformable linear objects, featuring strong sim-to-real generalization.

摘要（中文）: 我们提出了 SPiD，一个基于物理信息的自监督学习框架，用于可变形线性对象的动态操作，该框架将精确的可变形对象模型与增强的自监督训练策略相结合。在建模方面，我们扩展了一个质块-弹簧模型，以更准确地捕捉对象动力学，同时保持足够轻量，以便在自监督学习期间进行高吞吐量展开。在学习方面，我们使用面向任务的代价函数训练神经控制器，从而能够通过与可微分对象模型的交互进行端到端优化。此外，我们提出了一种自监督 DAgger 变体，能够检测部署期间的分布偏移，并执行离线自校正，以进一步增强鲁棒性，而无需专家监督。我们主要在绳索稳定任务上评估我们的方法，在该任务中，机器人必须尽可能快速平稳地使摆动的绳索静止。在仿真和真实世界的广泛实验表明，所提出的控制器实现了快速平稳的绳索稳定，并能泛化到未见的初始状态、绳索长度、质量、非均匀质量分布和外部扰动。此外，我们开发了一种经济实惠的无标记绳索感知方法，并证明我们的控制器在存在噪声和低频率状态更新的情况下仍能保持性能。更进一步，我们通过将其扩展到绳索轨迹跟踪任务，证明了该框架的通用性。总而言之，SPiD 为可变形线性对象的动态操作提供了一个数据高效、鲁棒且基于物理的框架，具有强大的从模拟到真实世界的泛化能力。

TLDR: The paper presents SPiD, a physics-informed self-supervised learning framework for dynamic manipulation of deformable linear objects, focusing on rope stabilization and trajectory tracking with strong sim-to-real generalization.

简要说明: 该论文提出了SPiD，一个基于物理信息的自监督学习框架，用于动态操作可变形线性对象，侧重于绳索稳定和轨迹跟踪，具有很强的从仿真到真实的泛化能力。

Relevance: (7/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)
Overall: (7/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Youyuan Long, Gokhan Solak, Sara Zeynalpour, Heng Zhang, Arash Ajoudani

HetroD: A High-Fidelity Drone Dataset and Benchmark for Autonomous Driving in Heterogeneous Traffic

Abstract (English): We present HetroD, a dataset and benchmark for developing autonomous driving systems in heterogeneous environments. HetroD targets the critical challenge of navi- gating real-world heterogeneous traffic dominated by vulner- able road users (VRUs), including pedestrians, cyclists, and motorcyclists that interact with vehicles. These mixed agent types exhibit complex behaviors such as hook turns, lane splitting, and informal right-of-way negotiation. Such behaviors pose significant challenges for autonomous vehicles but remain underrepresented in existing datasets focused on structured, lane-disciplined traffic. To bridge the gap, we collect a large- scale drone-based dataset to provide a holistic observation of traffic scenes with centimeter-accurate annotations, HD maps, and traffic signal states. We further develop a modular toolkit for extracting per-agent scenarios to support downstream task development. In total, the dataset comprises over 65.4k high- fidelity agent trajectories, 70% of which are from VRUs. HetroD supports modeling of VRU behaviors in dense, het- erogeneous traffic and provides standardized benchmarks for forecasting, planning, and simulation tasks. Evaluation results reveal that state-of-the-art prediction and planning models struggle with the challenges presented by our dataset: they fail to predict lateral VRU movements, cannot handle unstructured maneuvers, and exhibit limited performance in dense and multi-agent scenarios, highlighting the need for more robust approaches to heterogeneous traffic. See our project page for more examples: https://hetroddata.github.io/HetroD/

摘要（中文）: 我们提出了 HetroD，一个用于开发异构环境中自动驾驶系统的数据集和基准。 HetroD 旨在应对在真实世界中，以易受伤害道路使用者 (VRU) 为主的异构交通环境中的导航这一关键挑战，这些 VRU 包括行人、自行车骑行者和摩托车骑行者，他们与车辆进行交互。这些混合的智能体类型表现出复杂的行为，如钩型转弯、车道分割和非正式的路权协商。这些行为对自动驾驶车辆提出了重大挑战，但在现有专注于结构化、遵守车道规则的交通的数据集中，这些行为仍然代表性不足。为了弥补这一差距，我们收集了一个大规模的基于无人机的数据集，以提供对交通场景的全面观察，并提供厘米级精度的标注、高清地图和交通信号状态。我们进一步开发了一个模块化工具包，用于提取每个智能体的场景，以支持下游任务的开发。总计，该数据集包含超过 65.4k 条高保真智能体轨迹，其中 70% 来自 VRU。 HetroD 支持对密集异构交通中的 VRU 行为进行建模，并为预测、规划和仿真任务提供标准化基准。评估结果表明，最先进的预测和规划模型难以应对我们数据集提出的挑战：它们无法预测 VRU 的横向运动，无法处理非结构化机动，并且在密集和多智能体场景中表现出有限的性能，这突出了对更强大的异构交通处理方法的需求。更多示例请参见我们的项目页面：https://hetroddata.github.io/HetroD/

TLDR: HetroD is a new drone-based dataset and benchmark designed for autonomous driving in heterogeneous traffic, focusing on the challenges posed by vulnerable road users and their complex interactions, offering a valuable resource for forecasting, planning, and simulation research.

简要说明: HetroD是一个新的基于无人机的异构交通数据集和基准，专注于弱势道路使用者及其复杂交互带来的挑战，为预测、规划和仿真研究提供了宝贵的资源。

Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
